OpenAI Debug

Status: Stub preview (no live response)   Endpoint: stub   Model: gpt-4.1

Prompt sent to OpenAI
You are “Dan – Workday AI Research Agent.” 
Produce a comprehensive JSON object that matches the schema I will give you exactly.

DATE CONTEXT (ET)
- TODAY_ET = 2026-04-29
- LAST_24H_WINDOW_ET = 2026-04-28T08:06 → 2026-04-29T08:06 inclusive

RESEARCH SCOPE
Focus on Workday and its ecosystem — Workday HCM, Workday Extend, Workday Skills Cloud, Workday AI Marketplace, and all agentic or autonomous AI capabilities in SaaS solutions. 
Include:
- Workday product developments, patents, partnerships, and leadership commentary.
- Competitive analysis (SAP, Oracle, ServiceNow, UKG, ADP, Dayforce, etc.).
- GSI & boutique consultancy perspectives (Deloitte, Accenture, EY, KPMG, PwC, IBM, Capgemini, Infosys, TCS, Wipro, Cognizant, Kainos, Invisors, TopBloc, OSV, Alight, Mercer, etc.).
- Ecosystem vendors and hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP, Salesforce).
- Analyst & media insights (Gartner, Forrester, IDC, Reuters, Bloomberg, VentureBeat, etc.).
- Emerging risks, governance, and responsible AI developments affecting SaaS/HR technology.

WRITING EXPECTATIONS
- For each cited source, provide **multi-paragraph summaries (150–250 words)** that explain:
  - What was announced, found, or claimed.
  - Why it matters strategically, technically, and competitively.
  - Implications for Workday clients and Deloitte’s Workday practice.
- Incorporate synthesis across related articles (e.g., “Across multiple GSIs…” or “Several sources indicate…”).
- Prioritize insights, nuance, and context over mere headlines.
- Every key claim must cite a valid source URL from the provided SOURCE LIST.
- If no credible items are found in the last 24h, produce an honest “no significant updates” section.

FORMATTING RULES
- Return JSON only (no Markdown or prose outside the object).
- `html_body` must be valid, minimal HTML (<h2>, <h3>, <p>, <ul>, <li>, <a href="...">).
- Section order: Highlights; Competitive Watch; Enablement; Actions for Next Week; Risks & Mitigations; All Sources.
- Keep total HTML under 35 KB.
- `plain_text_body` must be readable text with visible URLs.

Use the following vetted sources to craft the brief.

Return JSON ONLY in this exact shape (no code fences, no extra text):

{
  "type": "daily or weekly",
  "run_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "title": "Short headline",
  "priority_focus": "1–2 sentences on what matters most now",
  "highlights": [
    { "headline": "…", "why_it_matters": "…", "source_url": "https://…" }
  ],
  "competitive_watch": [
    { "competitor": "Name", "move": "…", "implication": "…" }
  ],
  "enablement": [
    { "skill": "Topic", "resource_url": "https://…", "90_day_outcome": "…" }
  ],
  "actions_next_week": ["…"],
  "risks": [
    { "risk": "…", "mitigation": "…" }
  ],
  "sources": [
    { "title": "…", "url": "https://…" }
  ],
  "html_body": "<h2>…</h2> (well-formatted HTML with <a href="https://…">links</a>; no scripts/external CSS)",
  "plain_text_body": "Text-only with visible URLs"
}

Parameters:
- Set "run_date" to TODAY_ET = 2026-04-29.
- For DAILY: set "type":"daily"; target ~1000 words; STRICTLY restrict to the last 24 hours (from 2026-04-28T08:06 to 2026-04-29T08:06); include publication dates in highlight text.
- For WEEKLY: set "type":"weekly"; target 2000–2500 words; restrict to 2026-04-22…2026-04-29; include <h3>What changed this week</h3> and publication dates in highlight text.
- Section order for html_body: Highlights; Competitive Watch; Enablement; Actions for Next Week; Risks & Mitigations; All Sources.
- Every item must include at least one absolute URL (https://…).
- Keep total HTML under ~25KB.

ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS
- For every highlight and competitive_watch item, include a 2–3 paragraph detailed summary within the "why_it_matters" text.
- Where relevant, compare perspectives across multiple sources.
- Include a clear "so what" statement for Deloitte’s Workday practice.
- Ensure all cited URLs appear in the `sources` array and inside the `html_body` link text.
- Keep factual accuracy and avoid speculation.

      "content": "Ultimately, the advantage isn’t what organizations have. It’s how everything works together. Across all three solution areas, outputs feed into Appian Composer to generate applications with enterprise-grade security, audit trails, and AI integration—bringing critical business logic under governance and making it ready for the agentic workflows enterprises are building today.\n\n## About PwC\n\nAt PwC, we help clients build trust and reinvent so they can turn complexity into competitive advantage. Across audit and assurance, tax and legal, deals and consulting, we help clients build, accelerate, and sustain momentum. Find out more at www.pwc.com.\n\n© 2026 PwC. All rights reserved. [...] “Appian Composer was built to close the gap between the complexity of what enterprises actually run and the speed at which they need to modernize,” said Scott Van Valkenburgh, Senior Vice President, Global Partners & Channels, Appian. “PwC’s doc extraction capability brings exactly the kind of structured, business-grounded input that enables Composer to do its best work.       "url": "https://kpmg.com/xx/en/our-insights/ai-and-technology/asia-pacific-ai-adoption-advantage.html",
      "title": "Asia Pacific moves from AI adoption to AI advantage",
      "content": "These developments are not surprising. Companies — and consumers — in the region have long been early adopters of new technologies, absorbing them into everyday life and business. AI is no different. The KPMG survey results illustrate that the region’s companies are, in many ways, better prepared than their peers elsewhere for a future in which AI is integral to business growth and resilience.\n\nASPAC companies are looking to spend more and planning further ahead than most. The key building blocks, in the form of governance, talent and deployment, are falling into place. Companies here will soon have the capabilities to match their ambition.\n\n## Our insights\n\n### Global AI Quarterly Pulse: Q1 2026\n\n### Agentic AI advantage: Unlocking next-level value\n\n### Global tech report 2026 [...] Agents are being deployed in technology and IT (66 percent), operations (48 percent) and marketing and sales (44 percent), but the real value lies in cross functional coordination. Almost half the respondents in ASPAC say AI agents are automating workflows that span multiple functions. More interestingly, in the next two to three years, four in ten ASPAC firms expects AI agents to take the lead on managing specific projects, and not just assist with them. This is far more than the global share of 30 percent.\n\nThese numbers demonstrate confidence among ASPAC companies about the utility and value of AI. They are now organising around these results, building the right teams for an AI-prevalent world.\n\n### Talent as a multiplier [...] But the survey results provide enough indication that this gap is closing in ASPAC. The shift towards AI agents is accelerating globally, including in ASPAC, and reflects growing confidence in what AI can do. One in three (32 percent of) ASPAC firms are already scaling agents across multiple functions, led by Korea (41 percent).",
      "score": 0.06435309,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "title": "AWS Marketplace: Agent Observability & SLO Framework on Amazon CloudWatch GenAI",
      "content": "Three retainer tiers (opt-in monthly). Essentials $8K/mo (up to 3 agents; monthly SLO review; dashboard tuning; quarterly runbook refresh; 8×5 email) for AI-native Series B–E. Standard $14K/mo (up to 10 agents; biweekly SLO review; 24×5 on-call response, not primary; monthly post-mortem co-authorship; CI/CD alert tuning) for mid-sized with production SRE. Enterprise $20K/mo (unlimited agents; 0.25 FTE dedicated Kriv SRE engineer embedded; quarterly SLO audit; regulated-industry evidence, SOC 2 CC7 + SR 11-7 + EU AI Act Article 72 + HIPAA §164.308(a)(1)(ii)(D); quarterly executive readout) for regulated, G-SIB banks, top-25 payers + pharmas. Optional Extra Agent Implementation $12K. [...] Observability components. OpenTelemetry across Bedrock Agents, Claude Agent SDK, LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI, MCP servers (W3C Trace Context). X-Ray distributed tracing + service map. CloudWatch GenAI Observability native Bedrock telemetry. Bedrock Model Invocation Logging → CloudWatch Logs + S3. Custom metrics, tool-use correctness, grounding score, hallucination rate, refusal rate, trajectory length, P50/P95/P99 per-tool-call latency, cost-per-invocation, MCP-call success rate. SLO definitions with error budget tracking. RED + USE golden signals per agent. Dashboards in CloudWatch + QuickSight. Alerts via CloudWatch Alarms → EventBridge → SNS → PagerDuty / Opsgenie / Jira / ServiceNow / Slack / Microsoft Teams. Runbooks for silent hallucination, tool-use failure cascade, grounding [...] Existing AWS Marketplace listings don't close this gap. IBM Instana + IBM Consulting Advantage bundle inside platform engagements. SoftServe bundles inside modernization SOWs. Slalom / Caylent / Mission Cloud / Rackspace sell general observability at $75K–$300K. Big-4 off-Marketplace $250K–$2M. {

  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=1330-1001178431en-3BQ73O6470CEN8BM34DAGCJ0CO",
      "title": "Sage expands AI agents across finance, HR and operations to automate workflows and help teams act faster with control – Company Announcement - FT.com",
      "content": "# Company Announcements\n\n### Sage expands AI agents across finance, HR and operations to automate workflows and help teams act faster with control\n\nATLANTA, April 28, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Sage (FTSE: SGE), the leader in accounting, financial, HR and payroll technology for small and mid-sized businesses, today announced the advancement of AI agents across finance, HR and operations at its flagship event Sage Future, embedding intelligent automation directly into core business systems. These capabilities help teams act on work directly, not just analyze it. [...] Sage is also expanding its AI platform to enable partners and developers to build new solutions that integrate directly with Sage data and customer workflows. New capabilities including AI Gateway, Agent Builder tools and a dedicated Agent Marketplace will allow partners to design and deploy specialized AI agents tailored to specific industries while meeting Sage standards for trust, security, and interoperability. This opens the next wave of partner-led innovation, enabling partners to build agents that carry their expertise directly into customer workflows. [...] Notes to editors\n\nQ: What did Sage announce in this release?  \nSage announced a new generation of AI agents designed to automate financial, workforce and operational workflows while maintaining transparency, accuracy and human oversight in business-critical systems.\n\nQ: What is the Sage Intacct Finance Intelligence Agent?  \nThe Finance Intelligence Agent is an AI capability designed to help finance teams retrieve insights, analyze financial performance, identify anomalies and progress financial tasks such as payment reminders, approvals and write-offs. It is scheduled for general availability later in 2026.",
      "score": 0.7216064,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://investor.forrester.com/news-releases/news-release-details/forrester-launches-forrester-ai-agent-microsoft-365-copilot",
      "title": "Forrester Launches Forrester AI Agent For Microsoft 365 Copilot To Deliver Research-Driven Insights Into Leaders’ Workflows | Forrester Research, Inc",
      "content": "PHOENIX & CAMBRIDGE, Mass.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Apr. 28, 2026-- Forrester (Nasdaq: FORR) today launched the Forrester AI (formerly Izola) agent for Microsoft 365 Copilot, enabling clients to securely access trusted Forrester research and guidance directly within their daily workflows at no additional cost for existing license holders. This announcement follows Forrester’s recent Microsoft Teams integration, continuing to redefine how clients engage with research and advisory firms. [...] Skip to main navigation\n\nForrester\n\nInvestor Relations > News & Events > News Release Details\n\n# News Release Details\n\n## Forrester Launches Forrester AI Agent For Microsoft 365 Copilot To Deliver Research-Driven Insights Into Leaders’ Workflows\n\nApril 28, 2026\n\nPDF Version\n\nThe agent empowers leaders with secure access to actionable research within their everyday tools [...] “We have entered a new era of work where AI is embedded into every workflow and every decision,” said Carrie Johnson, chief product officer at Forrester. “From launching Forrester AI to scaling it with AI Access to delivering it inside applications like Microsoft Teams, we have focused on bringing trusted, research-driven insights to where work happens. By launching the Forrester AI agent for Microsoft 365 Copilot, we are accelerating this strategy, embedding Forrester AI deeper into daily workflows so our clients can turn our advice and insights into faster, better decisions. With a clear strategy and more than three years of consistent execution and learning, we are continuing to innovate and expand the value we deliver to our clients.”",
      "score": 0.67192334,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202604290700BIZWIRE_USPRX____20260427_BW054567-1",
      "title": "Hightouch Raises $150 Million to Reinvent How Marketing Works Using AI – Company Announcement",
      "content": "The company has grown more than 100% in each of the past two years as enterprises adopt AI agents to automate and execute marketing workflows, signaling a broader shift in how marketing operates.\n\n“Marketing is sorely in need of reinvention,” said Kashish Gupta, co-founder and co-CEO of Hightouch. “But most AI solutions haven’t actually changed how marketing works. Instead, they generate vast amounts of mediocre content that doesn’t really get used. We built Hightouch to rethink marketing end-to-end, so AI agents can operate directly on trusted data, find opportunities 24/7, and then generate and execute high-quality campaigns across channels.” [...] Companies including Domino's, PetSmart, DraftKings, Ramp and Whoop use Hightouch to activate customer data and power personalized marketing across channels.\n\n“AI is fundamentally changing how enterprises operate, and marketing is one of the largest functions poised for transformation,” said Darren Cohen, Partner at Goldman Sachs. “Hightouch has built a platform that enables companies to deploy AI agents directly on top of their most trusted data systems. We believe that approach positions them to define the next category of marketing infrastructure.”\n\nThis financing signals the arrival of agentic marketing as a category. As enterprises move beyond experimentation, AI agents are becoming core to how marketing is planned, created, and executed. [...] Over the past 18 months, many companies experimented with AI in marketing, but results have fallen short. Unlike engineering, where AI can operate on structured code, marketing depends on brand context, proprietary data, and complex workflows, areas where most AI tools lack access or understanding.\n\nHightouch addresses this gap with an agentic marketing platform built on top of a comprehensive enterprise context layer. By combining customer data, brand context, and marketing orchestration, Hightouch enables always-on AI agents to proactively research audiences, generate on-brand creative, and execute campaigns across advertising, email, SMS, and web, all within enterprise guardrails. Customers are seeing meaningful improvements in speed, output quality, and campaign performance.",
      "score": 0.67166495,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202604290300PR_NEWS_EURO_ND__EN46081-1",
      "title": "Appian Advances AI in Process to Deliver Enterprise Outcomes at Scale – Company Announcement - FT.com",
      "content": "AI agents in Appian are smarter, safer and more effective because they have better structure, context and guardrails. Appian is enhancing interoperabilityacross its AI ecosystem. By adopting powerful standards like Model Context Protocol (MCP), Appian agents will be able to interface securely with external enterprise systems. Third party AI agents will have access to powerful Appian tools like data fabric which uniquely provides unified read-write access to enterprise data. [...] # Company Announcements\n\n### Appian Advances AI in Process to Deliver Enterprise Outcomes at Scale\n\nNew capabilities in agentic automation and AI-assisted spec-driven development transform complex work.\n\nORLANDO, Fla.\n, April 29, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Appian [Nasdaq: APPN] today announced enhancements to the Appian Platform, including AI-assisted spec-driven development and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration for agents. By anchoring AI within processes, Appian eliminates the primary hurdles to AI value: fragmented data, and a lack of reliability and control. Process models provide the structure needed to deliver results safely, and at scale. [...] Appian is also advancing agent learning by providing users the ability to track agent performance, and then apply an agent's memory across processes to improve decision making. Users will soon be able to expand on this by giving AI guidance on what objectives to optimize against and recommend improvements that can be applied safely.\n\nCustomer value\n\nGlobal Excel Management, a worldwide healthcare risk management provider, uses Appian to transform claims processes with AI.",
      "score": 0.66959417,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202604280830PR_NEWS_USPRX____NE44088-1",
      "title": "PTC Launches Windchill AI Assistant to Simplify How Teams Find and Leverage Product Data Across the Enterprise – Company Announcement - FT.com",
      "content": "PTC plans to continue advancing Windchill AI Assistant by expanding how users interact with Windchill data. Future enhancements are expected to add AI agents across additional product domains, including parts and change management, deepen insight into document information, and embed AI‑driven actions directly into workflows. PTC also plans to incorporate broader process and domain knowledge to help guide users as they complete tasks in Windchill. These enhancements are intended to further improve efficiency, usability, and confidence as customers manage increasingly complex product data. [...] build transparency, security, and trust in the solution. The capability is deployed as a plugin, allowing customers to adopt new AI capabilities and enabling rapid innovation while reducing operational disruption. [...] # Company Announcements\n\n### PTC Launches Windchill AI Assistant to Simplify How Teams Find and Leverage Product Data Across the Enterprise\n\nBOSTON\n, April 28, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- PTC (NASDAQ: PTC) today announced the release of Windchill AI Assistant, a new artificial intelligence (AI) capability built in its Windchill® product lifecycle management (PLM) solution+solution). This new capability introduces generative AI into Windchill via a natural language chat interface, making it easier for users to find, understand, and work with critical product information already stored in the platform, reducing time spent searching and enabling increases in team productivity.\n\nImage courtesy of PTC.\n\nImage courtesy of PTC.",
      "score": 0.5760895,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202604290300BIZWIRE_USPRX____20260428_BW783532-1",
      "title": "AI-driven Bot Attacks Surged 12.5x According to Thales Bad Bot Report – Company Announcement - FT.com",
      "content": "More significantly, AI agents are now emerging as a third category of traffic, alongside traditional “good” and “bad” bots, interacting directly with applications and APIs to retrieve data and perform tasks. This shift is blurring the line between legitimate and malicious automation, making it increasingly difficult for organizations to determine intent.\n\n“AI is transforming automation from something organizations try to block into something they must also manage,”Tim Chang, Global Vice President and General Manager, Application Security at Thales, said. “The challenge is no longer identifying bots. It’s understanding what the bot, agent, or automation is doing, whether it aligns with business intent, and how it interacts with critical systems.” [...] ©Thales\n\n©Thales\n\nThe findings highlight three major structural changes: the emergence of AI agents as a new category of internet traffic, the dominance of automated activity over human interaction, and the rapid expansion of attacks targeting APIs and identity systems that serve as the backbone of digital business.\n\nAI Is Redefining Internet Traffic and SecurityThe report shows that AI is not just increasing the volume of bot activity, but fundamentally changing its nature. In 2025, AI-driven bot attacks surged 12.5x compared to the previous year. [...] Confronting the Rise of Uncontrolled AutomationThe report concludes that traditional security approaches focused on identifying and blocking bots are not sufficient in an environment where automation is both pervasive and often legitimate. Organizations must move toward a governance-based model, combining visibility, policy enforcement, and behavioral analysis to distinguish between acceptable and harmful automation. This includes defining which AI agents are allowed to interact with systems, implementing controls at the API and identity layer, and designing defenses that can adapt as bots evolve.\n\nFor more information and recommendations, please download the full report and join our webinar to learn more about technologies that can be deployed against malicious bots.",
      "score": 0.5372119,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202604280900PR_NEWS_USPRX____NY45161-1",
      "title": "Gen and xAI Partner to Build Trusted AI Assistants for Millions of Consumers – Company Announcement - FT.com",
      "content": "About Gen AI FoundryRooted in Gen's commitment to safety and transparency, the Gen AI Foundry is the engine behind its most recent AI innovations, from AI-powered security experiences to autonomous financial helpers and identity agents. It also serves as the place where core trust infrastructure is developed and operationalized, including the Agent Trust Hub, extending protection across the agent lifecycle from verification to execution. Here, Gen's AI strategy becomes real through visible ventures, prototypes, working products, partnerships and an open ecosystem designed to shape and secure the AI era with and for both people and agents. [...] Through this collaboration, Gen's AI Foundry will use xAI's unique access to coherent compute, data, and technology to bring Grok frontier intelligence to the next generation of secure and trusted consumer AI products, beginning with Norton Neo AI Browser and Assistant powered by Grok.\n\n\"As agentic AI systems grow increasingly capable, safely and effectively delivering intelligence to everyday consumers requires robust trust and security infrastructure,\" said Howie Xu, Chief AI & Innovation Officer. \"Through our co-architect partnership, our xAI-powered consumer products will utilize Grok's frontier models to not only answer questions but also take real-world action in a secure and trusted environment.\" [...] From Model Intelligence to Trusted ActionScaling powerful AI responsibly for widespread consumer use benefits from strong safety infrastructure.\n\nAs part of this collaboration:\n\nAll processing is designed by Gen and Norton with privacy and security by default, supported by enterprise-grade protections, including end-to-end encryption and globally recognized security certifications – putting the consumers in control of its privacy.",
      "score": 0.47782278,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/top-announcements-of-the-whats-next-with-aws-2026/",
      "title": "Top announcements of the What’s Next with AWS, 2026 | Amazon Web Services",
      "content": "Amazon Bedrock Managed Agents, powered by OpenAI (Limited preview): Amazon Bedrock Managed Agents combines frontier AI models with trusted AWS infrastructure, enabling customers to quickly and easily build production-ready OpenAI-powered agents in the cloud. It is built with the OpenAI harness, which is engineered to unlock the full potential of OpenAI frontier models, delivering faster execution, sharper reasoning, and reliable steering of long-running tasks. [...] To learn more, visit the About Amazon News post.\n\nAWS and OpenAI expanded partnershipAWS and OpenAI are bringing the latest OpenAI models to Amazon Bedrock, launching Codex on Amazon Bedrock, and launching Amazon Bedrock Managed Agents, powered by OpenAI (all in limited preview), giving enterprises the frontier intelligence they want on the infrastructure they trust. [...] To learn more, visit the About Amazon News post.\n\nAmazon Connectis expanding from a single product into a set of four agentic AI solutions designed to work within your existing workflows: Amazon Connect Decisions (supply chains), Talent (hiring), Customer (customer experience), and Health (health care).",
      "score": 0.46964246,

    {
      "url": "https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202604290600PR_NEWS_EURO_ND__EN46229-1",
      "title": "Cognizant to Acquire Astreya, Deepening Its AI-First Managed Services Capabilities at Scale – Company Announcement - FT.com",
      "content": "Astreya's AI OpsHub, with modules for readiness assessment, signal intelligence, analytics, and agentic automation, gives Cognizant a ready-built operations engine already generating measurable results. In addition to the Tech Innovation Office, Astreya's bespoke client AI solutions, extensive managed services capabilities and its ecosystem partnerships anchored by Google Cloud Platform and ServiceNow will become key assets within Cognizant's global delivery infrastructure. [...] Founded in 2001 and operating across more than thirty-five countries, Astreya brings a 25-year track record as a trusted partner to the world's largest technology companies, including long-term, outcome-based managed services relationships with six of the \"Magnificent Seven\" hyperscalers. Its proprietary AI OpsHub platform, with modules for readiness assessment, signal intelligence, analytics, and agentic automation and Tech Innovation Office are expected to deepen Cognizant's AI talent pool and strengthen its production-grade AI delivery capabilities, translating broad AI potential into specific business outcomes and enterprise-ready platforms tailored to individual client contexts. Combined with Cognizant's hybrid-by-design AI infrastructure strategy, Astreya's differentiated expertise [...] \"Astreya has redefined what it means to be a trusted partner in the AI era, embedding intelligence into every solution, without losing the human connection that drives real results. Joining Cognizant is the natural next chapter for the Astreya global team and importantly, the clients who have trusted us to operate their most critical technology environments. We have spent the last several years making deliberate, disciplined investments in AI: building platforms, training specialists and fundamentally redesigning how managed services are delivered. We look forward to attacking the AI infrastructure era as a part of Cognizant!\" said Romil Bahl, President and CEO, Astreya.",
      "score": 0.41037872,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.bain.com/insights/google_cloud_next_2026_the_agentic_enterprise_control_plane_comes_into_view/",
      "title": "Google Cloud Next 2026: The Agentic Enterprise Control Plane ...",
      "content": "The security announcements reinforced the same message. Google positioned Agentic Defense as a central part of the enterprise AI stack, with new Google Security Operations agents for threat hunting, detection engineering, and third-party context, as well as tighter integration with Wiz. The broader security story included Wiz AI Application Protection Platform, Wiz Security Agents, Wiz Workflows, Model Armor integration with Agent Gateway, and new Agent Identities designed to reduce shadow AI and secure agent traffic. [...] That matters because grounded AI is evolving. It is no longer enough for agents to retrieve documents or query databases. To work reliably in enterprise settings, agents need a governed and repeatable way to access metadata, permissions, source-of-truth relationships, and semantic structure that persist across systems and clouds.\n\nGrounded AI is evolving. Context is becoming a managed platform layer, not a custom pattern rebuilt for every use case.\n\nThat is why Knowledge Catalog may turn out to be one of the more strategic pieces of the announcement set. It suggests Google is trying to make context a managed platform layer rather than something rebuilt use case by use case through custom retrieval patterns. [...] That is why Google’s infrastructure story reads less like a chip announcement and more like a systems management argument. The platform is being positioned not just to run AI, but to absorb the operational consequences of running AI at scale.\n\nFinOps is part of that same control story. Google introduced a FinOps Explainability agent and project-level Spend Caps, explicitly linking AI adoption to deeper cost visibility and tighter budget controls.\n\nThat is strategically important because it suggests Google sees AI economics as part of the platform itself rather than as an after-the-fact reporting exercise. As usage scales, cost governance will need to sit closer to routing policy, workload design, and business value measurement.",
      "score": 0.40967003,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.reuters.com/business/retail-consumer/amazon-targets-mass-hiring-with-agentic-software-goal-humanize-ai-2026-04-28/",
      "title": "Amazon targets mass hiring with agentic software, goal to humanize AI",
      "content": "A focus of the event is autonomous artificial intelligence software, known as \"agents,\" that can run processes with ​little to no human intervention. The hope is that such agents can plan, decide and act on their own, a fast-growing field that has also sparked concerns over safety and oversight.\n\nAdvertisement · Scroll to continue\n\nAlphabet (GOOGL.O), opens new tab last week signaled it is also pushing deeper into enterprise software with its own AI agents, following others like ​OpenAI and Anthropic.\n\nAmazon's new mass hiring software, called Connect Talent, will help firms find, screen and recruit workers needed for large-scale ​hiring, such as retailers during the peak holiday selling season. [...] The Week in Breakingviews newsletter offers insights and ideas from Reuters' global financial commentary team. Sign up here.\n\nAdvertisement · Scroll to continue\n\nReport AdImage 3\n\nThe company announced ​the software offerings at an event where the CEO of Amazon Web Services, Matt Garman, as well as executives ​from OpenAI, are expected to appear.\n\nAmazon in February said it would invest up to $50 billion in OpenAI ⁠and Microsoft (MSFT.O), opens new tab said on Monday it would lose exclusive access to some of OpenAI's technology, clearing the path for the ChatGPT creator ​to sell its products to others. [...] The company on Tuesday also introduced a new product called Connect Decisions, which can analyze and compile ⁠data for ​supply chain planning and purchasing. Aubrey said Amazon's own supply chain experiences, such ​as materials for its network of warehouses, helped create the new software.\n\nWith Connect Decisions, companies will be \"able to have AI do that work behind the scenes ​and be able to equip a planner with the data that they need,\" she said.\n\nReporting by Greg Bensinger; Editing by Sriraj Kalluvila\n\nOur Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles., opens new tab\n\n   Suggested Topics:\n   Artificial Intelligence\n\n    \n    \n    \n    \n    \n\nPurchase Licensing Rights\n\n\n   , opens new tab\n\n### Stay Informed",
      "score": 0.3045797,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/bedrock-openai-models-codex-managed-agents/",
      "title": "Amazon Bedrock now offers OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed ...",
      "content": "Skip to main content\n\n\n\n Posted on:  Apr 28, 2026 \n\nAWS and OpenAI are expanding their partnership to bring frontier intelligence to the infrastructure millions of organizations already trust. Enterprises want the most capable AI models and agents, with the security, operational maturity, and data governance that production workloads demand. Today,we’re bringing those together with three new offerings on Amazon Bedrock, all in limited preview: the latest OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed Agents powered by OpenAI. [...] toward existing AWS cloud commitments.Lastly, Amazon Bedrock Managed Agents, powered by OpenAI, makes it fast to deploy production-ready OpenAI-powered agents on AWS.At the core are the latest OpenAI frontier models and the OpenAI agent harness, engineered for faster execution, sharper reasoning, and reliable steering of long-running tasks. Every agent has its own identity,logs each action, and runs in your environment with all inference on Amazon Bedrock. Managed Agents works with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, which provides the default compute environment. [...] # Amazon Bedrock now offers OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed Agents (Limited Preview) - AWS\n\n## Select your cookie preferences\n\nWe use essential cookies and similar tools that are necessary to provide our site and services. We use performance cookies to collect anonymous statistics, so we can understand how customers use our site and make improvements. Essential cookies cannot be deactivated, but you can choose “Customize” or “Decline” to decline performance cookies. \n\n If you agree, AWS and approved third parties will also use cookies to provide useful site features, remember your preferences, and display relevant content, including relevant advertising. To accept or decline all non-essential cookies, choose “Accept” or “Decline.” To make more detailed choices, choose “Customize.”",
      "score": 0.28173202,

    {
      "url": "https://www.reuters.com/business/retail-consumer/openai-brings-latest-ai-models-codex-coding-agent-amazon-bedrock-2026-04-28/",
      "title": "OpenAI's latest AI models, Codex now available on Amazon Bedrock",
      "content": "# OpenAI's latest AI models, Codex now available on Amazon Bedrock | Reuters\n\nSkip to main content\n\nExclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionals Learn more about Refinitiv\n\n - OpenAI is now offering its latest AI ​models and its Codex coding agent on Amazon's (AMZN.O), opens new tab cloud services platform, the companies said on ‌Tuesday, a day after the ChatGPT creator loosened its ties with long-time backer Microsoft (MSFT.O), opens new tab.\n\nOpenAI is also rolling out a service for developers to build and deploy advanced AI agents with its frontier models on Amazon Web Services.\n\nThe Week in Breakingviews newsletter offers insights and ideas from Reuters' global financial commentary team. Sign up here. [...] Advertisement · Scroll to continue\n\nFor OpenAI, making Codex available on AWS will help drive enterprise adoption and ​expand the reach of ​the coding agent, which ⁠has more than 4 million weekly active users.\n\nFor Amazon, being able to fully host and distribute OpenAI's products would allow it to expand beyond its reliance on ​Anthropic.\n\nThis bodes well for Amazon especially when it has pulled out all ​the stops in ⁠its efforts to reassure investors that its hefty AI investments are starting to pay off. [...] Amazon and OpenAI have been deepening their ties for several months now. While Amazon invested $50 billion in OpenAI, the ​AI startup committed to spend $100 billion on AWS over the next eight years. The companies have also ​reached an agreement for OpenAI to use two gigawatts of compute powered by Amazon's in-house Trainium AI chips.\n\nThe latest announcement ‌comes ⁠at a time when OpenAI is racing to secure more computing capacity to beef up its enterprise business and better compete with rival Anthropic, whose Claude models have surged in popularity and made the startup a front-runner in the AI race.\n\nAdvertisement · Scroll to continue",
      "score": 0.2245911,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202604281000BIZWIRE_USPRX____20260428_BW567428-1",
      "title": "U.S. Firms Boost ServiceNow's Impact with AI, Transparency",
      "content": "Many of these enterprises are embedding AI capabilities directly into both new ServiceNow deployments and ongoing operations. This includes agent-based systems supported by governance frameworks that include audit trails, control mechanisms and human oversight. This approach enables organizations to scale automation while maintaining compliance and reducing operational risk. Companies in highly regulated industries are especially focused on ensuring AI systems remain transparent and responsive to policy requirements. [...] # Company Announcements\n\n### U.S. Firms Boost ServiceNow’s Impact with AI, Transparency\n\nCompanies consolidate platforms, implement AI-enabled workflows to improve outcomes, compliance, ISG Provider Lens® report says\n\nSTAMFORD, Conn.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Apr. 28, 2026--\nEnterprises in the U.S. are transforming ServiceNow deployments into governed platforms that integrate AI, observability and standardized architectures for measurable business outcomes, according to a new research report published today by Information Services Group (ISG) (Nasdaq: III), a global AI-centered technology research and advisory firm. [...] The 2026 ISG Provider Lens®ServiceNow Ecosystem Partners report for the U.S. finds that companies are moving from project-based ServiceNow implementations to advisory-led modernization, using value engineering and continuous performance tracking to ensure platform investments meaningfully improve operations.\n\n“In the U.S., enterprise ServiceNow agendas have moved from modernization to measurable autonomy, and from projects delivered to platforms governed,” said Bill Huber, partner, Digital Platforms and Solutions, at ISG. “Buyers expect agentic AI to operate under explicit controls, observability to drive self-healing operations and upgrade-safe design to keep innovation adoptable.”",
      "score": 0.20875941,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.snowflake.com/en/blog/ai-agent-identity-governance-enterprise-trust/",
      "title": "The AI Agent Identity Problem: Why Governance Is the ... - Snowflake",
      "content": "Cloud icon\nMigrate icon\nPartner Network icon\nPartner Finder icon\nCalendar icon\nCustomer icon\nCloud icon\nUser with security lock icon\nCost Optimization icon\nLaunch\nNotebooks icon\nTraining icon\nWebinars icon\nCertification icon\nLive Demo icon\nEducation icon\nHands-on Labs icon\nCopy\nDocument with list\nDevelopers icon\nSolution Center icon\nDownload icon\nDocs icon\nOpen Source icon\nNorthstar logo\nDevelopers icon\nPartner Network icon\n\nLanguage\n\n##### Category\n\n# Your AI Agent Is a Nobody. And That’s a Problem.\n\nWe've spent the past two years making AI agents capable. They can query your databases, summarize your documents, route your workflows, and initiate transactions on your behalf. Some of them are genuinely impressive. [...] The result: our agent now empowers over 6,000+ employees and answers over 35,000 questions per week — an agent our teams trust to operate autonomously, with full auditability after the fact.\n\nAt scale, we’re supporting our customers in the same way. Customers across companies like TS Imagine, Fanatics and United Rentals are all building agents on Snowflake to accelerate their business. [...] LendingTree, an online lending marketplace, for example, uses Snowflake Cortex Code to rapidly build and deploy AI agents that deliver personalized financial guidance to consumers. The platform enables their teams to move from idea exploration to production in days rather than weeks, powering smarter financial decisioning workflows and more tailored consumer experiences that help borrowers navigate complex lending options.\n\n## Solving agent identity is your silver bullet for enterprise AI adoption\n\nSolving agent identity doesn't just reduce risk. It removes the friction that's stalling adoption.",

      "title": "Workday Aus & NZ Data Cloud: Enterprise Data Management & Analytics | Workday Aus & NZ",
      "content": "Advanced analytics and AI.\n\nBy embracing open industry standards like Apache Iceberg and JDBC, we maximize flexibility and avoid integration vendor lock-in, protecting your Workday investment as your data strategy evolves.\n\nAdvanced analytics and AI.\n\nBy embracing open industry standards like Apache Iceberg and JDBC, we maximize flexibility and avoid integration vendor lock-in, protecting your Workday investment as your data strategy evolves.\n\n## AI Agents\n\n### Go all in on agentic AI—simply and securely.\n\nWorkday Data Cloud uses trusted people and finance data to power Workday Agents, partner agents, and your custom agents built on Workday Build. This unified foundation ensures all agents act directly within your business processes with governance and audit controls. [...] ### Go all in on agentic AI—simply and securely.\n\nWorkday Data Cloud uses trusted people and finance data to power Workday Agents, partner agents, and your custom agents built on Workday Build. [...] ## Products\n\n### AI\n\nDiscover superintelligence for the new world of work.\n\n#### Artificial Intelligence\n\n#### Sana from Workday\n\n#### Agent System of Record\n\n#### Agents\n\n#### Responsible AI\n\n#### AI Masterclass\n\n### HR\n\nEmpower your people and adapt your workforce to the future of work.\n\n#### All HR Software & Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Human Capital Management\n\n#### Human Resource Management\n\n#### Workday Paradox\n\n#### Talent Management\n\n#### Workforce Management\n\n#### Employee Experience\n\n#### HR Service Delivery\n\n#### Employee Voice\n\n#### Workforce Planning\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting\n\n#### Payroll Software\n\n#### Vendor Management System\n\n### Finance\n\nDrive innovation, manage costs, and make smarter finance decisions, faster.\n\n#### All Finance Software & Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS",

      "content": "- Cloud integration brokerage\n    - Enterprise integration services\n    - Oracle\n    - Pega\n    - Process orchestration\n    - SAP\n    - ServiceNow\n    - Supply chain management\n    - Workday\n  + Experience\n\n    Experience\n\n    Optimize the employee and customer experiences of tomorrow with Cognizant Moment™.\n\n    - Customer service transformation\n    - Future of customer experience\n  + IoT & engineering\n\n    IoT & engineering\n\n    Let’s design bold solutions: Intelligent, connected, autonomous—built for scale.\n\n    - Enterprise GIS & geospatial solutions\n    - Intelligent automotive & mobility\n    - Intelligent operations\n    - Smart field services\n    - Smart products\n    - Sustainability services\n\n    WHITE PAPER\n\n    ##### Engineering AI into the factory of the future [...] Know more\n\n  \n\n  Digital engineering\n\n  Innovate with AI-led digital engineering\n\n  Learn how\n\n  ##### Legacy modernization\n\n  Outdated legacy systems can drain up to 80% of IT budgets. With our Human + AI approach, we reimagine these systems as engines of growth.\n\n  Lean more\n\n  \n\n  Engineering research & development\n\n  Boost operational efficiency, optimize costs and speed product development.\n\n  Get started\n\n  Enterprise platforms\n\n  Transform operations through intelligent orchestration, platform integration and strategic partnerships.\n\n  See how\n\n  + Cloud integration brokerage\n  + Enterprise integration services\n  + Oracle\n  + Pega\n  + Process orchestration\n  + SAP\n  + ServiceNow\n  + Supply chain management\n  + Workday\n\n  Experience [...] ##### Neuro® Cybersecurity\n\n    Orchestrating point solutions with AI for a holistic view of cyber threats, revolutionizing risk, threat and vulnerability management, and ensuring comprehensive security coverage.\n\n    Learn more\n  + Data & AI\n\n    Data & AI\n\n    Leading innovation with intelligence.\n\n    - AI & data advisory\n    - AI solutions\n    - Build and scale agentic AI\n    - Data & AI management\n    - Data modernization\n    - Enterprise AI agents\n    - Generative AI\n    - Modernize with AI\n    - Physical AI\n\n    COGNIZANT AI LAB\n\n    ##### Driving research and innovation using decision AI\n\n    Explore how Cognizant AI Lab advances decision AI through groundbreaking research, real-world systems, and tools that enable smarter decision-making.",

      "title": "Adaptive Planning: Enterprise Planning Software (EPM) | Workday HK & TW | Workday HK",
      "content": "TrustRadius Workday Adaptive Planning Top Rated\n\nG2 Top Finance Products Workday Adaptive Planning\n\n## AI FOR WORKDAY ADAPTIVE PLANNING\n\n### Planning Agent\n\nFind out why over 100 customers trust the Planning Agent to drive smarter decisions with conversational, dynamic prompts.\n\nLearn More\n\n \n\n## PARTNER ECOSYSTEM\n\n### Workday Marketplace\n\nDiscover custom apps from our trusted partner ecosystem to solve frontline operational challenges.\n\nLearn More\n\n \n\nHubspot logo\n\n“With Workday Adaptive Planning, my team can complete a forecast cycle in 15-20% less time than before.”\n\n—Vice President of Financial Planning and Analysis\n\nRead Story\n\nOne platform for every plan and decision.\n\nConnect financial, workforce, and operational plans for a full view across your business.\n\n### Financial planning. [...] # EPM Software\n\nUnlock limitless decision intelligence.\n\nFuel your next era of growth by keeping your brightest minds focused on strategy instead of manual workarounds—and discover why more cloud customers trust Workday Adaptive Planning than our largest competitors, combined.\n\nPlay Video (3:02)   Learn More\n\n \n\n### Make smarter decisions with AI-powered  planning.\n\nAnticipate what’s next.\n\nDrive smarter outcomes with AI-powered intelligence, backed by human-in-the-loop governance and grounded in enterprise-wide data.\n\nScale your impact.\n\nOperationalize any data source with automated connections—no storage limits or performance lag—just AI-powered insights that grow with your business.\n\nNavigate with ease. [...] ### Financial planning.\n\nFlexible, AI-driven budgeting, scenario planning, and reporting let you plan, budget, and forecast with confidence.\n\nLearn More\n\n### Workforce planning.\n\nPlan for the skills you need today and tomorrow with flexible hiring plans that align finance, sales, HR, marketing, and IT.\n\nLearn More\n\n### Operational planning.\n\nCompanywide, collaborative planning and real-time data enable you to deploy the right resources to meet business goals.\n\nLearn More\n\n### Close and consolidation.\n\nStreamline close and consolidation tasks for more accuracy, transparency, and efficiency in your consolidation process.\n\nLearn More\n\n### Planning FAQs.\n\n### How quickly can Workday Adaptive Planning be deployed?",

      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-au/why-workday/our-technology/data-cloud.html",
      "title": "Workday Data Cloud: Enterprise Data Management & Analytics",
      "content": "Advanced analytics and AI.\n\nBy embracing open industry standards like Apache Iceberg and JDBC, we maximize flexibility and avoid integration vendor lock-in, protecting your Workday investment as your data strategy evolves.\n\nAdvanced analytics and AI.\n\nBy embracing open industry standards like Apache Iceberg and JDBC, we maximize flexibility and avoid integration vendor lock-in, protecting your Workday investment as your data strategy evolves.\n\n## AI Agents\n\n### Go all in on agentic AI—simply and securely.\n\nWorkday Data Cloud uses trusted people and finance data to power Workday Agents, partner agents, and your custom agents built on Workday Build. This unified foundation ensures all agents act directly within your business processes with governance and audit controls. [...] ### Go all in on agentic AI—simply and securely.\n\nWorkday Data Cloud uses trusted people and finance data to power Workday Agents, partner agents, and your custom agents built on Workday Build. This unified foundation ensures all agents act directly within your business processes with governance and audit controls.\n\n''\nSalesforce logo\n\n“With Workday as part of our Zero-Copy Partner Network, we are giving customers secure access to comprehensive HR, finance, and CRM data on our connected platform.”\n\n“With Workday as part of our Zero-Copy Partner Network, we are giving customers secure access to comprehensive HR, finance, and CRM data on our connected platform.”\n\n## FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS\n\n### FAQs.\n\n### FAQs.       "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-us/midsize-business/overview.html",
      "title": "Midsize Business Software & Solutions | Workday GO",
      "content": "### The simplicity and AI you need at the price you want.\n\nOnly Workday simplifies your back office with one unified solution for HR, payroll, and finance that’s simple to buy, fast to activate, and designed for midsize budgets. Governed AI agents, powered by Sana, handle more of the everyday work, so your team spends less time on routine tasks and more time on your business from day one.\n\n### The simplicity and AI you need at the price you want.\n\nOnly Workday simplifies your back office with one unified solution for HR, payroll, and finance that’s simple to buy, fast to activate, and designed for midsize budgets. Governed AI agents, powered by Sana, handle more of the everyday work, so your team spends less time on routine tasks and more time on your business from day one. [...] Activated Workday GO in 8 weeks.\n\nVertex, Inc. logo\n\nScaled for global growth and operations.\n\nKainos logo\n\nExecuted reorganization in 1 day.\n\nOneSource Virtual logo\n\nReduced time to close from 30 days to 8 days.\n\n## RESOURCES\n\n### Resources for midsize businesses.\n\n### Resources for midsize businesses.\n\n### Questions? We have answers.\n\n### Questions? We have answers.\n\nThe built-in AI in Workday GO helps small and midsize businesses adopt enterprise AI affordably and without the complexity of building custom systems. Key benefits of our platform include:\n\nAI-powered workflows that automate many of the routine, time-consuming tasks that are common in human resources and finance.\n\nBuilt-in AI that helps organizations make more informed decisions by providing predictive insights [...] ## Products\n\n### AI\n\nDiscover superintelligence for the new world of work.\n\n#### Artificial Intelligence\n\n#### Sana from Workday\n\n#### Agent System of Record\n\n#### Agents\n\n#### Responsible AI\n\n#### AI Masterclass\n\n### HR\n\nEmpower your people and adapt your workforce to the future of work.\n\n#### HR Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Human Capital Management\n\n#### Human Resource Management\n\n#### Workday Paradox\n\n#### Talent Management\n\n#### Workforce Management\n\n#### Employee Experience\n\n#### HR Service Delivery\n\n#### Employee Voice\n\n#### Workforce Planning\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting\n\n#### Vendor Management System\n\n#### Local & Global Payroll\n\n### Finance\n\nDrive innovation, manage costs, and make smarter finance decisions, faster.\n\n#### Finance Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS",
      "score": 0.3667717,
      "raw_content": null

    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-ca/homepage.html",
      "title": "Workday Canada | The Enterprise AI Platform for HR & Finance",

    {
      "url": "https://news.microsoft.com/signal/articles/work-smarter-in-90-days-a-real-world-guide-to-using-ai/",
      "title": "Work smarter in 90 days: A real-world guide to using AI",
      "content": "Microsoft AI Microsoft Security Dynamics 365 Microsoft 365 Microsoft Power Platform Microsoft Teams Microsoft 365 Copilot Small Business   Azure Microsoft Developer Microsoft Learn Support for AI marketplace apps Microsoft Tech Community Microsoft Marketplace Marketplace Rewards Visual Studio   Careers About Microsoft Company news Privacy at Microsoft Investors Diversity and inclusion Accessibility Sustainability\n\n English (United States)\n\nYour Privacy Choices\n\nConsumer Health Privacy  Sitemap Contact Microsoft Privacy  Manage cookies Terms of use Trademarks Safety & eco Recycling About our ads [...] “The most important truth about this moment is that the outcome isn’t written yet,” Roslansky says. “The new world of work is being assembled right now, task by task, policy by policy, business by business. It will reflect the choices of the people who show up to build it.”\n\nSo if you’re looking to get started, here are the basics of their step-by-step plan — along with some specific AI prompts you can use along the way.\n\n## Days 1–30:  Build your base\n\nShifting your workday toward using AI tools and adding in uniquely human tasks can help you become more efficient and more valuable at the same time — so it’s helpful take a quick inventory of what you do and how AI fits in. List your top 12 daily or weekly tasks, then sort them into three buckets: [...] Tasks AI can do alone, like routine reports, data entry, scheduling\n Tasks you can do with AI tools, like strategic analysis with AI insights or creative work with AI as a partner\n Uniquely human tasks, like building trust, making ethical decisions and reading a room\n\nRestructure your time based on what you learn\n\nIf most of your tasks are in the first bucket, you may have time to take on new projects or work with more impact. If the second bucket is nearly empty, you might be missing the chance to incorporate AI more as a tool. If the third bucket is less than half your work, consider spending more time using or building your own unique human skills.\n\nStart small and stay consistent",
      "score": 0.20702262,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.bain.com/insights/google_cloud_next_2026_the_agentic_enterprise_control_plane_comes_into_view/",
      "title": "Google Cloud Next 2026: The Agentic Enterprise Control Plane ...",
      "content": "The open ecosystem story remains important. Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform offers access to more than 200 models through Model Garden, including Gemini 3.1 Pro, Gemini 3.1 Flash Image, Lyria 3, Gemma 4, and third-party models such as Anthropic’s Claude. The partner layer expanded as well through Agent Gallery and partner-built agents from Accenture, Adobe, Atlassian, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Workday, and others.\n\nBut the more interesting question is not whether Google is open. It is where Google is open. [...] Headshot of Dale Pedzinski\n\n##### Dale Pedzinski\n\n##### Expert Associate Partner, Dallas\n\n###### Related Consulting Services\n\n###### How We Can Help\n\n##### People and Organization\n\n###### Want More Out of Your AI Investments? Think People First\n\nTo unlock AI’s exponential productivity potential, companies must modernize workflow and workforce in tandem.\n\n##### CIO Insights\n\n###### The Rise of Autonomous, Intelligent Procurement\n\nEarly movers are redesigning procurement with agentic AI and pulling ahead.\n\n##### CIO Insights\n\n###### Tech Services Buyer Survey: Betting Big on AI and Resilience\n\nCompanies have moved beyond curiosity and now demand results and returns from their AI investment.\n\n##### CIO Insights\n\n###### Will IT Accelerate or Stall Your AI Transformation? [...] The enterprise challenge is shifting from experimentation to control. That means choosing where runtime governance will live, investing in context architecture instead of treating retrieval as a one-off engineering task, strengthening non-human identity and authorization models, and defining clear boundaries around autonomy before agent deployment outpaces oversight.\n\nThat is what makes this moment more important than a standard AI product launch.\n\nThe pressing question for enterprise leaders is no longer whether agents are coming. It is whether their organization is building the control plane required to govern them.\n\n###### Authors\n\nHeadshot of Chris Green\n\n##### Chris Green\n\n##### Partner, Atlanta\n\nHeadshot of Dale Pedzinski\n\n##### Dale Pedzinski",
      "score": 0.12509769,
      "raw_content": null
    },

  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://news.sap.com/2024/10/new-ai-sap-successfactors-hcm-suite-workforce-management-upskilling-organizational-evolution",
      "title": "New AI Across the SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite | SAP News Center",
      "content": "Title: New AI Across the SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite | SAP News Center\nSAP Announces New AI Across the SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite to Enhance Workforce Management, Upskilling and Organizational Evolution. # SAP Announces New AI Across the SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite to Enhance Workforce Management, Upskilling and Organizational Evolution. ***LISBON*** — SAP SE (NYSE: SAP) today announced new innovations and embedded artificial intelligence (AI) across the SAP SuccessFactors HCM suite that provides organizations with greater efficiencies and unparalleled experiences. “Our announcements today represent a major leap toward a future where every organization has a single source of truth for its people data and skills intelligence, enabling data-driven decisions across the entire business,” said Dan Beck, President and Chief Product Officer, SAP SuccessFactors solutions. SAP also has launched the SAP SuccessFactors Career and Talent Development solution, a fully integrated offering, which combines the robust features of the SAP SuccessFactors Succession & Development solution with the SAP SuccessFactors Opportunity Marketplace solution, powered by skills data from the talent intelligence hub. SAP leads the market with strong global core HR solutions, providing customers with country-specific compliance, content and support in more places than any other cloud HR vendor – 104 for the SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central solution and 52 for the SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll solution. Additionally, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll will include enhanced Payroll Control Center capabilities to manage payroll process activities and improve budgeting and control for public sector customers. At SuccessConnect, SAP announced its intent to add pre-built WalkMe content into SAP SuccessFactors solutions which will be commercially available in the first half of 2025, allowing customers to improve employee experience and adoption across common workflows. “Both leaders need to continuously align employee growth, goals, and motivators to ever-evolving sets of business OKRs. SAP SuccessFactors is furthering employee experience management while supporting HR and people managers to better partner with employees to mutually value their growth and engagement. From AI modeling and forecasting to dynamic skills and value framing, SAP SuccessFactors newest releases raise the bar for how to optimize and align employee experiences with the goals and needs of the enterprise.”. SAP SuccessFactors HCM: Building an AI-Driven Learning and Talent Strategy for Future Success. ### SAP SuccessFactors HCM: Building an AI-Driven Learning and Talent Strategy for Future Success.",
      "score": 0.86954874,
      "raw_content": null

      "content": "# Understanding SAP Business AI for SAP SuccessFactors HCM\n\nObjective\n\n## SAP Business AI Strategy Overview\n\nDiscover SAP Business AI, the suite of artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities that infuses intelligence into business applications. It highlights how SAP integrates AI with industry-specific data and deep process knowledge to develop innovative AI capabilities that drive their SAP applications.\n\n## Joule [...] ## Joule\n\nJoule is an advanced AI co-pilot embedded in SAP's cloud enterprise portfolio.       "title": "Introducing SAP Business AI for SAP SuccessFactors HCM",
      "content": "# Introducing SAP Business AI for SAP SuccessFactors HCM\n\nImage for Introducing SAP Business AI for SAP SuccessFactors HCM  banner\n\n## Learning outcome\n\nAfter completing this course, participants will be able to explain how SAP Business AI transforms cloud services, identify how SAP Business AI is infused in SAP SuccessFactors HCM products, provide examples of embedded AI in customer scenarios, and understand the legal issues related to using AI in SAP solutions. This knowledge will help them use SAP Business AI to improve processes within their organization.\n\n## Goals\n\nGet started with SAP\n\nDevelop your expertise\n\nExcel in your expertise\n\n## Course information\n\n## What you'll learn\n\n### Understanding SAP Business AI\n\n### Identifying SAP Business AI Offerings for SAP SuccessFactors HCM [...] ### Analyzing Embedded AI Use Cases\n\n### Navigating Resources for Deeper Understanding of Embedded AI\n\n### Goals\n\nGet started with SAP\n\nDevelop your expertise\n\nExcel in your expertise\n\n### Products and Roles\n\nDeveloper\n\nBusiness User\n\nSales\n\nPresales\n\nCEE\n\nConsultant\n\nMarketing\n\nSF other/cross\n\n### Prerequisites\n\nDiscovering SAP SuccessFactors HCM Solutions\n\n### Appears in\n\nSAP Logo\n\nLearning\n\n###### Quick links\n\n###### Learning Support\n\n###### About SAP\n\n###### Site Information\n\nSAP Facebook\nSAP Youtube\nSAP LinkedIn",

      "content": "# Exploring SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite\n\nObjective\n\n## SAP SuccessFactors HCM Overview\n\nSAP SuccessFactors Human Capital Management (HCM) is a suite of cloud-based HCM software applications that supports Core HR and Payroll, Talent Management, and HR Analytics and Reporting solutions to help drive your business forward.\n\nSAP understands that everything about work is changing. That's why SAP SuccessFactors HCM enables you to put your people at the center of everything – with AI-enabled solutions designed to meet individual needs and drive organizational agility at scale.\n\nSAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite includes cloud-based solutions comprised of these areas:\n\nThe image shows a diagram of the Human Capital Management Suite and the solution areas.\n\n## Summary\n\nSAP Logo\n\nLearning [...] ## Summary\n\nSAP Logo\n\nLearning\n\n###### Quick links\n\n###### Learning Support\n\n###### About SAP\n\n###### Site Information\n\nSAP Facebook\nSAP Youtube\nSAP LinkedIn",

    {
      "url": "https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discovering-the-sap-successfactors-hcm-portfolio/exploring-the-sap-successfactors-hcm-portfolio",
      "title": "Exploring the SAP SuccessFactors HCM Portfolio - SAP Learning",
      "content": "Exploring the SAP SuccessFactors HCM Portfolio / Browse / Learning Journeys / Discovering the SAP SuccessFactors HCM Portfolio / Exploring the SAP SuccessFactors HCM Portfolio Introducing SAP and the Overall Portfolio * Summarizing SAP's Cloud Strategy 10 mins * Explaining the Role of SAP SuccessFactors HCM 5 mins Connecting HCM to End-to-End Processes – Recruit to Retire Exploring the Key Solutions of the HCM Suite Integrating and Evaluating the Value of SAP Cross-Portfolio Components with HCM portfolio Tell us what you think We want to create the best possible experience for our learners. SAP SuccessFactors HCM covers all aspects of HR, including core HR and payroll, talent management, HR analytics and workforce planning, and employee experience management. **SAP products:** SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Service Center, SAP SuccessFactors Document Management Core by OpenText, SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. To learn more (SAP Universal ID required): Core HR and Payroll Management Software #### Talent management solutions * Adapt quickly to address any skill gaps with powerful software for talent intelligence, recruiting, onboarding, performance, and compensation. **Our products:** SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting, SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding, SAP Signature Management by Docusign, SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals, SAP SuccessFactors Learning, SAP SuccessFactors Succession & Development, SAP SuccessFactors Opportunity Marketplace. To learn more (SAP Universal ID required): Talent Management #### Employee experience management solutions * Understand the key drivers of engagement. **Our products:** SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone, SAP Employee Lifecycle by Qualtrics, SAP Candidate Experience by Qualtrics, SAP Employee Technology Experience by Qualtrics, SAP Employee Engagement Bundle by Qualtrics, To learn more (SAP Universal ID required): Employee Experience Management #### HR analytics and workforce planning solutions * Improve company performance and decision-making with data-driven people insights, workforce planning tools, and AI-driven recommendations. **Our products:** SAP SuccessFactors Workforce Analytics, workforce planning with SAP Analytics Cloud To learn more (SAP Universal ID required): SAP SuccessFactors Workforce Analytics #### Sales performance management solutions * Maximize sales performance with effective sales planning, automated processes, and impactful incentive compensation management. To learn more (SAP Universal ID required): Sales Performance Management Log in to track your progress & complete quizzes ###### Quick Links ###### Learning Support ###### About SAP ###### Site Information",
      "score": 0.5367749,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/03-sap-successfactors-hcm-implementation-phase-0-customer-readiness-and/ba-p/14292649",
      "title": "03 SAP SuccessFactors HCM Implementation - Phase 0... - SAP Community",
      "content": "7. Testing Strategy and execution\n\nDevelop a well-defined testing strategy and designate accountability. Comprehensive testing is essential to mitigate post-go-live disruptions and enhance overall satisfaction with the SAP SuccessFactors HCM solution.\n\n8. Training and Sustainability Strategy:\n\nThe success of an SAP SuccessFactors HCM implementation depends heavily on user adoption and the system’s continued relevance. Ensure that all users—including employees, managers, HR, and system administrators—are proficient with the platform, and that the system remains optimized and aligned with evolving business needs well beyond the initial go-live..\n\n9. Resource Allocation and Planning: [...] HR data is used by many internal and external applications, so implementing SAP SuccessFactors HCM requires thinking beyond just HR. From an enterprise architecture and infrastructure perspective, customers should involve enterprise and solution architects to assess the impact on the technology stack, help align the project with the overall technology architecture, and ensure that architecture, design, and adoption stay aligned with the broader strategy.\n\ncus3.jpg\n\ncus3.jpg\n\n         Check the blog below for clean core guidelines for SAP SuccessFactors HCM.\n\n4. Business and HR Ownership, Module Leads [...] 4. Business and HR Ownership, Module Leads\n\nClear lines of accountability are essential to ensure the implementation meets business requirements and is adopted effectively. A designated workstream (module) lead should be identified for each SAP SuccessFactors HCM module, including Integration, Testing, and Data Migration. Each module lead will act as the primary point of contact for their assigned scope.",

      "raw_content": null
    }

{

  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://www.cognizant.com/uk/en/insights/blog/articles/the-new-ways-of-working-leading-with-agent-powered-hybrid-teams",
      "title": "The New Ways of Working: Leading with Agent-Powered Hybrid ...",
      "content": "##### What Hybrid Teams Actually Look Like\n\nA hybrid team is not this #copilot phase, where it’s \"humans plus chatbots.\" But rather is an intentionally designed operating unit where human professionals and AI agents share a workflow, with clearly defined decision rights, supervision patterns, and escalation boundaries.\n\nConsider how Stanford's AI researchers describe the top practitioners in this space: the best engineers are not writing more code.\n\n They are orchestrating agent workflows.\n They start with a single agent handling one well-scoped task.\n They verify it works.\n Then they add a second agent for an independent, isolated task.\n Then a third. Each addition is deliberate — not a \"throw ten agents at the problem\" approach.\n\nThe new critical skill? Context Management [switching]. [...] Yet most enterprise operating models were designed for a world where every task was performed by a human. The org chart assumes human decision-making at every node. The approval workflows assume human reviewers. The escalation paths assume human judgement. An agent that can resolve 90% of Level 1 IT service requests — as ServiceNow's Autonomous Workforce is already demonstrating — does not need a quarterly performance review. It needs a governance framework, an escalation policy, and a human supervisor who understands when to intervene.\n\nThat is a fundamentally differentoperating model.\n\nThe question is no longer whether AI agents will change how enterprises operate. The question is whether your ways of working are designed for a workforce that is part human and part machine. [...] This is not data science. It is not DevOps. It is not prompt engineering either. Some call it Product Engineering, tracing the value from requirements to development and solution realisation.  It is the discipline of designing, deploying, governing, and operating AI agent systems within real enterprise constraints — security reviews, compliance requirements, budget ceilings, organisational politics, and the overnight production incident that nobody planned for.",
      "score": 0.2265358,
      "raw_content": null
    },

The company hasn't said. We've reached out to Oracle for comment, but given the sheer scale of the project, it's safe to assume that much like OpenAI's Stargate facility in Abilene, Texas, it'll be built in phases. ®\n\n#### More about\n\n AI\n Datacenter\n OpenAI\n\nMore like these\n\n×\n\n### More about\n\n AI\n Datacenter\n OpenAI\n Oracle\n\n### Narrower topics\n\n AIOps\n ChatGPT\n Copilot\n Database\n DeepSeek\n Disaster recovery\n Gemini\n Google AI\n GPT-3\n GPT-4\n Large Language Model\n Machine Learning\n Mark Hurd\n MCubed\n MySQL\n Neural Networks\n NLP\n Open Compute Project\n PUE\n Retrieval Augmented Generation\n Software defined data center\n Star Wars\n Tensor Processing Unit\n TOPS\n\n### Broader topics\n\n Larry Ellison\n Self-driving Car",

    {
      "url": "https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/28/amazon_quick_connect_expansion/",
      "title": "Amazon unveils a Copilot for all your apps • The Register",
      "content": "Each solution comprises a set of AI agents, or \"teammates,\" to take care of business, although Amazon insists it is the user who remains in control.\n\nThe market for AI agents running business processes is crowded. Salesforce has staked out its agents everywhere vision, albeit while recovering from a now-patched vulnerability where Agentforce could be tricked into leaking sales data. Similarly, Google offers Gemini Enterprise for workflow automation. Microsoft has gone all in with a new Microsoft 365 tier with features including Agent 365, a dedicated control plane for AI agents.\n\nAmazon will need to work hard to pry customers away from vendors they're already embedded with. ®\n\n#### More about\n\n AI\n Amazon\n AWS\n\nMore like these\n\n×\n\n### More about\n\n AI\n Amazon\n AWS\n\n### Narrower topics [...] The second service is a significant overhaul of Amazon Connect, which expands from a single product into four agentic AI components: Connect Decisions for supply chains, Connect Talent for hiring (the company demonstrated a job interview conducted by an AI chatbot, which is a depressing glimpse of the future of work), Connect Health for healthcare, and Connect Customer AI - a rebrand of the original service - focused on customer experience.\n\nAmazon Connect was launched by AWS in 2017 as a cloud-based contact center tech to let businesses establish and manage customer service ops themselves.\n\n\"It began as the technology powering Amazon's retail customer service, and we've spent years learning how to run it at scale,\" the company said. [...] The Register Home Page  \n\nSearch   \n   Navigation  \n\n#### AI + ML\n\n1 comment bubble on white\n\n# Amazon unveils a Copilot for all your apps\n\n1 comment bubble on white\n\n## Retailer touts 'teammates' and always-on context as it muscles into an already crowded enterprise market\n\nicon  Richard Speed and Matt Rosoff\n\nTue 28 Apr 2026  //  17:06 UTC\n\nAmazon has announced two AI services pitched with typical techbro hyperbole, aimed at changing the way you work.\n\nThe first is \"a new experience\" for Amazon Quick, taking direct aim at Microsoft Copilot and its ilk, only across a wide variety of software. It requires an email address to get started and a new desktop app that, in Amazon's words, \"lives on your computer and connects directly to your work.\"",
      "score": 0.17021844,
      "raw_content": null

      "title": "Mitchell Hashimoto says GitHub 'no longer for serious work",

Apr 2026 | 39\n\n#### Vintage chatbot lives in the past like an elderly relative\n\nTalkie's training data stops at the end of 1930, and its creators hope it'll help us better understand how AI thinks\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 | 15\n\n#### IBM's AI coding 'partner' Bob hits general availability\n\n80,000 internal guinea pigs, Bobcoins, mainframe dreams and a name that really should have raised more flags\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 | 8\n\n#### Amazon unveils a Copilot for all your apps\n\nRetailer touts 'teammates' and always-on context as it muscles into an already crowded enterprise market\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 | 3 [...] ### Broader topics\n\n Bill Gates\n Git\n\n#### TIP US OFF\n\nSend us news\n\n### Other stories you might like\n\n#### 30 ClawHub skills secretly turn AI agents into a crypto swarm\n\nYet another reason not to feast on OpenClaw\n\nSecurity29 Apr 2026 |\n\n#### Future holiday horror: ‘A robot lost my luggage in Tokyo’\n\nHaneda airport will start testing humanoid robots, because everything that gets a plane flying was designed for our species\n\nAI + ML29 Apr 2026 | 1\n\n#### The future of software development: Now with less software development\n\nAt AI Dev 26 x SF, code slingers confront their relationship with AI\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 | 3\n\n#### How JumpCloud unifies IT management to tame shadow AI\n\nIdentity is the secret to ensuring enterprise network visibility in a world of shadow AI\n\nSponsored Feature [...] Microsoft's GitHub shifts to metered AI billing amid cost crisis\n Ongoing supply-chain attack 'explicitly targeting' security, dev tools\n GitHub opts all CLI users into telemetry collection whether they want it or not\n Microsoft's GitHub grounds Copilot account sign-ups amid capacity crunch\n\n“We have a plan but I'm also very much still in discussions with multiple providers (both commercial and FOSS),” Hashimoto wrote. “It'll take us time to remove all of our dependencies on GitHub and we have a plan in place to do it as incrementally as possible.”\n\nHe’s doing the equivalent of leaving a toothbrush at a former partner’s house by leaving a read-only mirror of Ghostty on GitHub, and by keeping his personal projects on the Microsoft-owned service.",

      "url": "https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/28/software_development_ai_dev25xsf/?td=keepreading",
      "title": "The future of software development has less development • The Register",
      "content": "80,000 internal guinea pigs, Bobcoins, mainframe dreams and a name that really should have raised more flags\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 | 8\n\n#### Amazon unveils a Copilot for all your apps\n\nRetailer touts 'teammates' and always-on context as it muscles into an already crowded enterprise market\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 | 3\n\n#### Have I Been Pwned claims Pitney Bowes hit by 8.2M email address leak\n\nNames, phone numbers, physical addresses also included in Shiny Hunters alleged data dump\n\nCyber-crime28 Apr 2026 | 3\n\n#### Despite proposed science cuts, NASA boss says 'We haven't canceled anything yet'\n\nThat 'yet' is sure doing a lot of heavy lifting if the budget for science is slashed\n\nScience28 Apr 2026 | 18\n\n#### Tenstorrent’s Galaxy Blackhole AI servers escape the event horizon [...] Alake said he expects in the future, software development will look a lot more like agent orchestration and agent management. And he expects a lot of roles will blur, with software engineers taking on elements of product management, design, and marketing – speaking to customers to understand their needs.\n\nAndrew Ng during his presentation at AI Dev x 26\n\nAndrew Ng during his presentation at AI Dev 26 x SF - Click to enlarge\n\nAndrew Ng, founder of DeepLearning.AI, said something similar during his keynote. He argued that small teams of generalists overseeing AI agents look like the way forward. And he suggested that instead of just having AI agents write a portion of code, they should write all of it. [...] ### Broader topics\n\n Self-driving Car\n\n#### TIP US OFF\n\nSend us news\n\n### Other stories you might like\n\n#### Oracle plans to power its New Mexico mega datacenter with a 2.45GW fuel cell farm\n\nNo sense in OpenAI stressing over its cloud bills if Oracle can't get the lights on\n\nOn-Prem28 Apr 2026 |\n\n#### Cloudera had US candidates send resumes to a fake email address, DoJ charges\n\nPERM filings require employers to show American workers had a fair shot at the role\n\nLegal28 Apr 2026 | 3\n\n#### OpenAI jumps out of Microsoft's bed, into Amazon's Bedrock\n\nAltman's gaggle of GPTs now available in limited preview in an AWS region near you\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 |\n\n#### How JumpCloud unifies IT management to tame shadow AI",
