    {
      "url": "https://news.microsoft.com/signal/articles/work-smarter-in-90-days-a-real-world-guide-to-using-ai/",
      "title": "Work smarter in 90 days: A real-world guide to using AI",
      "content": "“The most important truth about this moment is that the outcome isn’t written yet,” Roslansky says. “The new world of work is being assembled right now, task by task, policy by policy, business by business. It will reflect the choices of the people who show up to build it.”\n\nSo if you’re looking to get started, here are the basics of their step-by-step plan — along with some specific AI prompts you can use along the way.\n\n## Days 1–30:  Build your base\n\nShifting your workday toward using AI tools and adding in uniquely human tasks can help you become more efficient and more valuable at the same time — so it’s helpful take a quick inventory of what you do and how AI fits in. List your top 12 daily or weekly tasks, then sort them into three buckets: [...] Try this in Microsoft 365 Copilot:\n\n> Help me write a short summary of how I’ve used AI to improve my work based on my emails, Teams conversations and work documents. Then turn it into: 1) a first draft of a LinkedIn post I can personalize and share with my network, and 2) three bullet points I can use in a conversation with my manager about my unique skills and how I can apply them to higher-impact work.\n\nDay 90 check-in:\n\nYou can clearly demonstrate how your work has changed, explain your AI-powered edge and articulate what’s next — whether that’s growing in your role, evolving it or pursuing new projects. [...] ## Days 61–90: Chart a path forward\n\nBy the final stretch, you’ll be using AI to help tackle many routine tasks and focusing your human skills on more strategic, interpersonal work. Now, it’s time to put the pieces together and think through what you want from your career. Assess what’s helping or holding you back. Do you have access to the right tools? Is there leadership support to use AI thoughtfully and responsibly?\n\nClarify your story\n\nTry articulating answers to these three questions, really thinking about them in depth to give thorough, honest answers:",

      "url": "https://www.bain.com/insights/google_cloud_next_2026_the_agentic_enterprise_control_plane_comes_into_view/",
      "title": "Google Cloud Next 2026: The Agentic Enterprise Control Plane ...",
      "content": "The open ecosystem story remains important. Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform offers access to more than 200 models through Model Garden, including Gemini 3.1 Pro, Gemini 3.1 Flash Image, Lyria 3, Gemma 4, and third-party models such as Anthropic’s Claude. The partner layer expanded as well through Agent Gallery and partner-built agents from Accenture, Adobe, Atlassian, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Workday, and others.\n\nBut the more interesting question is not whether Google is open. It is where Google is open. [...] ###### Will IT Accelerate or Stall Your AI Transformation?\n\nHow CIOs can fund, scale, and modernize to keep pace with escalating AI demands.\n\n##### CIO Insights\n\n###### Claude Mythos and the AI Cybersecurity Wake-Up Call\n\nA business risk of the highest order, not a technology problem to be delegated downward.\n\n###### Tags\n\n### How We've Helped Clients\n\n#### A Beauty Company Enables Always-On Brand Acceleration\n\n#### Digital Reimagining Insurance for the AI Era\n\n#### AI, Insights, and Solutions Blockchain-enabled Payment Flows: A Payments Company Reviews its Strategy\n\n### Ready to talk?\n\nWe work with ambitious leaders who want to define the future, not hide from it. Together, we achieve extraordinary outcomes. [...] The enterprise challenge is shifting from experimentation to control. That means choosing where runtime governance will live, investing in context architecture instead of treating retrieval as a one-off engineering task, strengthening non-human identity and authorization models, and defining clear boundaries around autonomy before agent deployment outpaces oversight.\n\nThat is what makes this moment more important than a standard AI product launch.\n\nThe pressing question for enterprise leaders is no longer whether agents are coming. It is whether their organization is building the control plane required to govern them.\n\n###### Authors\n\nHeadshot of Chris Green\n\n##### Chris Green\n\n##### Partner, Atlanta\n\nHeadshot of Dale Pedzinski\n\n##### Dale Pedzinski",

    {
      "url": "https://news.microsoft.com/signal/articles/work-smarter-in-90-days-a-real-world-guide-to-using-ai/",
      "title": "Work smarter in 90 days: A real-world guide to using AI",
      "content": "“The most important truth about this moment is that the outcome isn’t written yet,” Roslansky says. “The new world of work is being assembled right now, task by task, policy by policy, business by business. It will reflect the choices of the people who show up to build it.”\n\nSo if you’re looking to get started, here are the basics of their step-by-step plan — along with some specific AI prompts you can use along the way.\n\n## Days 1–30:  Build your base\n\nShifting your workday toward using AI tools and adding in uniquely human tasks can help you become more efficient and more valuable at the same time — so it’s helpful take a quick inventory of what you do and how AI fits in. List your top 12 daily or weekly tasks, then sort them into three buckets: [...] Try this in Microsoft 365 Copilot:\n\n> Help me write a short summary of how I’ve used AI to improve my work based on my emails, Teams conversations and work documents. Then turn it into: 1) a first draft of a LinkedIn post I can personalize and share with my network, and 2) three bullet points I can use in a conversation with my manager about my unique skills and how I can apply them to higher-impact work.\n\nDay 90 check-in:\n\nYou can clearly demonstrate how your work has changed, explain your AI-powered edge and articulate what’s next — whether that’s growing in your role, evolving it or pursuing new projects. [...] ## Days 61–90: Chart a path forward\n\nBy the final stretch, you’ll be using AI to help tackle many routine tasks and focusing your human skills on more strategic, interpersonal work. Now, it’s time to put the pieces together and think through what you want from your career. Assess what’s helping or holding you back. Do you have access to the right tools? Is there leadership support to use AI thoughtfully and responsibly?\n\nClarify your story\n\nTry articulating answers to these three questions, really thinking about them in depth to give thorough, honest answers:",

      "content": "Be innovative in AI.HR operating model redesign – bringing different and new approaches and ideas\n Be passionate about using technology to create more human, inclusive experiences\n Be a people leader, with experience of leading others, supporting junior team members through client delivery to support their development\n Be platform-agnostic however have functional knowledge of leading platforms such as SAP SuccessFactors, Workday, ServiceNow, Moveworks, Eightfold,\n Be a strong communicator with the ability to influence senior stakeholders\n Be curious, innovative and driven by a desire to help our clients achieve their goals\n Be professional, quickly establishing personal credibility and demonstrating expertise [...] Experience in leading and shaping a AI.HR Point of View – being able to articulate trends, forces and resultant strategies for HR functions credibly\n Ability to bring provocation and thinking on the future role of HR in the AI Workforce – and translate this into new HR operating models of the future – holding debate and challenge conversations with clients on the topic\n Experience in business development and sales including demonstrating meeting / exceeding set financial performance targets. Proven track record in pursuing and leading delivery of £1m+ HR transformations – shaping specific solutions, responses, and commercials. Ability to build a pipeline of work and hold an active book of business [...] Lead development of a new AI.HR Point of View which is relevant, scalable and drive opportunities with our clients\n Bring leading insights from the external market and thought-provoking ideas to our clients as the world of work changes\n Reimagine HR operating and service delivery models, embedding GBS, automation, and AI.\n Shape AI and people technology strategies, simplifying landscapes and enabling human-centred experiences.\n Drive HR GBS transformation – from strategy and design to implementation – optimising efficiency, scalability, and impact.\n Guide clients to become AI-ready across data, process, governance, and people skills.\n Build trusted relationships with senior stakeholders, influencing C-suite agendas.\n\nBeing successful as a Director",

      "title": "PwC faculty newsletter - April 2026",
      "content": "### Workforce Hopes and Fears Survey: US insights\n\nAI isn’t just changing how work gets done—it’s reshaping how people feel about work. PwC’s Workforce Hopes & Fears Survey shows employees are more excited than anxious about AI—yet daily GenAI use remains low. Learn how HR leaders play a key role in AI adoption based on our Workforce Hopes & Fears Survey.\n\n### AI-ready skills go beyond prompting\n\nA recent Computerworld article featuring PwC’s Margaret Burke explores why AI readiness is less about mastering tools and more about building human skills like critical thinking, judgment, and storytelling alongside AI fluency. Learn more here.\n\n### New PwC survey explores what separates companies that keep pace from those that pull ahead [...] PwC supported the San Francisco 49ers in launching a new app that uses generative AI and personalization to help fans access team news, navigate gameday, and connect more deeply with the franchise from anywhere in the world. Learn more here.\n\n### How organizations break AI gridlock by going beyond sandbox experiments\n\nPwC’s George Korizis emphasizes that scaling AI requires disciplined execution, not unchecked expansion. Organizations must balance innovation with governance, trust, and customer value, ensuring responsible deployment. By embedding structure, strategy, and human-centric design, PwC helps enterprises move beyond pilots to scalable, outcome-driven AI transformation that delivers measurable business impact. Read more here. [...] Earlier this month, we hosted an AI webinar for faculty that showcased several AI classroom activities and discussed the integration of AI within Audit and Tax. The recording of this session is now available on our Artificial Intelligence Resources page that can be accessed here.       "content": "- Bachelor’s Degree (or equivalent)\n\n- Basic understanding of time management, attendance, scheduling, or HR systems\n\n- Experience working with Excel, Word, and PowerPoint\n\n- Willingness to travel as needed and work in a hybrid environment\n\nIdeally, you’ll also have\n\n- Exposure to consulting, HR technology, or workforce management through internships, projects, or early-career roles\n\n- Familiarity with HR or WFM platforms such as UKG, ADP, Workday, SAP, Oracle, or Ceridian\n\n- Strong attention to detail and eagerness to build technical and consulting expertise\n\n- Interest in pursuing long-term growth within People Consulting and workforce transformation [...] Are you ready to shape your future with confidence? Apply today.  \nEY accepts applications for this position on an on-going basis.\n\nFor those living in California, please click here for additional information.\n\nEY focuses on high-ethical standards and integrity among its employees and expects all candidates to demonstrate these qualities.\n\nEY  |  Building a better working world\n\nEY is building a better working world by creating new value for clients, people, society and the planet, while building trust in capital markets.\n\nEnabled by data, AI and advanced technology, EY teams help clients shape the future with confidence and develop answers for the most pressing issues of today and tomorrow.     {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/press-releases/pwc-appian-expand-alliance.html",
      "title": "PwC and Appian expand alliance for enterprise AI",
      "content": "These solutions demonstrate what it looks like when AI is applied directly to the systems and processes that run the enterprise, transforming fragmented logic into governed, production-ready applications, not just maintaining existing systems, but accelerating innovation.\n\n### Labeling and product information management [...] Across industries, many AI initiatives struggle to scale, delivering value in isolated pockets rather than translating into how the business runs day to day. While enterprises have invested heavily in AI, much of that investment has stopped short of the processes where cost and risk actually live—the manual regulatory workflows, decades-old code, and fragmented spreadsheets that sit outside IT governance and beyond the reach of modern AI tools. The expanded PwC and Appian alliance is designed to close that gap, combining PwC’s AI tech-enabled solutions with Appian Composer, an AI-augmented development tool on the Appian Platform, to convert locked-away business logic into governed, production-ready applications. [...] > “Enterprises don’t have an AI access problem—they have an AI application problem. Most of the cost and risk in a large organization sits in processes that AI can’t easily reach: regulatory workflows coordinated by email, code written decades ago, spreadsheets running critical calculations. By pairing PwC’s AI capabilities with Appian Composer, we’re helping give clients a practical way to turn those processes into governed applications—and to start realizing the return on their AI investments now, not years from now.”",
      "score": 0.06037975,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "title": "EY Careers | EY - US",
      "content": "### Services\n\nEY helps clients create long-term value for all stakeholders. Enabled by data and technology, our services and solutions provide trust through assurance and help clients transform, grow and operate.\n\n#### Spotlight\n\nArtificial Intelligence (AI)\n\nStrategy, Transaction & Transformation\n\nAudit\n\nTax Function Operations\n\nClimate Change & Sustainability\n\nEY Partner Ecosystem\n\nTechnology Transformation\n\nSupply Chain & Operations\n\n### Industries\n\nDiscover how EY insights and services are helping to reframe the future of your industry.\n\n#### Case studies\n\nPower Utilities\n\nHow digital twin technology powers the future at Xcel Energy\n\n28 Aug 2025Subbu Natarajan\n\nStrategy and Transactions\n\nHow Bristol Myers Squibb overhauled working capital to fund its future\n\n20 Aug 2025Gerado Gonzalez [...] See more\n\n#### Top news\n\n### Careers\n\nWe bring together extraordinary people, like you, to build a better working world.\n\n#### Spotlight\n\nExperienced professionals\n\nEarly career opportunities\n\nMBA and advanced-degree students\n\nContract workers\n\nEY-Parthenon careers\n\nNo results have been found\n\nTopics\n\nGeneral\n\nPeople\n\nRecent Searches\n\nTrending\n\nWhy some tax functions use data effectively while others struggle\n\nThere’s a divide between tax functions with AI-ready data and those without. Learn more.\n\nAudit committee resources\n\nFind the latest information, updates and resources for audit committees as well as sample charters, agendas and assessments.\n\nHow do you build a security roadmap for a shifting AI terrain? [...] Three no-regret moves to free the clinical workforce.\n\n Redesign clinical work, flow, and capacity as a single system, and make room for new clinical profiles.\n Know the workforce as well as the patients and then act on it.\n Technology should serve the redesigned model, not prop up the old one.\n\nComing May 12, more on freeing the clinical workforce.\n\nThree no-regret moves to free the clinical workforce.\n\nComing May 12, more on freeing the clinical workforce.\n\n### Owning the visit is no longer enough\n\nFive no-regret moves to develop a deep understanding of consumers.\n\n Own the journey, not the door.\n Make “in‑between visit engagement” a core operating capability.\n Segment for action, not messaging.\n Make hyper‑personalization practical without waiting for perfection.\n Preserve trust.",

    {
      "url": "https://www.mercer.com/en-us/insights/us-health-news/podcasts/",
      "title": "The beneficial workplace - Mercer",
      "content": "In this episode of the Beneficial Workplace, host Emily Ferreira sits down with Tracy Watts (Mercer’s National Leader of U.S. Health Policy), to discuss a key issue facing employers: healthcare affordability. As you’ll hear, the sharp upturn in health benefit cost growth is putting pressure on employers as well employees, but there are creative strategies – including a focus on financial wellness – that can help.\n\nSources:\n\nResume Now survey 92% of Americans Cut Back Spending in 2025  \nAflac Wellness Matters Survey 2025 Aflac Wellness Matters Survey  \nCommonwealth research Transforming Workplace Benefits into an Engine for Financial Health\n\n### New data reveals the impact of rising healthcare costs on employers\n\n### AI, Rx and rising costs: What we learned at HLTH this year [...] ### What’s behind the biggest health benefit cost increase in 15 years?\n\n### Health on Demand Survey results: Designing benefit programs for all generations\n\n### The future of AI in benefits is already here\n\n### How employers can support caregivers in the workplace\n\n### Compare, contrast and project: Benchmarking for benefits strategy\n\n### A look back at 2024 suggests new directions for benefit strategies\n\n### Behind the numbers: Understanding the rising health benefit cost trend\n\n### Trends in time off: New survey results\n\n### Voluntary benefits: The next frontier in flexibility\n\n### Creating a workplace that supports mental health\n\n### Longevity and the impact of an aging workforce\n\n### Women's health: Now essential to an inclusive benefits program [...] ## We're evolving. Mercer is now part of the new, expanded Marsh brand\n\nMercer\n\nMercer helps to optimize benefits, costs and investments strategies to help healthcare organizations thrive\n\n# The Beneficial Workplace\n\n## The Beneficial Workplace features data-driven conversations about employee health and well-being.\n\n## Latest episodes\n\n### Workforce response to economic uncertainty and technological change\n\nSean Connelly joins Emily Ferreira to discuss the results of a new survey of US workers, Inside Employees’ Minds. New technology, the economy, and even extreme weather are heightening stress in the workplace, but there are ways employers can help.\n\nRelated sources: Inside Employees’ Minds 2025-2026\n\n### Affordability concerns are top of mind for employees and employers",
      "score": 0.022527957,
      "raw_content": null
    }

      "title": "Workday Canada | The Enterprise AI Platform for HR & Finance",
      "content": "Drive faster, smarter frontline operations and enable a connected, agile workforce with AI agents and mobile tools.\n\nGartner white logo\n\nA Leader in the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Cloud HCM Suites for 1,000+ Employee Enterprises. Learn how it actively supports your people and enables them to do their best work.\n\nAI that truly understands work.\n\nWorkday AI is the engine that powers our products and AI agents. [...] ### Investor Relations\n\n### Newsroom\n\n## Welcome,\n\nYou are automatically signed in to workday.com with your Workday Digital ID account.\n\nNeed help signing in?\n\nTo access your Workday account, please reach out to your HR or IT department for a link to your company's unique sign in page.\n\n# Superintelligence for work. [...] ## Products\n\n### AI\n\nDiscover superintelligence for the new world of work.\n\n#### Artificial Intelligence\n\n#### Sana from Workday\n\n#### Agent System of Record\n\n#### Agents\n\n#### Responsible AI\n\n#### AI Masterclass\n\n### HR\n\nEmpower your people and adapt your workforce to the future of work.\n\n#### HR Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Human Capital Management\n\n#### Human Resource Management\n\n#### Workday Paradox\n\n#### Talent Management\n\n#### Workforce Management\n\n#### Employee Experience\n\n#### HR Service Delivery\n\n#### Employee Voice\n\n#### Workforce Planning\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting\n\n#### Vendor Workforce Management\n\n#### Local & Global Payroll\n\n### Finance\n\nDrive innovation, manage costs, and make smarter finance decisions, faster.\n\n#### Finance Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS",

    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-au/elevate-new-zealand/agenda.html",
      "title": "Agenda - Elevate New Zealand - Workday",
      "content": "This session will unveil a vision for a New Work Day for HR—a fundamental shift from systems that simply track work to those that actively enable it through Agentic HR.  \n  \nGrounded in three core imperatives, this strategy demonstrates how Workday’s Enterprise AI platform reinvents core HR with trusted AI, delivers a new level of business value, and builds for the future through a unified, AI-driven architecture.  \n  \nBy leveraging Workday’s unparalleled context—encompassing 75 million users and 20 years of experience—HR leaders can now deploy specialised agents across payroll, recruiting, and performance to eliminate \"talent trapped in trivia\" and allow their teams to lead with empathy and creativity.\n\nJo-Anne Ruhl\n\nKaty Colletto\n\nVice President oCHRO Product Marketing, Workday [...] Chief Digital Officer, University of Auckland\n\nIn unpredictable times, unified planning is essential for building resilience. Join us for this forward-looking breakout session exploring the strategy and vision for Workday Adaptive Planning, and how agentic AI is enabling smarter, faster decision-making across finance, HR, and operations.  \n  \nThe session will feature a live demonstration, providing a firsthand look at the latest innovations and AI-powered capabilities in Workday Adaptive Planning, and an engaging fireside chat featuring Public Trust of New Zealand, who will share their experience and key learnings from their Adaptive Planning journey.\n\nShan Moorthy\n\nVince Randall\n\nVice President Sales, Asia Pacific and Japan, Workday\n\nShan Moorthy\n\nErik Ersfjord [...] Guillem Bonavia\n\nChief Financial Officer, Shuttlerock\n\nAs AI moves from experiment to core infrastructure, CIOs are the primary architects of the machine-human workforce. This keynote sets the stage for the IT track, exploring how Workday’s open, AI-native platform eliminates technical debt by unifying the enterprise’s two most critical data sets: People and Money. We move beyond the hype to show how an open architecture allows IT leaders to stop simply maintaining systems and start orchestrating intelligence—creating a flexible digital backbone that turns raw data into measurable business agility.\n\nShan Moorthy\n\nShan Moorthy\n\nChief Technology Officer, Asia Pacific, Workday",
      "score": 0.8455478,
      "raw_content": null
    },

    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-us/midsize-business/overview.html",
      "title": "Midsize Business Software & Solutions | Workday GO",
      "content": "AI agents such as Deployment Agent, Self-Service Agent, and Payroll Agent available on day one that help you do more with less.\n\nFlexibility to try and add new AI agents to test environments at no costs.\n\nNot at all. Workday actually reduces complexity for midsize organizations. You get a single platform for HR, global payroll, finance, and AI agents with streamlined processes, automation, and integration capabilities built in—so you’re managing your business from one system. [...] ### The simplicity and AI you need at the price you want.\n\nOnly Workday simplifies your back office with one unified solution for HR, payroll, and finance that’s simple to buy, fast to activate, and designed for midsize budgets. Governed AI agents, powered by Sana, handle more of the everyday work, so your team spends less time on routine tasks and more time on your business from day one.\n\n### The simplicity and AI you need at the price you want.\n\nOnly Workday simplifies your back office with one unified solution for HR, payroll, and finance that’s simple to buy, fast to activate, and designed for midsize budgets. Governed AI agents, powered by Sana, handle more of the everyday work, so your team spends less time on routine tasks and more time on your business from day one. We have answers.\n\n### Questions? We have answers.\n\nThe built-in AI in Workday GO helps small and midsize businesses adopt enterprise AI affordably and without the complexity of building custom systems. Key benefits of our platform include:\n\nAI-powered workflows that automate many of the routine, time-consuming tasks that are common in human resources and finance.\n\nBuilt-in AI that helps organizations make more informed decisions by providing predictive insights",

    {
      "url": "https://en-gb.newsroom.workday.com/2026-04-28-SNG-Deploys-Workday-to-Transform-HR-and-Finance-Operations-and-Accelerate-Community-Impact",
      "title": "SNG Deploys Workday to Transform HR and Finance Operations ...",
      "content": "About Workday  \n Workday is the enterprise AI platform for managing people, money, and agents.Workday unifies HR and finance on one intelligent platform with AI at the core to empower people at every level with the clarity, confidence, and insights they need to adapt quickly, make better decisions, and deliver outcomes that matter. Workday is used by more than 11,500 organizations around the world and across industries – from medium-sized businesses to more than 65% of the Fortune 500. For more information about Workday, visit workday.com.\n\n© 2026 Workday, Inc. All rights reserved. Workday and the Workday logo are registered trademarks of Workday, Inc. All other brand and product names are trademarks or registered trademarks of their respective holders. [...] LONDON, UK, April 29, 2026 – Workday Inc. (NASDAQ: WDAY), the enterprise AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, today announced that SNG (Sovereign Network Group)  has successfully deployed Workday Financial Management and Workday Human Capital Management (HCM. SNG will now operate on the unified and intelligent Workday platform, designed to scale alongside the organisation and its social mission. [...] SNG’s unified platform approach provides the foundation necessary for agile, data-driven decision-making. With Workday HCM, SNG is stripping away manual HR administration to enable teams to work faster and smarter. The platform provides intuitive tools that simplify routine tasks, while simultaneously giving leaders real-time insights into talent and skills to actively empower a high-performing workforce. Connecting these workforce insights directly to financial performance, Workday Financial Management delivers critical insights to enable smarter, faster accounting processes.",

      "title": "Workday Data Cloud: Enterprise Data Management & Analytics",
      "content": "Advanced analytics and AI.\n\nBy embracing open industry standards like Apache Iceberg and JDBC, we maximize flexibility and avoid integration vendor lock-in, protecting your Workday investment as your data strategy evolves.\n\nAdvanced analytics and AI.\n\nBy embracing open industry standards like Apache Iceberg and JDBC, we maximize flexibility and avoid integration vendor lock-in, protecting your Workday investment as your data strategy evolves.\n\n## AI Agents\n\n### Go all in on agentic AI—simply and securely.\n\nWorkday Data Cloud uses trusted people and finance data to power Workday Agents, partner agents, and your custom agents built on Workday Build. This unified foundation ensures all agents act directly within your business processes with governance and audit controls.\n\n''\nSalesforce logo\n\n“With Workday as part of our Zero-Copy Partner Network, we are giving customers secure access to comprehensive HR, finance, and CRM data on our connected platform.”\n\n“With Workday as part of our Zero-Copy Partner Network, we are giving customers secure access to comprehensive HR, finance, and CRM data on our connected platform.”\n\n## FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS\n\n### FAQs.\n\n### FAQs.",

      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-hk/products/adaptive-planning/overview.html",
      "title": "Adaptive Planning: Enterprise Planning Software (EPM) - Workday",
      "content": "# EPM Software\n\nUnlock limitless decision intelligence.\n\nFuel your next era of growth by keeping your brightest minds focused on strategy instead of manual workarounds—and discover why more cloud customers trust Workday Adaptive Planning than our largest competitors, combined.\n\nPlay Video (3:02)   Learn More\n\n \n\n### Make smarter decisions with AI-powered  planning.\n\nAnticipate what’s next.\n\nDrive smarter outcomes with AI-powered intelligence, backed by human-in-the-loop governance and grounded in enterprise-wide data.\n\nScale your impact.\n\nOperationalize any data source with automated connections—no storage limits or performance lag—just AI-powered insights that grow with your business.\n\nNavigate with ease. [...] TrustRadius Workday Adaptive Planning Top Rated\n\nG2 Top Finance Products Workday Adaptive Planning\n\n## AI FOR WORKDAY ADAPTIVE PLANNING\n\n### Planning Agent\n\nFind out why over 100 customers trust the Planning Agent to drive smarter decisions with conversational, dynamic prompts.\n\nLearn More\n\n \n\n## PARTNER ECOSYSTEM\n\n### Workday Marketplace\n\nDiscover custom apps from our trusted partner ecosystem to solve frontline operational challenges.\n\nLearn More\n\n \n\nHubspot logo\n\n“With Workday Adaptive Planning, my team can complete a forecast cycle in 15-20% less time than before.”\n\n—Vice President of Financial Planning and Analysis\n\nRead Story\n\nOne platform for every plan and decision.\n\nConnect financial, workforce, and operational plans for a full view across your business.\n\n### Financial planning. [...] ### Financial planning.\n\nFlexible, AI-driven budgeting, scenario planning, and reporting let you plan, budget, and forecast with confidence.\n\nLearn More\n\n### Workforce planning.\n\nPlan for the skills you need today and tomorrow with flexible hiring plans that align finance, sales, HR, marketing, and IT.\n\nLearn More\n\n### Operational planning.\n\nCompanywide, collaborative planning and real-time data enable you to deploy the right resources to meet business goals.\n\nLearn More\n\n### Close and consolidation.\n\nStreamline close and consolidation tasks for more accuracy, transparency, and efficiency in your consolidation process.\n\nLearn More\n\n### Planning FAQs.\n\n### How quickly can Workday Adaptive Planning be deployed?",

    {
      "url": "https://news.microsoft.com/signal/articles/work-smarter-in-90-days-a-real-world-guide-to-using-ai/",
      "title": "Work smarter in 90 days: A real-world guide to using AI",
      "content": "“The most important truth about this moment is that the outcome isn’t written yet,” Roslansky says. “The new world of work is being assembled right now, task by task, policy by policy, business by business. It will reflect the choices of the people who show up to build it.”\n\nSo if you’re looking to get started, here are the basics of their step-by-step plan — along with some specific AI prompts you can use along the way.\n\n## Days 1–30:  Build your base\n\nShifting your workday toward using AI tools and adding in uniquely human tasks can help you become more efficient and more valuable at the same time — so it’s helpful take a quick inventory of what you do and how AI fits in. List your top 12 daily or weekly tasks, then sort them into three buckets: [...] Try this in Microsoft 365 Copilot:\n\n> Help me write a short summary of how I’ve used AI to improve my work based on my emails, Teams conversations and work documents. Then turn it into: 1) a first draft of a LinkedIn post I can personalize and share with my network, and 2) three bullet points I can use in a conversation with my manager about my unique skills and how I can apply them to higher-impact work.\n\nDay 90 check-in:\n\nYou can clearly demonstrate how your work has changed, explain your AI-powered edge and articulate what’s next — whether that’s growing in your role, evolving it or pursuing new projects. [...] ## Days 61–90: Chart a path forward\n\nBy the final stretch, you’ll be using AI to help tackle many routine tasks and focusing your human skills on more strategic, interpersonal work. Now, it’s time to put the pieces together and think through what you want from your career. Assess what’s helping or holding you back. Do you have access to the right tools? Is there leadership support to use AI thoughtfully and responsibly?\n\nClarify your story\n\nTry articulating answers to these three questions, really thinking about them in depth to give thorough, honest answers:",
      "score": 0.67140645,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "content": "Categories\n Analytics & Insights\n Application Development\n Artificial Intelligence\n Contract Management\n Customers & Community\n Finance\n Human Resources\n Medium Enterprise\n Planning\n Procurement\n Security & Trust\n Technology Innovation\n Workday News & Culture\n Industries\n Financial Services\n Government\n Healthcare\n Higher Education\n Hospitality\n Media and Entertainment\n Nonprofit\n Professional and Business Services\n Retail\n Technology\n Podcasts\n Videos\n Authors\n\nLanguage Selection [...] The Future of Restaurant Management: A View From 2025\n\nLearn how restaurants can operate with agility by bridging the front and back office into a cohesive, data-driven powerhouse for strategic insights.\n\nRead Report\n\nPosted in:   Customers and Community,   Human Resources\n\n Customers Retail\n\nMore Reading\n\n Man staring out of a window \n\n  Customers and Community Workday News and Culture\n\n  ### Honoring Service With Action: How Workday Supports Our Veteran Community\n\n  Discover how Workday is bridging the civilian opportunity gap for transitioning service members. From philanthropic investments and dedicated hiring programs to innovative skills translation technology, we’re empowering veterans to thrive in the modern workforce.\n\n   \n\n  Workday Staff Writers\n workday partner awards [...] Workday Staff Writers\n workday partner awards \n\n  Customers and Community Workday News and Culture\n\n  ### Announcing Our 2026 Workday Partner Award Winners\n\n  The Workday Partner Awards recognize the exceptional contributions of our partners, honoring their innovation and unwavering dedication to delivering outstanding value to customers. This recognition is a testament to the power of our ecosystem and the incredible results we deliver together to fuel their best work. Read on to meet this year’s winners.\n\n   \n\n  Workday Staff Writers\n A woman in a HR meeting listening attentively \n\n  Human Resources\n\n  ### What Are the Biggest HR Challenges of 2026?",

    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/careers/university-relations/faculty-newsletter/april-2026.html",
      "title": "PwC faculty newsletter - April 2026",
      "content": "### Workforce Hopes and Fears Survey: US insights\n\nAI isn’t just changing how work gets done—it’s reshaping how people feel about work. PwC’s Workforce Hopes & Fears Survey shows employees are more excited than anxious about AI—yet daily GenAI use remains low. Learn how HR leaders play a key role in AI adoption based on our Workforce Hopes & Fears Survey.\n\n### AI-ready skills go beyond prompting\n\nA recent Computerworld article featuring PwC’s Margaret Burke explores why AI readiness is less about mastering tools and more about building human skills like critical thinking, judgment, and storytelling alongside AI fluency. Learn more here.\n\n### New PwC survey explores what separates companies that keep pace from those that pull ahead [...] PwC supported the San Francisco 49ers in launching a new app that uses generative AI and personalization to help fans access team news, navigate gameday, and connect more deeply with the franchise from anywhere in the world. Learn more here.\n\n### How organizations break AI gridlock by going beyond sandbox experiments\n\nPwC’s George Korizis emphasizes that scaling AI requires disciplined execution, not unchecked expansion. Organizations must balance innovation with governance, trust, and customer value, ensuring responsible deployment. By embedding structure, strategy, and human-centric design, PwC helps enterprises move beyond pilots to scalable, outcome-driven AI transformation that delivers measurable business impact. Read more here. [...] Earlier this month, we hosted an AI webinar for faculty that showcased several AI classroom activities and discussed the integration of AI within Audit and Tax. The recording of this session is now available on our Artificial Intelligence Resources page that can be accessed here. If you do not have access to our site, you can request it here.\n\n### PwC recognized among Fortune’s best workplaces shaping future-ready culture\n\nPwC ranks #35 on Fortune’s 2026 “100 Best Companies to Work For,” highlighting its commitment to employee experience, trust, and innovation. Learn more here.\n\n### PwC Foundation invests $2 million to advance human skills development",

    {
      "url": "https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/28/ukgov_seeks_cdio_to_lead/",
      "title": "DCMS to new CDIO: Microsoft migration, overhaul ERP, survive",
      "content": "Sponsored Feature\n\n#### IBM's AI coding 'partner' Bob hits general availability\n\n80,000 internal guinea pigs, Bobcoins, mainframe dreams and a name that really should have raised more flags\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 | 6\n\n#### Amazon unveils a Copilot for all your apps\n\nRetailer touts 'teammates' and always-on context as it muscles into an already crowded enterprise market\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 | 1\n\n#### Have I Been Pwned claims Pitney Bowes hit by 8.2M email address leak\n\nNames, phone numbers, physical addresses also included in Shiny Hunters alleged data dump\n\nCyber-crime28 Apr 2026 | 3\n\n#### Despite proposed science cuts, NASA boss says 'We haven't canceled anything yet'\n\nThat 'yet' is sure doing a lot of heavy lifting if the budget for science is slashed\n\nScience28 Apr 2026 | 18 [...] Science28 Apr 2026 | 18\n\n#### Tenstorrent’s Galaxy Blackhole AI servers escape the event horizon\n\nRISC-V-based systems pack 32 Blackhole accelerators in a 6U, $110K chassis\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 | 3\n\n#### Brussels orders Google to share Android's AI sandbox with the other kids\n\nDMA enforcers want rival assistants to get same deep device access as Gemini\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 | 5\n\n#### Two men charged over series of arson attacks on 5G masts\n\nPair accused of creating literal flame war as bonkers conspiracy theories grow\n\nNetworks28 Apr 2026 | 51\n\n#### Microsoft Outlook for iOS still down and out for many after 'service change'\n\nSign-in failures, unexpected sign-outs... just another day for users\n\nSaaS28 Apr 2026 | 15 [...] Wrenching department staff away from Google Docs is only the start. The new CDIO must prepare DCMS to join the NEO platform - a new HR and finance system that is part of a Government shared services programme - in the second half of 2026, “working with four other departments and taking services from a shared service provider.\"\n\nDCMS is part of the shared service cluster “Matrix”, which has opted for Workday HR and finance software and systems integrator Cognizant in a £144.3 million deal. The job ad says four other departments will join, though the National Audit Office counted six earlier this year, including project lead, the Department for Science, Innovation & Technology, where the head of digital government now sits.",
      "score": 0.063441865,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/press-releases/pwc-appian-expand-alliance.html",
      "title": "PwC and Appian expand alliance for enterprise AI",
      "content": "These solutions demonstrate what it looks like when AI is applied directly to the systems and processes that run the enterprise, transforming fragmented logic into governed, production-ready applications, not just maintaining existing systems, but accelerating innovation.\n\n### Labeling and product information management [...] Across industries, many AI initiatives struggle to scale, delivering value in isolated pockets rather than translating into how the business runs day to day. While enterprises have invested heavily in AI, much of that investment has stopped short of the processes where cost and risk actually live—the manual regulatory workflows, decades-old code, and fragmented spreadsheets that sit outside IT governance and beyond the reach of modern AI tools. The expanded PwC and Appian alliance is designed to close that gap, combining PwC’s AI tech-enabled solutions with Appian Composer, an AI-augmented development tool on the Appian Platform, to convert locked-away business logic into governed, production-ready applications. [...] > “Enterprises don’t have an AI access problem—they have an AI application problem. Most of the cost and risk in a large organization sits in processes that AI can’t easily reach: regulatory workflows coordinated by email, code written decades ago, spreadsheets running critical calculations. By pairing PwC’s AI capabilities with Appian Composer, we’re helping give clients a practical way to turn those processes into governed applications—and to start realizing the return on their AI investments now, not years from now.”",
      "score": 0.06037975,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.databricks.com/company/careers/finance/senior-manager-international-equity-administration-8493150002",

      "content": "About Databricks\n\nDatabricks is the data and AI company. More than 10,000 organizations worldwide — including Comcast, Condé Nast, Grammarly, and over 50% of the Fortune 500 — rely on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform to unify and democratize data, analytics and AI. Databricks is headquartered in San Francisco, with offices around the globe and was founded by the original creators of Lakehouse, Apache Spark™, Delta Lake and MLflow. To learn more, follow Databricks on Twitter, LinkedIn and Facebook.  \n  \nBenefitsAt Databricks, we strive to provide comprehensive benefits and perks that meet the needs of all of our employees. For specific details on the benefits offered in your region click here.\n\nOur Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion [...] Data+ai summit promo\n\nJUNE 15–18|SAN FRANCISCO\n\nLast chance to save 50% — ends April 30.\n\nData+ai summit promo\n\nJUNE 15–18|SAN FRANCISCO\n\nLast chance to save 50% — ends April 30.\n\n# Senior Manager, International Equity Administration\n\nApply now\n\nGAQ227R280\n\nWe are seeking an experienced Senior Manager, International Equity Administration to lead and scale our global equity operations across ~45 countries, including ~20 Employer of Record (EOR) jurisdictions. This role is critical to ensuring compliance with complex international regulations, managing global equity transactions, and delivering a best-in-class employee equity experience. [...] Compliance\n\nIf access to export-controlled technology or source code is required for performance of job duties, it is within Employer's discretion whether to apply for a U.S. government license for such positions, and Employer may decline to proceed with an applicant on this basis alone.\n\ndatabricks logo\ndatabricks logo\ndatabricks logo\ndatabricks logo\n\nDatabricks Inc.  \n160 Spear Street, 15th Floor  \nSan Francisco, CA 94105  \n1-866-330-0121\n\nSee Careers  \nat Databricks\n\n© Databricks 2026. All rights reserved. Apache, Apache Spark, Spark, the Spark Logo, Apache Iceberg, Iceberg, and the Apache Iceberg logo are trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.",

      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-au/elevate-new-zealand/agenda.html",
      "title": "Agenda - Elevate New Zealand - Workday",
      "content": "This session will unveil a vision for a New Work Day for HR—a fundamental shift from systems that simply track work to those that actively enable it through Agentic HR.  \n  \nGrounded in three core imperatives, this strategy demonstrates how Workday’s Enterprise AI platform reinvents core HR with trusted AI, delivers a new level of business value, and builds for the future through a unified, AI-driven architecture.  \n  \nBy leveraging Workday’s unparalleled context—encompassing 75 million users and 20 years of experience—HR leaders can now deploy specialised agents across payroll, recruiting, and performance to eliminate \"talent trapped in trivia\" and allow their teams to lead with empathy and creativity.\n\nJo-Anne Ruhl\n\nKaty Colletto\n\nVice President oCHRO Product Marketing, Workday [...] Chief Digital Officer, University of Auckland\n\nIn unpredictable times, unified planning is essential for building resilience. Join us for this forward-looking breakout session exploring the strategy and vision for Workday Adaptive Planning, and how agentic AI is enabling smarter, faster decision-making across finance, HR, and operations.  \n  \nThe session will feature a live demonstration, providing a firsthand look at the latest innovations and AI-powered capabilities in Workday Adaptive Planning, and an engaging fireside chat featuring Public Trust of New Zealand, who will share their experience and key learnings from their Adaptive Planning journey.\n\nShan Moorthy\n\nVince Randall\n\nVice President Sales, Asia Pacific and Japan, Workday\n\nShan Moorthy\n\nErik Ersfjord [...] Guillem Bonavia\n\nChief Financial Officer, Shuttlerock\n\nAs AI moves from experiment to core infrastructure, CIOs are the primary architects of the machine-human workforce. This keynote sets the stage for the IT track, exploring how Workday’s open, AI-native platform eliminates technical debt by unifying the enterprise’s two most critical data sets: People and Money. We move beyond the hype to show how an open architecture allows IT leaders to stop simply maintaining systems and start orchestrating intelligence—creating a flexible digital backbone that turns raw data into measurable business agility.\n\nShan Moorthy\n\nShan Moorthy\n\nChief Technology Officer, Asia Pacific, Workday",
      "score": 0.8455478,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "title": "SNG Deploys Workday to Transform HR and Finance Operations ...",
      "content": "About Workday  \n Workday is the enterprise AI platform for managing people, money, and agents.Workday unifies HR and finance on one intelligent platform with AI at the core to empower people at every level with the clarity, confidence, and insights they need to adapt quickly, make better decisions, and deliver outcomes that matter. Workday is used by more than 11,500 organizations around the world and across industries – from medium-sized businesses to more than 65% of the Fortune 500. For more information about Workday, visit workday.com.\n\n© 2026 Workday, Inc. All rights reserved. Workday and the Workday logo are registered trademarks of Workday, Inc. All other brand and product names are trademarks or registered trademarks of their respective holders. [...] LONDON, UK, April 29, 2026 – Workday Inc. (NASDAQ: WDAY), the enterprise AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, today announced that SNG (Sovereign Network Group)  has successfully deployed Workday Financial Management and Workday Human Capital Management (HCM. SNG will now operate on the unified and intelligent Workday platform, designed to scale alongside the organisation and its social mission. [...] SNG’s unified platform approach provides the foundation necessary for agile, data-driven decision-making. With Workday HCM, SNG is stripping away manual HR administration to enable teams to work faster and smarter. The platform provides intuitive tools that simplify routine tasks, while simultaneously giving leaders real-time insights into talent and skills to actively empower a high-performing workforce. Connecting these workforce insights directly to financial performance, Workday Financial Management delivers critical insights to enable smarter, faster accounting processes.",

[...] TrustRadius Workday Adaptive Planning Top Rated\n\nG2 Top Finance Products Workday Adaptive Planning\n\n## AI FOR WORKDAY ADAPTIVE PLANNING\n\n### Planning Agent\n\nFind out why over 100 customers trust the Planning Agent to drive smarter decisions with conversational, dynamic prompts.\n\nLearn More\n\n \n\n## PARTNER ECOSYSTEM\n\n### Workday Marketplace\n\nDiscover custom apps from our trusted partner ecosystem to solve frontline operational challenges.\n\nLearn More\n\n \n\nHubspot logo\n\n“With Workday Adaptive Planning, my team can complete a forecast cycle in 15-20% less time than before.”\n\n—Vice President of Financial Planning and Analysis\n\nRead Story\n\nOne platform for every plan and decision.\n\nConnect financial, workforce, and operational plans for a full view across your business.\n\n### Financial planning. AI-Fueled Efficiencies Could Make It Happen\n\nThe proliferation of artificial intelligence in the workplace, and the ensuing expected increase in productivity and efficiency, could help usher in the four-day workweek, some experts predict. [...] ##### Rising Demand for Workforce AI Skills Leads to Calls for Upskilling\n\nAs artificial intelligence technology continues to develop, the demand for workers with the ability to work alongside and manage AI systems will increase.       "url": "https://community.ibm.com/HigherLogic/System/DownloadDocumentFile.ashx?DocumentFileKey=0e5d8c4f-1fa1-4e4d-b8a5-08e4bbb32eba&forceDialog=0",

      "content": "• Enum transformation – Logic that controls how an incoming source value gets mapped to a target value e.g. map low to low; urgent to high; M to male, etc.\n• Pattern transformation – Logic that converts incoming data format to match with desired target pattern e.g. date formats, telephone format, address format, etc.\n• AI - Learns the patterns and auto-generates data transformation expressions without you requiring to write any code.       "url": "https://careers.salesforce.com/en/jobs/jr337927/senior-people-specialist/",
      "title": "Senior People Specialist, Ireland - Dublin - Salesforce Careers",
      "content": "Job Requirements\n\n 4+ years of experience consulting on human resources policies and processes with employees and managers\n Bachelor’s degree required or equivalent\n Prior experience as an HR Generalist ideal\n Prior experience in a centralized HR department or shared services organization preferred\n Experience with case management systems and Workday preferred.\n Ability to manage a high volume of employee cases\n Experience applying company policies and procedures\n Strong conflict resolution skills\n Ability to work effectively in a virtual team environment\n Strong professional verbal and written communications\n\n## Unleash Your Potential [...] Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options.\n\n### Equal Opportunity Statement. [...] ## Unleash Your Potential\n\nWhen you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and be your best, and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can do your best. Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world.\n\n### At Salesforce, we strive to create an accessible and inclusive experience for all candidates.\n\nIf you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodation Request Form.",
      "score": 0.057966188,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.bain.com/insights/google_cloud_next_2026_the_agentic_enterprise_control_plane_comes_into_view/",
      "title": "Google Cloud Next 2026: The Agentic Enterprise Control Plane ...",
      "content": "The open ecosystem story remains important. Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform offers access to more than 200 models through Model Garden, including Gemini 3.1 Pro, Gemini 3.1 Flash Image, Lyria 3, Gemma 4, and third-party models such as Anthropic’s Claude. The partner layer expanded as well through Agent Gallery and partner-built agents from Accenture, Adobe, Atlassian, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Workday, and others.\n\nBut the more interesting question is not whether Google is open. It is where Google is open. [...] ###### Will IT Accelerate or Stall Your AI Transformation?\n\nHow CIOs can fund, scale, and modernize to keep pace with escalating AI demands.\n\n##### CIO Insights\n\n###### Claude Mythos and the AI Cybersecurity Wake-Up Call\n\nA business risk of the highest order, not a technology problem to be delegated downward.\n\n###### Tags\n\n### How We've Helped Clients\n\n#### A Beauty Company Enables Always-On Brand Acceleration\n\n#### Digital Reimagining Insurance for the AI Era\n\n#### AI, Insights, and Solutions Blockchain-enabled Payment Flows: A Payments Company Reviews its Strategy\n\n### Ready to talk?\n\nWe work with ambitious leaders who want to define the future, not hide from it. Together, we achieve extraordinary outcomes. [...] The enterprise challenge is shifting from experimentation to control. That means choosing where runtime governance will live, investing in context architecture instead of treating retrieval as a one-off engineering task, strengthening non-human identity and authorization models, and defining clear boundaries around autonomy before agent deployment outpaces oversight.\n\nThat is what makes this moment more important than a standard AI product launch.\n\nThe pressing question for enterprise leaders is no longer whether agents are coming. It is whether their organization is building the control plane required to govern them.\n\n###### Authors\n\nHeadshot of Chris Green\n\n##### Chris Green\n\n##### Partner, Atlanta\n\nHeadshot of Dale Pedzinski\n\n##### Dale Pedzinski",
      "score": 0.05695076,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://www.mercer.com/en-us/insights/us-health-news/podcasts/",
      "title": "The beneficial workplace - Mercer",
      "content": "In this episode of the Beneficial Workplace, host Emily Ferreira sits down with Tracy Watts (Mercer’s National Leader of U.S. Health Policy), to discuss a key issue facing employers: healthcare affordability. As you’ll hear, the sharp upturn in health benefit cost growth is putting pressure on employers as well employees, but there are creative strategies – including a focus on financial wellness – that can help.\n\nSources:\n\nResume Now survey 92% of Americans Cut Back Spending in 2025  \nAflac Wellness Matters Survey 2025 Aflac Wellness Matters Survey  \nCommonwealth research Transforming Workplace Benefits into an Engine for Financial Health\n\n### New data reveals the impact of rising healthcare costs on employers\n\n### AI, Rx and rising costs: What we learned at HLTH this year [...] ### What’s behind the biggest health benefit cost increase in 15 years?\n\n### Health on Demand Survey results: Designing benefit programs for all generations\n\n### The future of AI in benefits is already here\n\n### How employers can support caregivers in the workplace\n\n### Compare, contrast and project: Benchmarking for benefits strategy\n\n### A look back at 2024 suggests new directions for benefit strategies\n\n### Behind the numbers: Understanding the rising health benefit cost trend\n\n### Trends in time off: New survey results\n\n### Voluntary benefits: The next frontier in flexibility\n\n### Creating a workplace that supports mental health\n\n### Longevity and the impact of an aging workforce\n\n### Women's health: Now essential to an inclusive benefits program [...] ## We're evolving. Mercer is now part of the new, expanded Marsh brand\n\nMercer\n\nMercer helps to optimize benefits, costs and investments strategies to help healthcare organizations thrive\n\n# The Beneficial Workplace\n\n## The Beneficial Workplace features data-driven conversations about employee health and well-being.\n\n## Latest episodes\n\n### Workforce response to economic uncertainty and technological change\n\nSean Connelly joins Emily Ferreira to discuss the results of a new survey of US workers, Inside Employees’ Minds. New technology, the economy, and even extreme weather are heightening stress in the workplace, but there are ways employers can help.\n\nRelated sources: Inside Employees’ Minds 2025-2026\n\n### Affordability concerns are top of mind for employees and employers",

      "url": "https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202604280940CANADANWCANADAPR_C9666-1",
      "title": "Workday Named a 2026 LinkedIn Top Company in Canada for the ...",
      "content": "For more information\n\nAbout Workday\nWorkday is the enterprise AI platform for managing people, money, and agents. Workday unifies HR and Finance on one intelligent platform with AI at the core to empower people at every level with the clarity, confidence, and insights they need to adapt quickly, make better decisions, and deliver outcomes that matter. Workday is used by more than 11,500 organizations around the world and across industries – from medium-sized businesses to more than 65% of the Fortune 500. For more information about Workday, visit workday.com.\n\n© 2026 Workday, Inc. All rights reserved. Workday and the Workday logo are trademarks of Workday, Inc. All other brand and product names are trademarks or registered trademarks of their respective holders. [...] This recognition comes as more Canadian businesses, industries, and government organizations turn to unified, AI-powered platforms like Workday that modernize HR and finance while supporting compliance and responsible innovation. As part of its previously announced investment of CAD $1 billion in Canada over the next five years, Workday is helping Canadian organizations bring people and AI agents together to transform how work gets done. Canada is one of Workday's largest global markets, with more than 1,100 employees supporting over 500 customers from various industries including financial services, technology and media, higher education, and retail.\n\nFor more information [...] # Company Announcements\n\n### Workday Named a 2026 LinkedIn Top Company in Canada for the Fourth Consecutive Year\n\nPLEASANTON, Calif.\n, April 28, 2026 /CNW/ -- Workday, Inc. (NASDAQ: WDAY), the enterprise AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, today announced it has been named a 2026 LinkedIn Top Company in Canada for the fourth consecutive year.\n\nWorkday (PRNewsfoto/Workday)\")\n\nWorkday (PRNewsfoto/Workday)\")\n\nThe annual ranking highlights the top workplaces for professionals to grow their careers in Canada, based on LinkedIn data. This recognition reflects Workday's investment in its employees in Canada, including a commitment to skills growth, opportunities to advance, and a supportive company culture.",

    {
      "url": "https://news.microsoft.com/signal/articles/work-smarter-in-90-days-a-real-world-guide-to-using-ai/",
      "title": "Work smarter in 90 days: A real-world guide to using AI",
      "content": "“The most important truth about this moment is that the outcome isn’t written yet,” Roslansky says. “The new world of work is being assembled right now, task by task, policy by policy, business by business. It will reflect the choices of the people who show up to build it.”\n\nSo if you’re looking to get started, here are the basics of their step-by-step plan — along with some specific AI prompts you can use along the way.\n\n## Days 1–30:  Build your base\n\nShifting your workday toward using AI tools and adding in uniquely human tasks can help you become more efficient and more valuable at the same time — so it’s helpful take a quick inventory of what you do and how AI fits in. List your top 12 daily or weekly tasks, then sort them into three buckets: [...] Try this in Microsoft 365 Copilot:\n\n> Help me write a short summary of how I’ve used AI to improve my work based on my emails, Teams conversations and work documents. Then turn it into: 1) a first draft of a LinkedIn post I can personalize and share with my network, and 2) three bullet points I can use in a conversation with my manager about my unique skills and how I can apply them to higher-impact work.\n\nDay 90 check-in:\n\nYou can clearly demonstrate how your work has changed, explain your AI-powered edge and articulate what’s next — whether that’s growing in your role, evolving it or pursuing new projects. [...] ## Days 61–90: Chart a path forward\n\nBy the final stretch, you’ll be using AI to help tackle many routine tasks and focusing your human skills on more strategic, interpersonal work. Now, it’s time to put the pieces together and think through what you want from your career. Assess what’s helping or holding you back. Do you have access to the right tools? Is there leadership support to use AI thoughtfully and responsibly?\n\nClarify your story\n\nTry articulating answers to these three questions, really thinking about them in depth to give thorough, honest answers:",

      "content": "• 7.5 hours daily reviewing documents • ~50 documents processed per day • 55 days in average, topping 268 days application processing time She doesn’t know yet but soon her daily routine and professional life will change forever… Meet Maria Kowalska AI generated #IBMTechXchange 6 Maria’s Transformation Journey AI generated Daily workload: works on 15 applications Processing time: 28-268 days per application Workday: 7,5 hours of manual review, check every document Stress level: High, constant pressure and errors, manual tasks and review Applicant satisfaction: Low – long waiting times, poor informed Before APA / AI Daily workload: 50+ documents processed, more applications reviewed Processing time: 14-28 days per application Workday: 7,5 hours, focused on complex cases, pre-decisions on [...] On-premise AI ensures full control, sovereignty, and regulatory compliance by keeping data, LLMs, and processes within the organization’s trusted infrastructure The convergence of BAW business workflows and AI brings agentic intelligence that is both predictable and flexible.\nOn-premise requires ongoing effort and care to stay aligned with evolving technology.\nWork paradigm shift: AI handles routine tasks, humans oversee and manage complex cases On-premise, with its strategic trust, security, and AI sovereignty, fulfills critical requirements for government and local administration environments.\n#IBMTechXchange 33 Thank you. [...] 7,5 hours, focused on complex cases, pre-decisions on easy cases Stress level: Low - AI handles routine tasks, gives summaries and insights Applicant satisfaction: High – informed, clear process, quicker decisions After APA / AI #IBMTechXchange 7 Scale • ~410,000 work permit applications per year Numbers: Ukraine 64% Belarus 10% Georgia 5% Moldova 2% Nepal 2% Other countries 17% • Ukraine 63.9% • Belarus 10.5% • Georgia 4.6% • Philippines 2.2% • Nepal 2.1% 150 nationalities applied for work permits in Poland 205k applications, 173.5k permits, 4.6k negative decisions, 7.1k discontinuations Other countries: Moldova 2.1%, India 2.0%, Colombia 1.8%, Uzbekistan 1.5%, Bangladesh 1.2%, Turkey, Indonesia, Kazakhstan, Turkmenistan • 240,000 declarations on entrusting work to a foreigner • 39,000",

      "content": "share icon\nbookmark icon\nA large group of Coca-Cola red cans stacked in a supermarket in Spain.\n\nWas this resource helpful?\n\nSHRM-CP Promo Image\n\n##### Validate your HR expertise\n\nEarning your SHRM-CP credential makes you a recognized expert and leader in the HR field.\n\n### Related Content\n\n##### How One Company Uses Digital Tools to Boost Employee Well-Being\n\nLearn how Marsh McLennan successfully boosts staff well-being with digital tools, improving productivity and work satisfaction for more than 20,000 employees.\n\n##### A 4-Day Workweek? AI-Fueled Efficiencies Could Make It Happen\n\nThe proliferation of artificial intelligence in the workplace, and the ensuing expected increase in productivity and efficiency, could help usher in the four-day workweek, some experts predict. [...] ##### Rising Demand for Workforce AI Skills Leads to Calls for Upskilling\n\nAs artificial intelligence technology continues to develop, the demand for workers with the ability to work alongside and manage AI systems will increase. This means that workers who are not able to adapt and learn these new skills will be left behind in the job market.\n\n#### HR Daily Newsletter\n\nStay up to date with the latest HR news, trends, and expert advice each business day.\n\n#### Success title\n\nSuccess caption\n\n###### Our Brands\n\nSHRM Foundation Logo\nSHRM Executive Network Logo\nCEO Circle Logo\nSHRM Business Logo\nSHRM Linkage Logo\nSHRM Labs\n\n###### Overview\n\nSHRM Named to Newsweek's 2026 America's Top Online Learning Provider List\n\n###### Advocacy\n\n###### Brand Partnership\n\n###### Member Resources [...] Designed and delivered by HR experts to empower you with the knowledge and tools you need to drive lasting change in the workplace.\n\nEasily find a local professional or student chapter in your area.\n\nPost polls, get crowdsourced answers to your questions and network with other HR professionals online.\n\nLearn about SHRM's five regional councils and the Membership Advisory Council (MAC).\n\nLearn about volunteer opportunities with SHRM.\n\nShop for HR certifications, credentials, learning, events, merchandise and more.\n\nAs a SHRM Member®, you’ll pave the path of your success with invaluable resources, world-class educational opportunities and premier events.\n\nValidate your skills with the gold standard in HR\n\nExpert-led training for real workplace change",

    {
      "url": "https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202604281300BIZWIRE_USPRX____20260428_BW144665-1",
      "title": "Box Launches Box Automate to Orchestrate Agentic Workflows",
      "content": "\"We're excited about the potential of Box Automate to transform our onboarding process,” said Evelyn Ngai, Head of GRC, Samsung, “It has the potential to make our onboarding workflow far more scalable by processing documents from Greenhouse, Workday, and new hire documents, extracting metadata we choose, and sending it to Box DocGen to generate personalized documents for new employees at scale. Additionally, by leveraging Box Automate's capabilities, we can programmatically trigger workflows based on the extracted metadata, automating task assignments to different teams and streamlining our overall onboarding process.\"\n\nAvailable Today: Box Automate [...] \"Today, industries are seeing the biggest AI ROI come from automation,\" said Aaron Levie, Co-founder and CEO of Box. \"For enterprises, this means completing in minutes what once took days, with greater accuracy and zero compromise on security - reclaiming the hours lost to previously manual and repetitive work. With Box Automate, customers can access Box’s entire AI ecosystem to create new content-driven processes that completely reimagine how work gets done. Every individual now has the opportunity to seamlessly drive efficiency at scale.\" [...] # Company Announcements\n\n### Box Launches Box Automate to Orchestrate Agentic Workflows\n\nGenerally available today, Box Automate unleashes AI across the enterprise by supporting a safe, trusted and consistent workflow platform",
      "score": 0.11055777,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/careers/university-relations/faculty-newsletter/april-2026.html",
      "title": "PwC faculty newsletter - April 2026",
      "content": "### Workforce Hopes and Fears Survey: US insights\n\nAI isn’t just changing how work gets done—it’s reshaping how people feel about work. PwC’s Workforce Hopes & Fears Survey shows employees are more excited than anxious about AI—yet daily GenAI use remains low. Learn how HR leaders play a key role in AI adoption based on our Workforce Hopes & Fears Survey.\n\n### AI-ready skills go beyond prompting\n\nA recent Computerworld article featuring PwC’s Margaret Burke explores why AI readiness is less about mastering tools and more about building human skills like critical thinking, judgment, and storytelling alongside AI fluency. Learn more here.\n\n### New PwC survey explores what separates companies that keep pace from those that pull ahead [...] PwC supported the San Francisco 49ers in launching a new app that uses generative AI and personalization to help fans access team news, navigate gameday, and connect more deeply with the franchise from anywhere in the world. Learn more here.\n\n### How organizations break AI gridlock by going beyond sandbox experiments\n\nPwC’s George Korizis emphasizes that scaling AI requires disciplined execution, not unchecked expansion. Organizations must balance innovation with governance, trust, and customer value, ensuring responsible deployment. By embedding structure, strategy, and human-centric design, PwC helps enterprises move beyond pilots to scalable, outcome-driven AI transformation that delivers measurable business impact. Read more here. [...] Earlier this month, we hosted an AI webinar for faculty that showcased several AI classroom activities and discussed the integration of AI within Audit and Tax. The recording of this session is now available on our Artificial Intelligence Resources page that can be accessed here. If you do not have access to our site, you can request it here.\n\n### PwC recognized among Fortune’s best workplaces shaping future-ready culture\n\nPwC ranks #35 on Fortune’s 2026 “100 Best Companies to Work For,” highlighting its commitment to employee experience, trust, and innovation. Learn more here.\n\n### PwC Foundation invests $2 million to advance human skills development",
      "score": 0.08744704,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/press-releases/pwc-appian-expand-alliance.html",
      "title": "PwC and Appian expand alliance for enterprise AI",
      "content": "These solutions demonstrate what it looks like when AI is applied directly to the systems and processes that run the enterprise, transforming fragmented logic into governed, production-ready applications, not just maintaining existing systems, but accelerating innovation.\n\n### Labeling and product information management [...] Across industries, many AI initiatives struggle to scale, delivering value in isolated pockets rather than translating into how the business runs day to day. While enterprises have invested heavily in AI, much of that investment has stopped short of the processes where cost and risk actually live—the manual regulatory workflows, decades-old code, and fragmented spreadsheets that sit outside IT governance and beyond the reach of modern AI tools. The expanded PwC and Appian alliance is designed to close that gap, combining PwC’s AI tech-enabled solutions with Appian Composer, an AI-augmented development tool on the Appian Platform, to convert locked-away business logic into governed, production-ready applications. [...] > “Enterprises don’t have an AI access problem—they have an AI application problem. Most of the cost and risk in a large organization sits in processes that AI can’t easily reach: regulatory workflows coordinated by email, code written decades ago, spreadsheets running critical calculations. By pairing PwC’s AI capabilities with Appian Composer, we’re helping give clients a practical way to turn those processes into governed applications—and to start realizing the return on their AI investments now, not years from now.”",
      "score": 0.06037975,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.bain.com/insights/google_cloud_next_2026_the_agentic_enterprise_control_plane_comes_into_view/",
      "title": "Google Cloud Next 2026: The Agentic Enterprise Control Plane ...",
      "content": "The open ecosystem story remains important. Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform offers access to more than 200 models through Model Garden, including Gemini 3.1 Pro, Gemini 3.1 Flash Image, Lyria 3, Gemma 4, and third-party models such as Anthropic’s Claude. The partner layer expanded as well through Agent Gallery and partner-built agents from Accenture, Adobe, Atlassian, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Workday, and others.\n\nBut the more interesting question is not whether Google is open. It is where Google is open. [...] ###### Will IT Accelerate or Stall Your AI Transformation?\n\nHow CIOs can fund, scale, and modernize to keep pace with escalating AI demands.\n\n##### CIO Insights\n\n###### Claude Mythos and the AI Cybersecurity Wake-Up Call\n\nA business risk of the highest order, not a technology problem to be delegated downward.\n\n###### Tags\n\n### How We've Helped Clients\n\n#### A Beauty Company Enables Always-On Brand Acceleration\n\n#### Digital Reimagining Insurance for the AI Era\n\n#### AI, Insights, and Solutions Blockchain-enabled Payment Flows: A Payments Company Reviews its Strategy\n\n### Ready to talk?\n\nWe work with ambitious leaders who want to define the future, not hide from it. Together, we achieve extraordinary outcomes. [...] The enterprise challenge is shifting from experimentation to control. That means choosing where runtime governance will live, investing in context architecture instead of treating retrieval as a one-off engineering task, strengthening non-human identity and authorization models, and defining clear boundaries around autonomy before agent deployment outpaces oversight.\n\nThat is what makes this moment more important than a standard AI product launch.\n\nThe pressing question for enterprise leaders is no longer whether agents are coming. It is whether their organization is building the control plane required to govern them.\n\n###### Authors\n\nHeadshot of Chris Green\n\n##### Chris Green\n\n##### Partner, Atlanta\n\nHeadshot of Dale Pedzinski\n\n##### Dale Pedzinski",
      "score": 0.05695076,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/lu/en/our-thinking/future-of-advice/the-ai-paradox.html",
      "title": "The AI paradox: Why your data foundation matters most - Deloitte",
      "content": "Ultimately, organizations will succeed with AI only if their data foundations operate at AI speed. Strong data enables effective AI, which in turn enhances and accelerates those foundations. This makes scalable, trusted, and adaptive data platforms essential for scaling AI.\n\n#### Introduction\n\nAI is steadily transitioning from experimentation into operational use, with organizations embedding chatbots, copilots, and intelligent automation into customer journeys, internal processes, and decision-making workflows across industries. Some of these initiatives are already delivering measurable value by improving service quality, increasing efficiency, and enabling new forms of interaction. [...] Although governance, quality, and architecture are widely recognized as critical, many data investments still reflect pre-AI priorities such as compliance, reporting, or technology renewal. Meanwhile, AI changes how data is consumed through real-time, machine-driven decisions that require continuous quality, traceability, explainability, and secure access to both structured and unstructured information. AI can also accelerate data operations by interpreting business intent, locating and profiling data, detecting issues, and helping generate pipelines or analytical outputs, reducing work that once took weeks to hours while keeping humans responsible for intent and accountability. [...] In an AI-augmented environment, intelligent agents can interpret business intent, connect requests to governed semantic definitions, identify and profile relevant data sources, detect quality issues, and recommend remediation strategies informed by historical knowledge. They can assist in constructing or adapting data pipelines with embedded controls and even generate an initial dataset or analytical output to accelerate validation and alignment. Activities that once required extended manual effort can therefore be reduced from weeks to hours.",
      "score": 0.051429514,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/alliances/events/dell-technologies-world.html",
      "title": "Deloitte at Dell Technologies World 2026",
      "content": "While many organizations have succeeded in AI pilots, scaling those initiatives into enterprise-wide value remains a defining challenge. Deloitte helps bridge the gap between AI strategy and execution through the Deloitte Ascend™ platform, combining demonstrated solutions with deep industry experience to drive measurable outcomes. Central to this approach is the Deloitte Enterprise AI Navigator, built on Deloitte Ascend™ on Dell. By integrating financial impact, workflow implications, and technology readiness into a unified view, it equips leaders with the clarity needed to make confident AI investment decisions, translating them into quantifiable time and cost savings. Join us to learn how this end-to-end approach can help you accelerate your AI journey and turn enterprise-wide [...] you accelerate your AI journey and turn enterprise-wide transformation from ambition into reality. [...] #### From ambition to reality: Unlocking enterprise AI at scale\n\n##### Tuesday, May 19 | 1:30-2:30 p.m. PT | Lando 4305",
      "score": 0.046729412,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/alliances/dell-technology-solutions-hybrid-cloud-and-ai.html",
      "title": "Dell Technologies Solutions: Hybrid Infrastructure and AI | Deloitte US",
      "content": "#### Agentic AI\n\n### Agentic AI\n\nDeploy purpose-built AI within your private Dell infrastructure. With a multi-agent AI platform from Deloitte layered on your Dell infrastructure, you can move beyond individual tasks to automate complex, end-to-end workflows. Define, run, and monitor autonomous agents with human-in-the-loop oversight, leveraging our asset accelerator to keep AI systems operating safely and effectively.\n\n#### Hybrid cloud infrastructure\n\n### Hybrid cloud infrastructure\n\nScale with confidence from on-premises to the edge and into cloud. Deloitte’s hybrid infrastructure approach tailors workload requirements to data consumption patterns. By balancing security and scalability across compute, storage, and network, our solutions provide flexibility without sacrificing control. [...] © 2026.
