      "content": "deloitte_logo\ndeloitte_logo\ntwo girls smiling with books in hand\n\n### World Impact\n\nmaking an impact that matters\n\n### Our History\n\ntwo girls smiling with books in hand\n\n### World Impact\n\nmaking an impact that matters\n\n### Our History\n\ntwo girls smiling with books in hand\n\n### World Impact\n\nmaking an impact that matters\n\n### Our History\n\ntwo girls smiling with books in hand\n\n### World Impact\n\nmaking an impact that matters\n\n### Our History\n\ntwo girls smiling with books in hand\n\n### World Impact\n\nmaking an impact that matters\n\n### Our History\n\ntwo girls smiling with books in hand\n\n### World Impact\n\nmaking an impact that matters\n\n### Our History\n\ntwo girls smiling with books in hand\n\n### World Impact\n\nmaking an impact that matters\n\n### Our History [...] making an impact that matters\n\n### Our History\n\nA person is lying on their stomach on the wooden deck\n\n### 2025 Impact Report\n\n### Empowering Generative AI through trust\n\nA person is lying on their stomach on the wooden deck\n\n### 2025 Impact Report\n\n### Empowering Generative AI through trust\n\nA person is lying on their stomach on the wooden deck\n\n### 2025 Impact Report\n\n### Empowering Generative AI through trust\n\nA person is lying on their stomach on the wooden deck\n\n### 2025 Impact Report\n\n### Empowering Generative AI through trust\n\nA person is lying on their stomach on the wooden deck\n\n### 2025 Impact Report\n\n### Empowering Generative AI through trust\n\n### TMT Predictions 2026\n\n### TMT Predictions 2026\n\n### TMT Predictions 2026\n\n### TMT Predictions 2026\n\n### TMT Predictions 2026 [...] © 2026.       "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/services/deloitte-private/articles/ai-in-private-markets-survey.html",
      "title": "AI in Private Markets: Investment & ROI Survey | Deloitte US",
      "content": "# Key survey takeaways\n\nView full report\n\n\"Private companies are moving beyond AI experimentation and investing in it to drive growth, improve productivity, and make faster decisions,” said Wolfe Tone, vice chair and US Deloitte Private leader. “Many are starting to see returns, and continuing the momentum will depend on how they integrate AI across leadership, governance, and the workforce. Private companies have an advantage because they can often move with greater agility and align decisions more quickly, but realizing the full value of AI will depend on how effectively they translate that agility into disciplined execution at scale.\"  \nWolfe Tone, US Deloitte Private leader\n\n### About the survey [...] © 2026.     {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-au/elevate-new-zealand/agenda.html",
      "title": "Agenda - Elevate New Zealand - Workday",
      "content": "This session will unveil a vision for a New Work Day for HR—a fundamental shift from systems that simply track work to those that actively enable it through Agentic HR.  \n  \nGrounded in three core imperatives, this strategy demonstrates how Workday’s Enterprise AI platform reinvents core HR with trusted AI, delivers a new level of business value, and builds for the future through a unified, AI-driven architecture.  \n  \nBy leveraging Workday’s unparalleled context—encompassing 75 million users and 20 years of experience—HR leaders can now deploy specialised agents across payroll, recruiting, and performance to eliminate \"talent trapped in trivia\" and allow their teams to lead with empathy and creativity.\n\nJo-Anne Ruhl\n\nKaty Colletto\n\nVice President oCHRO Product Marketing, Workday [...] Chief Digital Officer, University of Auckland\n\nIn unpredictable times, unified planning is essential for building resilience. Join us for this forward-looking breakout session exploring the strategy and vision for Workday Adaptive Planning, and how agentic AI is enabling smarter, faster decision-making across finance, HR, and operations.  \n  \nThe session will feature a live demonstration, providing a firsthand look at the latest innovations and AI-powered capabilities in Workday Adaptive Planning, and an engaging fireside chat featuring Public Trust of New Zealand, who will share their experience and key learnings from their Adaptive Planning journey.\n\nShan Moorthy\n\nVince Randall\n\nVice President Sales, Asia Pacific and Japan, Workday\n\nShan Moorthy\n\nErik Ersfjord [...] Guillem Bonavia\n\nChief Financial Officer, Shuttlerock\n\nAs AI moves from experiment to core infrastructure, CIOs are the primary architects of the machine-human workforce. This keynote sets the stage for the IT track, exploring how Workday’s open, AI-native platform eliminates technical debt by unifying the enterprise’s two most critical data sets: People and Money. We move beyond the hype to show how an open architecture allows IT leaders to stop simply maintaining systems and start orchestrating intelligence—creating a flexible digital backbone that turns raw data into measurable business agility.\n\nShan Moorthy\n\nShan Moorthy\n\nChief Technology Officer, Asia Pacific, Workday",
      "score": 0.8455478,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-us/midsize-business/overview.html",
      "title": "Midsize Business Software & Solutions | Workday GO",
      "content": "AI agents such as Deployment Agent, Self-Service Agent, and Payroll Agent available on day one that help you do more with less.\n\nFlexibility to try and add new AI agents to test environments at no costs.\n\nNot at all. Workday actually reduces complexity for midsize organizations. You get a single platform for HR, global payroll, finance, and AI agents with streamlined processes, automation, and integration capabilities built in—so you’re managing your business from one system. [...] ### The simplicity and AI you need at the price you want.\n\nOnly Workday simplifies your back office with one unified solution for HR, payroll, and finance that’s simple to buy, fast to activate, and designed for midsize budgets. Governed AI agents, powered by Sana, handle more of the everyday work, so your team spends less time on routine tasks and more time on your business from day one.\n\n### The simplicity and AI you need at the price you want.\n\nOnly Workday simplifies your back office with one unified solution for HR, payroll, and finance that’s simple to buy, fast to activate, and designed for midsize budgets. Governed AI agents, powered by Sana, handle more of the everyday work, so your team spends less time on routine tasks and more time on your business from day one. [...] Activated Workday GO in 8 weeks.\n\nVertex, Inc. logo\n\nScaled for global growth and operations.\n\nKainos logo\n\nExecuted reorganization in 1 day.\n\nOneSource Virtual logo\n\nReduced time to close from 30 days to 8 days.\n\n## RESOURCES\n\n### Resources for midsize businesses.\n\n### Resources for midsize businesses.\n\n### Questions? We have answers.\n\n### Questions? We have answers.\n\nThe built-in AI in Workday GO helps small and midsize businesses adopt enterprise AI affordably and without the complexity of building custom systems. Key benefits of our platform include:\n\nAI-powered workflows that automate many of the routine, time-consuming tasks that are common in human resources and finance.\n\nBuilt-in AI that helps organizations make more informed decisions by providing predictive insights",
      "score": 0.80405265,

    {
      "url": "https://en-gb.newsroom.workday.com/2026-04-28-SNG-Deploys-Workday-to-Transform-HR-and-Finance-Operations-and-Accelerate-Community-Impact",
      "title": "SNG Deploys Workday to Transform HR and Finance Operations ...",
      "content": "About Workday  \n Workday is the enterprise AI platform for managing people, money, and agents.Workday unifies HR and finance on one intelligent platform with AI at the core to empower people at every level with the clarity, confidence, and insights they need to adapt quickly, make better decisions, and deliver outcomes that matter. Workday is used by more than 11,500 organizations around the world and across industries – from medium-sized businesses to more than 65% of the Fortune 500. For more information about Workday, visit workday.com.\n\n© 2026 Workday, Inc. All rights reserved. Workday and the Workday logo are registered trademarks of Workday, Inc. All other brand and product names are trademarks or registered trademarks of their respective holders. [...] LONDON, UK, April 29, 2026 – Workday Inc. (NASDAQ: WDAY), the enterprise AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, today announced that SNG (Sovereign Network Group)  has successfully deployed Workday Financial Management and Workday Human Capital Management (HCM. SNG will now operate on the unified and intelligent Workday platform, designed to scale alongside the organisation and its social mission. [...] SNG’s unified platform approach provides the foundation necessary for agile, data-driven decision-making. With Workday HCM, SNG is stripping away manual HR administration to enable teams to work faster and smarter. The platform provides intuitive tools that simplify routine tasks, while simultaneously giving leaders real-time insights into talent and skills to actively empower a high-performing workforce. Connecting these workforce insights directly to financial performance, Workday Financial Management delivers critical insights to enable smarter, faster accounting processes.",

    },
    {
      "url": "https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202604280940CANADANWCANADAPR_C9666-1",
      "title": "Workday Named a 2026 LinkedIn Top Company in Canada for the ...",
      "content": "For more information\n\nAbout Workday\nWorkday is the enterprise AI platform for managing people, money, and agents. Workday unifies HR and Finance on one intelligent platform with AI at the core to empower people at every level with the clarity, confidence, and insights they need to adapt quickly, make better decisions, and deliver outcomes that matter. Workday is used by more than 11,500 organizations around the world and across industries – from medium-sized businesses to more than 65% of the Fortune 500. For more information about Workday, visit workday.com.\n\n© 2026 Workday, Inc. All rights reserved. Workday and the Workday logo are trademarks of Workday, Inc. All other brand and product names are trademarks or registered trademarks of their respective holders. [...] This recognition comes as more Canadian businesses, industries, and government organizations turn to unified, AI-powered platforms like Workday that modernize HR and finance while supporting compliance and responsible innovation. As part of its previously announced investment of CAD $1 billion in Canada over the next five years, Workday is helping Canadian organizations bring people and AI agents together to transform how work gets done. Canada is one of Workday's largest global markets, with more than 1,100 employees supporting over 500 customers from various industries including financial services, technology and media, higher education, and retail.\n\nFor more information [...] # Company Announcements\n\n### Workday Named a 2026 LinkedIn Top Company in Canada for the Fourth Consecutive Year\n\nPLEASANTON, Calif.\n, April 28, 2026 /CNW/ -- Workday, Inc. (NASDAQ: WDAY), the enterprise AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, today announced it has been named a 2026 LinkedIn Top Company in Canada for the fourth consecutive year.\n\nWorkday (PRNewsfoto/Workday)\")\n\nWorkday (PRNewsfoto/Workday)\")\n\nThe annual ranking highlights the top workplaces for professionals to grow their careers in Canada, based on LinkedIn data. This recognition reflects Workday's investment in its employees in Canada, including a commitment to skills growth, opportunities to advance, and a supportive company culture.",

      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-hk/products/adaptive-planning/overview.html",
      "title": "Adaptive Planning: Enterprise Planning Software (EPM) - Workday",
      "content": "# EPM Software\n\nUnlock limitless decision intelligence.\n\nFuel your next era of growth by keeping your brightest minds focused on strategy instead of manual workarounds—and discover why more cloud customers trust Workday Adaptive Planning than our largest competitors, combined.\n\nPlay Video (3:02)   Learn More\n\n \n\n### Make smarter decisions with AI-powered  planning.\n\nAnticipate what’s next.\n\nDrive smarter outcomes with AI-powered intelligence, backed by human-in-the-loop governance and grounded in enterprise-wide data.\n\nScale your impact.\n\nOperationalize any data source with automated connections—no storage limits or performance lag—just AI-powered insights that grow with your business.\n\nNavigate with ease. [...] TrustRadius Workday Adaptive Planning Top Rated\n\nG2 Top Finance Products Workday Adaptive Planning\n\n## AI FOR WORKDAY ADAPTIVE PLANNING\n\n### Planning Agent\n\nFind out why over 100 customers trust the Planning Agent to drive smarter decisions with conversational, dynamic prompts.\n\nLearn More\n\n \n\n## PARTNER ECOSYSTEM\n\n### Workday Marketplace\n\nDiscover custom apps from our trusted partner ecosystem to solve frontline operational challenges.\n\nLearn More\n\n \n\nHubspot logo\n\n“With Workday Adaptive Planning, my team can complete a forecast cycle in 15-20% less time than before.”\n\n—Vice President of Financial Planning and Analysis\n\nRead Story\n\nOne platform for every plan and decision.\n\nConnect financial, workforce, and operational plans for a full view across your business.\n\n### Financial planning.       "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-gb/company/latest/events-webinars/elevate-emea.html",
      "title": "Discover your local Workday Elevate EMEA event",
      "content": "## Workday Elevate AP&J\n\nWe’re thrilled to be taking Workday Elevate global with a series of events in AP&J. Join us and be inspired by the new work day we’re creating, powered by our AI platform and superintelligence for work. For updates and how to attend, please email us at workday.apj.events@workday.com\n\n## Auckland\n\n29 April 2026  \nNZICC\n\n## Seoul\n\n14 May 2026  \nWestin Seoul Parnas\n\n## Singapore\n\n21 May 2026  \nSands Expo & Convention\n\n## Sydney\n\n26 May 2026  \nICC Sydney\n\n## Tokyo\n\n28 May 2026  \nANA Intercontinental Tokyo\n\n## Hong Kong\n\n28 May 2026  \nRosewood\n\n##### Please accept cookies to continue. [...] Elevate logo\nElevate logo\n\n## Title\n\n# Workday Elevate® EMEA\n\nIn 2026, we’re showcasing the AI-elevated future of work – and we’re bringing those cutting-edge insights to a city near you. Discover your nearest Workday Elevate event in EMEA below and sign up now for updates. If you’re interested in sponsoring Workday Elevate EMEA, we’d love to hear from you. Get in touch with our team at elevateemeasponsors@workday.com to explore partnership opportunities.\n\n# Workday Elevate® EMEA [...] ## Madrid\n\n23 April 2026  \nEspacio Pablo VI\n\n## Berlin\n\n28 April 2026  \nWesthafen Event & Convention Center\n\n## London\n\n14 May 2026  \nInterContinental London – The O2\n\n## Amsterdam\n\n28 May 2026  \nTaets Art & Event Park\n\n## Paris\n\n23 June 2026  \nCarrousel du Louvre\n\n## Workday Elevate AP&J\n\nWe’re thrilled to be taking Workday Elevate global with a series of events in AP&J. Join us and be inspired by the new work day we’re creating, powered by our AI platform and superintelligence for work. For updates and how to attend, please email us at workday.apj.events@workday.com\n\n## Workday Elevate AP&J",

    {
      "url": "https://www.cio.com/article/4163021/samsung-galaxy-ai-for-business-productivity-meets-security.html",
      "title": "Samsung Galaxy AI for business: Productivity meets security - CIO",
      "content": "That’s where Galaxy AI for business comes in.\n\nAn illustration of a smartphone using the Circle to Search gesture on a coffee machine, with related results displayed on a webpage next to it.\n\nSamsung\n\n## Increase productivity throughout the workday\n\nA lot of AI value depends on whether it actually saves time during the workday. I’ve noticed that inefficiencies tend to show up in the same parts of the workflow: piecing together fragmented information (bonus points if it’s across different platforms!), figuring out what actually mattered in a meeting, losing time to research and source-checking, and identifying action items for your team.\n\nNone of it sounds particularly time-consuming on its own, but together, it can quietly add up to a meaningful amount of lost productivity. [...] If this sounds familiar, that’s because it is—and when it keeps happening across calls, chats, and teams, it eats up more of the workday than it should.\n\nThis is where Galaxy AI can cut down on that unnecessary back-and-forth:\n\nTLDR: Galaxy AI reduces communication delays by helping teams communicate across languages and spend less time revisiting calls or reworking messages, enabling them to stay clear, responsive, and aligned.\n\n## Keep AI on enterprise terms\n\nAn illustration of a smartphone displaying a folder containing documents, next to a cloud with a prohibition symbol and a toggle set to on.\n\nSamsung [...] Samsung\n\nFinally, we need to address the elephant in the room for AI adoption. Once employees start using AI to summarize meeting notes, process internal information, or speed up every day work, enterprises need to know where that data goes (like whether it’s retained or used for model training) and how much control IT actually has.\n\nTo address those concerns, Galaxy AI is built around a few important safeguards:\n\nTLDR: Galaxy AI helps reduce one of the biggest barriers to enterprise AI adoption by giving organizations clearer guardrails around data handling, cloud exposure, and IT oversight.\n\n## Take the next step with Galaxy AI for business",
      "score": 0.52173984,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202604281300BIZWIRE_USPRX____20260428_BW144665-1",
      "title": "Box Launches Box Automate to Orchestrate Agentic Workflows",
      "content": "\"We're excited about the potential of Box Automate to transform our onboarding process,” said Evelyn Ngai, Head of GRC, Samsung, “It has the potential to make our onboarding workflow far more scalable by processing documents from Greenhouse, Workday, and new hire documents, extracting metadata we choose, and sending it to Box DocGen to generate personalized documents for new employees at scale. Additionally, by leveraging Box Automate's capabilities, we can programmatically trigger workflows based on the extracted metadata, automating task assignments to different teams and streamlining our overall onboarding process.\"\n\nAvailable Today: Box Automate [...] \"Today, industries are seeing the biggest AI ROI come from automation,\" said Aaron Levie, Co-founder and CEO of Box. \"For enterprises, this means completing in minutes what once took days, with greater accuracy and zero compromise on security - reclaiming the hours lost to previously manual and repetitive work. With Box Automate, customers can access Box’s entire AI ecosystem to create new content-driven processes that completely reimagine how work gets done. Every individual now has the opportunity to seamlessly drive efficiency at scale.\" [...] # Company Announcements\n\n### Box Launches Box Automate to Orchestrate Agentic Workflows\n\nGenerally available today, Box Automate unleashes AI across the enterprise by supporting a safe, trusted and consistent workflow platform",
      "score": 0.11055777,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.accenture.com/us-en/careers/explore-careers/area-of-interest/technology-platforms-careers",
      "title": "Technology Platforms Careers & Jobs - Accenture",
      "content": "These are the 10,000 most relevant open jobs. To see more, refine your search query above.\n\n## Work you’ll do\n\nCreate custom applications, software solutions and engineer tomorrow’s tech infrastructure.\n\nWork with our partners, the world’s leading tech platform and app companies, to solve complex business challenges and help companies build resiliency.\n\n## Why we’re different\n\nPush the boundaries\n\nPartner with the technology leaders—including, Adobe, SAP, Oracle, Salesforce and Workday—and with the start-ups changing the tech landscape.\n\nBe a tech pioneer\n\nBe part of one of the largest global communities of innovators working in a fast-paced environment to come up with and deliver solutions that make an impact.\n\nLevel-up your skills [...] Skip to footer\n\nCareers\n\n# Jobs in technology platforms: Create foundations for the future\n\nYou’ll build the tech that transforms how business works for the better.\n\n###### PRO TIP\n\nTry searching using a descriptive phrase or sentence describing your dream job to see customized AI-powered results\n\n###### You've reached the character limit\n\nPress enter to see the search results\n\nReady To Explore?\n\nFind your next opportunity\n\nResults\n\n### Filters -1\n\nNo results found for ‘’\n\nResults\n\nThere are currently no jobs available that match your search criteria.\n\nSign up to get job alerts\n\n/\n\nClick here to view our equal opportunity and other employement statements.\n\nThese are our 10,000 newest job listings. To see more, refine your search query above. [...] I was always an avid science student and loved travel, and I get to enjoy a mix of both in the world of technology consulting. I find utmost pride in helping bring value and drive change for our clients. I get to help solve real-world problems and continue to progress in my career at Accenture.\n\nThe inclusive culture at Accenture has been inspiring. I’ve always had a strong voice and have been given every stage I deserved to represent myself and technology.\n\nThere are four pillars that have helped me further my career ambitions and at the same time continue to add value for business: stay relevant; think beyond; add value; and never stop learning.”\n\n \n\n## Explore more on working at Accenture\n\n Working here\n Redefining hiring with AI\n Work environment\n Technology Insights",

      "url": "https://www.gartner.com/reviews/market/document-management",
      "title": "Best Document Management Reviews 2026 | Gartner Peer Insights",
      "content": "Show More Details\n\nImage 26: Logo of Hyland OnBase\n### Hyland OnBase\n\nBy Hyland\n\n4.2\n\n(290 Ratings)\n\nOVERVIEW ALTERNATIVES\n\nOnBase provides a broad spectrum of turn-key industry and departmental solutions that are expertly\n\ntailored to meet specific business challenges. With repeatable industry solutions, OnBase provides the\n\ncapability to intelligently automate your business processes so your team can focus on higher value\n\nwork without the need to build costly customized solutions. With business ownership of solutions,\n\nOnBase enables expansion beyond IT, delivering faster ROI.\n\nShow More Details [...] Doxis Intelligent Content Automation is an AI-powered content services platform designed to bridge and automate content across best-of-breed apps like SAP, Salesforce & Microsoft to deliver a contextualized content experience and a future-ready digital workplace. What’s inside: Composable and contextual AI to harness and disseminate hidden insights; cloud-native solution suites, e.g. Doxis Intelligent Purchase-to-Pay Automation, Doxis Content Bridge for SAP & Salesforce, Doxis Healthcare; in addition to easily configurable, custom content services applications, e.g. intelligent archiving, document management, workflow automation, contract management, HR, QM, invoice and order confirmation automation; and solutions for verticals like banking, healthcare, insurance, logistics, manufacturing [...] Software \n           Enterprise Agile Planning Tools \n           Integration Platform as a Service \n           AI-Augmented Software Testing Tools \n           View All \n\n       Artificial Intelligence   \n\n           AI Code Assistants (Transitioning to Enterprise AI Coding Agents) \n           AI Application Development Platforms \n           Generative AI Knowledge Management Apps/General Productivity \n           Artificial Intelligence Applications in IT Service Management (Transitioning to AI Applications in IT Service Management) \n           Conversational AI Platforms \n           View All \n\n       Cloud Computing",

      "url": "https://www.accenture.com/my-en/careers/jobdetails?id=R00324476_en",
      "title": "HR Transformation Manager (Talent & Organization) - Accenture",
      "content": "Support clients in selecting fit-for-purpose HR technology solutions that align with their HR/Talent strategy\n Bring functional expertise and market insights to clients in specific domains (e.g. talent acquisition, workforce planning, total rewards, learning, etc.)\n Help deliver Digital HR Transformation programs, supporting the design and deploying of an integrated digital core (with HCM/Experience platform solutions such as Workday, SuccessFactors, Oracle HCM Cloud, ServiceNow)\n Manage and execute a program plan using planning, communication and training skills [...] Key Competencies and Skills:\n\nCandidate should possess:\n\n Techno-functional HR knowledge (hands-on experience on SuccessFactors/ Workday/ Other HR platforms). Certification on key modules is an added benefit\n Experience with HR process design, service delivery models and managing change - especially related to HCM platforms\n Project management skills including establishment of tracking and monitoring mechanisms\n Experience working in or with any international markets especially APAC, Middle East, Europe is an added benefit\n\nCandidate should be: [...] Minimum 8 years and above of work-experience, post a Bachelor's Degree, of which significant experience is in Human Resource transformation space. Consulting experience would be preferred.\n Fundamental understanding of the HR domains across the value chain and an awareness of key trends in the talent & organization world:\n  + Cloud / Digital HR\n  + HR Process\n  + HR Diagnostics / Analytics\n  + HR Technology\n  + Workforce Planning\n  + Talent Management including Recruitment, Performance Management, Competency Modelling, Career Framework, Reward Design, Strategic Role Assessment, etc.\n Design and deployment of HR Service Delivery Model across diverse set of organizations\n Desired knowledge of HRIT and ERP (Oracle/SAP/Workday)\n Knowledge of outsourcing business model of key HR processes",

      "url": "https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202604280940CANADANWCANADAPR_C9666-1",
      "title": "Workday Named a 2026 LinkedIn Top Company in Canada for the ...",
      "content": "For more information\n\nAbout Workday\nWorkday is the enterprise AI platform for managing people, money, and agents. Workday unifies HR and Finance on one intelligent platform with AI at the core to empower people at every level with the clarity, confidence, and insights they need to adapt quickly, make better decisions, and deliver outcomes that matter. Workday is used by more than 11,500 organizations around the world and across industries – from medium-sized businesses to more than 65% of the Fortune 500. For more information about Workday, visit workday.com.\n\n© 2026 Workday, Inc. All rights reserved. Workday and the Workday logo are trademarks of Workday, Inc. All other brand and product names are trademarks or registered trademarks of their respective holders. [...] This recognition comes as more Canadian businesses, industries, and government organizations turn to unified, AI-powered platforms like Workday that modernize HR and finance while supporting compliance and responsible innovation. As part of its previously announced investment of CAD $1 billion in Canada over the next five years, Workday is helping Canadian organizations bring people and AI agents together to transform how work gets done. Canada is one of Workday's largest global markets, with more than 1,100 employees supporting over 500 customers from various industries including financial services, technology and media, higher education, and retail.\n\nFor more information [...] # Company Announcements\n\n### Workday Named a 2026 LinkedIn Top Company in Canada for the Fourth Consecutive Year\n\nPLEASANTON, Calif.\n, April 28, 2026 /CNW/ -- Workday, Inc. (NASDAQ: WDAY), the enterprise AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, today announced it has been named a 2026 LinkedIn Top Company in Canada for the fourth consecutive year.\n\nWorkday (PRNewsfoto/Workday)\")\n\nWorkday (PRNewsfoto/Workday)\")\n\nThe annual ranking highlights the top workplaces for professionals to grow their careers in Canada, based on LinkedIn data. This recognition reflects Workday's investment in its employees in Canada, including a commitment to skills growth, opportunities to advance, and a supportive company culture.",
      "score": 0.78473216,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://news.microsoft.com/signal/articles/work-smarter-in-90-days-a-real-world-guide-to-using-ai/",
      "title": "Work smarter in 90 days: A real-world guide to using AI",
      "content": "“The most important truth about this moment is that the outcome isn’t written yet,” Roslansky says. “The new world of work is being assembled right now, task by task, policy by policy, business by business. It will reflect the choices of the people who show up to build it.”\n\nSo if you’re looking to get started, here are the basics of their step-by-step plan — along with some specific AI prompts you can use along the way.\n\n## Days 1–30:  Build your base\n\nShifting your workday toward using AI tools and adding in uniquely human tasks can help you become more efficient and more valuable at the same time — so it’s helpful take a quick inventory of what you do and how AI fits in. List your top 12 daily or weekly tasks, then sort them into three buckets: [...] Try this in Microsoft 365 Copilot:\n\n> Help me write a short summary of how I’ve used AI to improve my work based on my emails, Teams conversations and work documents. Then turn it into: 1) a first draft of a LinkedIn post I can personalize and share with my network, and 2) three bullet points I can use in a conversation with my manager about my unique skills and how I can apply them to higher-impact work.\n\nDay 90 check-in:\n\nYou can clearly demonstrate how your work has changed, explain your AI-powered edge and articulate what’s next — whether that’s growing in your role, evolving it or pursuing new projects. [...] ## Days 61–90: Chart a path forward\n\nBy the final stretch, you’ll be using AI to help tackle many routine tasks and focusing your human skills on more strategic, interpersonal work. Now, it’s time to put the pieces together and think through what you want from your career. Assess what’s helping or holding you back. Do you have access to the right tools? Is there leadership support to use AI thoughtfully and responsibly?\n\nClarify your story\n\nTry articulating answers to these three questions, really thinking about them in depth to give thorough, honest answers:",
      "score": 0.67140645,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.cio.com/article/4163021/samsung-galaxy-ai-for-business-productivity-meets-security.html",
      "title": "Samsung Galaxy AI for business: Productivity meets security - CIO",
      "content": "That’s where Galaxy AI for business comes in.\n\nAn illustration of a smartphone using the Circle to Search gesture on a coffee machine, with related results displayed on a webpage next to it.\n\nSamsung\n\n## Increase productivity throughout the workday\n\nA lot of AI value depends on whether it actually saves time during the workday. I’ve noticed that inefficiencies tend to show up in the same parts of the workflow: piecing together fragmented information (bonus points if it’s across different platforms!), figuring out what actually mattered in a meeting, losing time to research and source-checking, and identifying action items for your team.\n\nNone of it sounds particularly time-consuming on its own, but together, it can quietly add up to a meaningful amount of lost productivity. [...] If this sounds familiar, that’s because it is—and when it keeps happening across calls, chats, and teams, it eats up more of the workday than it should.\n\nThis is where Galaxy AI can cut down on that unnecessary back-and-forth:\n\nTLDR: Galaxy AI reduces communication delays by helping teams communicate across languages and spend less time revisiting calls or reworking messages, enabling them to stay clear, responsive, and aligned.\n\n## Keep AI on enterprise terms\n\nAn illustration of a smartphone displaying a folder containing documents, next to a cloud with a prohibition symbol and a toggle set to on.\n\nSamsung [...] Samsung\n\nFinally, we need to address the elephant in the room for AI adoption. Once employees start using AI to summarize meeting notes, process internal information, or speed up every day work, enterprises need to know where that data goes (like whether it’s retained or used for model training) and how much control IT actually has.\n\nTo address those concerns, Galaxy AI is built around a few important safeguards:\n\nTLDR: Galaxy AI helps reduce one of the biggest barriers to enterprise AI adoption by giving organizations clearer guardrails around data handling, cloud exposure, and IT oversight.\n\n## Take the next step with Galaxy AI for business",
      "score": 0.521886,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "title": "How Coca-Cola Is Reframing Talent Decisions with Assessments",
      "content": "share icon\nbookmark icon\nA large group of Coca-Cola red cans stacked in a supermarket in Spain.\n\nWas this resource helpful?\n\nSHRM-CP Promo Image\n\n##### Validate your HR expertise\n\nEarning your SHRM-CP credential makes you a recognized expert and leader in the HR field.\n\n### Related Content\n\n##### How One Company Uses Digital Tools to Boost Employee Well-Being\n\nLearn how Marsh McLennan successfully boosts staff well-being with digital tools, improving productivity and work satisfaction for more than 20,000 employees.\n\n##### A 4-Day Workweek? AI-Fueled Efficiencies Could Make It Happen\n\nThe proliferation of artificial intelligence in the workplace, and the ensuing expected increase in productivity and efficiency, could help usher in the four-day workweek, some experts predict. [...] ##### Rising Demand for Workforce AI Skills Leads to Calls for Upskilling\n\nAs artificial intelligence technology continues to develop, the demand for workers with the ability to work alongside and manage AI systems will increase. This means that workers who are not able to adapt and learn these new skills will be left behind in the job market.\n\n#### HR Daily Newsletter\n\nStay up to date with the latest HR news, trends, and expert advice each business day.\n\n#### Success title\n\nSuccess caption\n\n###### Our Brands\n\nSHRM Foundation Logo\nSHRM Executive Network Logo\nCEO Circle Logo\nSHRM Business Logo\nSHRM Linkage Logo\nSHRM Labs\n\n###### Overview\n\nSHRM Named to Newsweek's 2026 America's Top Online Learning Provider List\n\n###### Advocacy\n\n###### Brand Partnership\n\n###### Member Resources [...] Designed and delivered by HR experts to empower you with the knowledge and tools you need to drive lasting change in the workplace.\n\nEasily find a local professional or student chapter in your area.\n\nPost polls, get crowdsourced answers to your questions and network with other HR professionals online.\n\nLearn about SHRM's five regional councils and the Membership Advisory Council (MAC).\n\nLearn about volunteer opportunities with SHRM.\n\nShop for HR certifications, credentials, learning, events, merchandise and more.\n\nAs a SHRM Member®, you’ll pave the path of your success with invaluable resources, world-class educational opportunities and premier events.\n\nValidate your skills with the gold standard in HR\n\nExpert-led training for real workplace change",

      "url": "https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202604281300BIZWIRE_USPRX____20260428_BW144665-1",
      "title": "Box Launches Box Automate to Orchestrate Agentic Workflows",
      "content": "\"We're excited about the potential of Box Automate to transform our onboarding process,” said Evelyn Ngai, Head of GRC, Samsung, “It has the potential to make our onboarding workflow far more scalable by processing documents from Greenhouse, Workday, and new hire documents, extracting metadata we choose, and sending it to Box DocGen to generate personalized documents for new employees at scale. Additionally, by leveraging Box Automate's capabilities, we can programmatically trigger workflows based on the extracted metadata, automating task assignments to different teams and streamlining our overall onboarding process.\"\n\nAvailable Today: Box Automate [...] \"Today, industries are seeing the biggest AI ROI come from automation,\" said Aaron Levie, Co-founder and CEO of Box. \"For enterprises, this means completing in minutes what once took days, with greater accuracy and zero compromise on security - reclaiming the hours lost to previously manual and repetitive work. With Box Automate, customers can access Box’s entire AI ecosystem to create new content-driven processes that completely reimagine how work gets done. Every individual now has the opportunity to seamlessly drive efficiency at scale.\" [...] # Company Announcements\n\n### Box Launches Box Automate to Orchestrate Agentic Workflows\n\nGenerally available today, Box Automate unleashes AI across the enterprise by supporting a safe, trusted and consistent workflow platform",

I’ve always had a strong voice and have been given every stage I deserved to represent myself and technology.\n\nThere are four pillars that have helped me further my career ambitions and at the same time continue to add value for business: stay relevant; think beyond; add value; and never stop learning.”\n\n \n\n## Explore more on working at Accenture\n\n Working here\n Redefining hiring with AI\n Work environment\n Technology Insights",

      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/careers/university-relations/faculty-newsletter/april-2026.html",
      "title": "PwC faculty newsletter - April 2026",
      "content": "### Workforce Hopes and Fears Survey: US insights\n\nAI isn’t just changing how work gets done—it’s reshaping how people feel about work. PwC’s Workforce Hopes & Fears Survey shows employees are more excited than anxious about AI—yet daily GenAI use remains low. Learn how HR leaders play a key role in AI adoption based on our Workforce Hopes & Fears Survey.\n\n### AI-ready skills go beyond prompting\n\nA recent Computerworld article featuring PwC’s Margaret Burke explores why AI readiness is less about mastering tools and more about building human skills like critical thinking, judgment, and storytelling alongside AI fluency. Learn more here.\n\n### New PwC survey explores what separates companies that keep pace from those that pull ahead [...] PwC supported the San Francisco 49ers in launching a new app that uses generative AI and personalization to help fans access team news, navigate gameday, and connect more deeply with the franchise from anywhere in the world. Learn more here.\n\n### How organizations break AI gridlock by going beyond sandbox experiments\n\nPwC’s George Korizis emphasizes that scaling AI requires disciplined execution, not unchecked expansion. Organizations must balance innovation with governance, trust, and customer value, ensuring responsible deployment. By embedding structure, strategy, and human-centric design, PwC helps enterprises move beyond pilots to scalable, outcome-driven AI transformation that delivers measurable business impact. Read more here. [...] Earlier this month, we hosted an AI webinar for faculty that showcased several AI classroom activities and discussed the integration of AI within Audit and Tax. The recording of this session is now available on our Artificial Intelligence Resources page that can be accessed here. If you do not have access to our site, you can request it here.\n\n### PwC recognized among Fortune’s best workplaces shaping future-ready culture\n\nPwC ranks #35 on Fortune’s 2026 “100 Best Companies to Work For,” highlighting its commitment to employee experience, trust, and innovation. Learn more here.\n\n### PwC Foundation invests $2 million to advance human skills development",

      "content": "No Match Found\n\nHuman-centered. Results-driven.\n\n# Readiness, adoption, and workforce enablement\n\ntest with image\n\n## Overview\n\n### We don't do change management. We put organizations in motion.\n\nChange is accelerating: driven by AI, new ways of working and evolving roles. Yet too often, change management focuses on timelines instead of adoption and impact. Our outcome-based, human-centered approach uses data, digital tools and immersive experiences to drive engagement and deliver lasting results.       "content": "Summary:   \nAs an Application Developer, a typical day involves designing, building, and configuring applications that align with business processes and application requirements. The role demands adaptability to evolving project demands and a proactive approach to problem-solving within a dynamic work environment.  \n   \nRoles & Responsibilities:   \n- Expected to be an SME, collaborate and manage the team to perform.  \n- Responsible for team decisions. [...] ## Discover where this job fits at Accenture\n\n#### Software engineer jobs: Imagine it, build it, scale it\n\nCreate software that will power change and empower people.\n\nLearn more\n\n#### Technology jobs: Be the catalyst\n\nGet hands-on with the technologies that our clients need to reinvent, work in new ways and change the world for the better.\n\nLearn more\n\n#### Jobs in technology platforms: Create foundations for the future\n\nYou’ll build the tech that transforms how business works for the better.\n\nLearn more\n\n## Learn more about the hiring process at Accenture\n\n Benefits information\n Hiring process (Q&A)\n Interview tips\n Using AI [...] We work with one shared purpose: to deliver on the promise of technology and human ingenuity. Together, we drive positive change and deliver value to our clients, partners, shareholders, communities, and each other.\n\nWe believe that delivering value requires innovation, and innovation thrives in an inclusive and diverse environment.       "url": "https://www.accenture.com/in-en/careers/jobdetails?id=ATCI-5535707-S2019231_en",
      "title": "Custom Software Engineer - Accenture",
      "content": "Summary:   \nAs a Custom Software Engineer, a typical day involves designing, building, and configuring software applications tailored to meet specific business processes and application requirements. This role requires a thoughtful approach to understanding organizational needs and translating them into effective software solutions. The workday often includes collaborating with various stakeholders to ensure that the applications developed align with business goals and provide seamless functionality. Continuous evaluation and refinement of the software components are integral to maintaining high standards and supporting evolving business demands.  \n   \nRoles & Responsibilities:   \n- Expected to be an SME, collaborate and manage the team to perform.  \n- Responsible for team decisions.     {
      "url": "https://www.accenture.com/in-en/careers/jobdetails?id=ATCI-5513679-S2019328_en",
      "title": "Custom Software Engineer",
      "content": "Summary:   \nAs a Custom Software Engineer, a typical day involves designing, building, and configuring software applications tailored to meet specific business processes and application requirements. This role requires a thoughtful approach to understanding the needs of the organization and translating them into functional software solutions. The workday often includes collaborating with various stakeholders to ensure the applications align with business goals, while continuously refining and adapting the software to evolving requirements. Attention to detail and a commitment to delivering reliable and efficient applications are central to daily activities in this position.  \n   \nRoles & Responsibilities:   \n- Expected to be an SME, collaborate and manage the team to perform. [...] We work with one shared purpose: to deliver on the promise of technology and human ingenuity. Every day, more than 775,000 of us help our stakeholders continuously reinvent. Together, we drive positive change and deliver value to our clients, partners, shareholders, communities, and each other.\n\nWe believe that delivering value requires innovation, and innovation thrives in an inclusive and diverse environment. We actively foster a workplace free from bias, where everyone feels a sense of belonging and is respected and empowered to do their best work. [...] - Responsible for team decisions.  \n- Engage with multiple teams and contribute on key decisions.  \n- Provide solutions to problems for their immediate team and across multiple teams.  \n- Lead efforts to identify and implement process improvements within the team to enhance productivity and quality.  \n- Mentor junior team members by providing guidance and support to foster their professional growth.  \n- Coordinate cross-functional communication to ensure alignment and timely delivery of project milestones.  \n   \nProfessional & Technical Skills:   \n- Must To Have Skills: Proficiency in TOSCA Testsuite.  \n- Strong knowledge of software development lifecycle and application configuration.  \n- Experience in designing and implementing automated testing frameworks.",
      "score": 0.061182514,

    {
      "url": "https://www.cio.com/article/4163021/samsung-galaxy-ai-for-business-productivity-meets-security.html",
      "title": "Samsung Galaxy AI for business: Productivity meets security - CIO",
      "content": "That’s where Galaxy AI for business comes in.\n\nAn illustration of a smartphone using the Circle to Search gesture on a coffee machine, with related results displayed on a webpage next to it.\n\nSamsung\n\n## Increase productivity throughout the workday\n\nA lot of AI value depends on whether it actually saves time during the workday. I’ve noticed that inefficiencies tend to show up in the same parts of the workflow: piecing together fragmented information (bonus points if it’s across different platforms!), figuring out what actually mattered in a meeting, losing time to research and source-checking, and identifying action items for your team.\n\nNone of it sounds particularly time-consuming on its own, but together, it can quietly add up to a meaningful amount of lost productivity. [...] If this sounds familiar, that’s because it is—and when it keeps happening across calls, chats, and teams, it eats up more of the workday than it should.\n\nThis is where Galaxy AI can cut down on that unnecessary back-and-forth:\n\nTLDR: Galaxy AI reduces communication delays by helping teams communicate across languages and spend less time revisiting calls or reworking messages, enabling them to stay clear, responsive, and aligned.\n\n## Keep AI on enterprise terms\n\nAn illustration of a smartphone displaying a folder containing documents, next to a cloud with a prohibition symbol and a toggle set to on.\n\nSamsung [...] Galaxy AI tackles those routine pain points in a few key ways:\n\nTLDR: Galaxy AI helps reduce routine bottlenecks, making day-to-day work more organized, efficient, and easier to move through.\n\n## Simplify communication across the business\n\nAn illustration of a smartphone displaying a language selection pop-up with English selected and Korean listed below, beside a webpage with text blocks.\n\nSamsung\n\nCommunication has a way of getting messy in ordinary, unremarkable ways. A call moves too quickly, and someone misses a key detail. A message needs to be translated, then rewritten so it still sounds natural. The one that gets me the most is reworking the same email draft over and over because the tone is somehow still off.",
      "score": 0.3280777,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "title": "HR Transformation Manager (Talent & Organization) - Accenture",
      "content": "Support clients in selecting fit-for-purpose HR technology solutions that align with their HR/Talent strategy\n Bring functional expertise and market insights to clients in specific domains (e.g. talent acquisition, workforce planning, total rewards, learning, etc.)\n Help deliver Digital HR Transformation programs, supporting the design and deploying of an integrated digital core (with HCM/Experience platform solutions such as Workday, SuccessFactors, Oracle HCM Cloud, ServiceNow)\n Manage and execute a program plan using planning, communication and training skills [...] Key Competencies and Skills:\n\nCandidate should possess:\n\n Techno-functional HR knowledge (hands-on experience on SuccessFactors/ Workday/ Other HR platforms). Consulting experience would be preferred.\n Fundamental understanding of the HR domains across the value chain and an awareness of key trends in the talent & organization world:\n  + Cloud / Digital HR\n  + HR Process\n  + HR Diagnostics / Analytics\n  + HR Technology\n  + Workforce Planning\n  + Talent Management including Recruitment, Performance Management, Competency Modelling, Career Framework, Reward Design, Strategic Role Assessment, etc.\n Design and deployment of HR Service Delivery Model across diverse set of organizations\n Desired knowledge of HRIT and ERP (Oracle/SAP/Workday)\n Knowledge of outsourcing business model of key HR processes",

You’ll help clients understand what they need to reinvent for the future.\n\nLearn more\n\n## Learn more about the hiring process at Accenture\n\n Benefits information\n Hiring process (Q&A)\n Interview tips\n Using AI",

    {
      "url": "https://www.bain.com/insights/google_cloud_next_2026_the_agentic_enterprise_control_plane_comes_into_view/",
      "title": "Google Cloud Next 2026: The Agentic Enterprise Control Plane ...",
      "content": "The open ecosystem story remains important. Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform offers access to more than 200 models through Model Garden, including Gemini 3.1 Pro, Gemini 3.1 Flash Image, Lyria 3, Gemma 4, and third-party models such as Anthropic’s Claude. The partner layer expanded as well through Agent Gallery and partner-built agents from Accenture, Adobe, Atlassian, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Workday, and others.\n\nBut the more interesting question is not whether Google is open. It is where Google is open. [...] The enterprise challenge is shifting from experimentation to control. That means choosing where runtime governance will live, investing in context architecture instead of treating retrieval as a one-off engineering task, strengthening non-human identity and authorization models, and defining clear boundaries around autonomy before agent deployment outpaces oversight.\n\nThat is what makes this moment more important than a standard AI product launch.\n\nThe pressing question for enterprise leaders is no longer whether agents are coming. It is whether their organization is building the control plane required to govern them.\n\n###### Authors\n\nHeadshot of Chris Green\n\n##### Chris Green\n\n##### Partner, Atlanta\n\nHeadshot of Dale Pedzinski\n\n##### Dale Pedzinski [...] ###### Will IT Accelerate or Stall Your AI Transformation?\n\nHow CIOs can fund, scale, and modernize to keep pace with escalating AI demands.\n\n##### CIO Insights\n\n###### Claude Mythos and the AI Cybersecurity Wake-Up Call\n\nA business risk of the highest order, not a technology problem to be delegated downward.\n\n###### Tags\n\n### How We've Helped Clients\n\n#### A Beauty Company Enables Always-On Brand Acceleration\n\n#### Digital Reimagining Insurance for the AI Era\n\n#### AI, Insights, and Solutions Blockchain-enabled Payment Flows: A Payments Company Reviews its Strategy\n\n### Ready to talk?\n\nWe work with ambitious leaders who want to define the future, not hide from it. Together, we achieve extraordinary outcomes.",
      "score": 0.062887095,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/careers/university-relations/faculty-newsletter/april-2026.html",
      "title": "PwC faculty newsletter - April 2026",
      "content": "### Workforce Hopes and Fears Survey: US insights\n\nAI isn’t just changing how work gets done—it’s reshaping how people feel about work. PwC’s Workforce Hopes & Fears Survey shows employees are more excited than anxious about AI—yet daily GenAI use remains low. Learn how HR leaders play a key role in AI adoption based on our Workforce Hopes & Fears Survey.\n\n### AI-ready skills go beyond prompting\n\nA recent Computerworld article featuring PwC’s Margaret Burke explores why AI readiness is less about mastering tools and more about building human skills like critical thinking, judgment, and storytelling alongside AI fluency. Learn more here.\n\n### New PwC survey explores what separates companies that keep pace from those that pull ahead [...] PwC supported the San Francisco 49ers in launching a new app that uses generative AI and personalization to help fans access team news, navigate gameday, and connect more deeply with the franchise from anywhere in the world. Learn more here.\n\n### How organizations break AI gridlock by going beyond sandbox experiments\n\nPwC’s George Korizis emphasizes that scaling AI requires disciplined execution, not unchecked expansion. Organizations must balance innovation with governance, trust, and customer value, ensuring responsible deployment. By embedding structure, strategy, and human-centric design, PwC helps enterprises move beyond pilots to scalable, outcome-driven AI transformation that delivers measurable business impact. Read more here. [...] Earlier this month, we hosted an AI webinar for faculty that showcased several AI classroom activities and discussed the integration of AI within Audit and Tax. The recording of this session is now available on our Artificial Intelligence Resources page that can be accessed here. If you do not have access to our site, you can request it here.\n\n### PwC recognized among Fortune’s best workplaces shaping future-ready culture\n\nPwC ranks #35 on Fortune’s 2026 “100 Best Companies to Work For,” highlighting its commitment to employee experience, trust, and innovation. Learn more here.\n\n### PwC Foundation invests $2 million to advance human skills development",

    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/about-us/newsroom/press-releases/pwc-appian-expand-alliance.html",
      "title": "PwC and Appian expand alliance for enterprise AI",
      "content": "Across industries, many AI initiatives struggle to scale, delivering value in isolated pockets rather than translating into how the business runs day to day. While enterprises have invested heavily in AI, much of that investment has stopped short of the processes where cost and risk actually live—the manual regulatory workflows, decades-old code, and fragmented spreadsheets that sit outside IT governance and beyond the reach of modern AI tools. The expanded PwC and Appian alliance is designed to close that gap, combining PwC’s AI tech-enabled solutions with Appian Composer, an AI-augmented development tool on the Appian Platform, to convert locked-away business logic into governed, production-ready applications. [...] These solutions demonstrate what it looks like when AI is applied directly to the systems and processes that run the enterprise, transforming fragmented logic into governed, production-ready applications, not just maintaining existing systems, but accelerating innovation.\n\n### Labeling and product information management [...] > “Enterprises don’t have an AI access problem—they have an AI application problem. Most of the cost and risk in a large organization sits in processes that AI can’t easily reach: regulatory workflows coordinated by email, code written decades ago, spreadsheets running critical calculations. By pairing PwC’s AI capabilities with Appian Composer, we’re helping give clients a practical way to turn those processes into governed applications—and to start realizing the return on their AI investments now, not years from now.”",
      "score": 0.055458937,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.mercer.com/insights/talent-and-transformation/attracting-and-retaining-talent/balancing-support--cost-and-continuity-in-uncertain-times/",

      "content": "## We’re evolving. Here is how HR and business leaders can balance resilience, continuity and workforce flexibility while protecting trust and recovery readiness.",

      "title": "Workday Canada | The Enterprise AI Platform for HR & Finance",
      "content": "Drive faster, smarter frontline operations and enable a connected, agile workforce with AI agents and mobile tools.\n\nGartner white logo\n\nA Leader in the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Cloud HCM Suites for 1,000+ Employee Enterprises.       "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-au/elevate-new-zealand/agenda.html",
      "title": "Agenda - Elevate New Zealand - Workday",
      "content": "This session will unveil a vision for a New Work Day for HR—a fundamental shift from systems that simply track work to those that actively enable it through Agentic HR.  \n  \nGrounded in three core imperatives, this strategy demonstrates how Workday’s Enterprise AI platform reinvents core HR with trusted AI, delivers a new level of business value, and builds for the future through a unified, AI-driven architecture.  \n  \nBy leveraging Workday’s unparalleled context—encompassing 75 million users and 20 years of experience—HR leaders can now deploy specialised agents across payroll, recruiting, and performance to eliminate \"talent trapped in trivia\" and allow their teams to lead with empathy and creativity.\n\nJo-Anne Ruhl\n\nKaty Colletto\n\nVice President oCHRO Product Marketing, Workday [...] Chief Digital Officer, University of Auckland\n\nIn unpredictable times, unified planning is essential for building resilience. Join us for this forward-looking breakout session exploring the strategy and vision for Workday Adaptive Planning, and how agentic AI is enabling smarter, faster decision-making across finance, HR, and operations.  \n  \nThe session will feature a live demonstration, providing a firsthand look at the latest innovations and AI-powered capabilities in Workday Adaptive Planning, and an engaging fireside chat featuring Public Trust of New Zealand, who will share their experience and key learnings from their Adaptive Planning journey.\n\nShan Moorthy\n\nVince Randall\n\nVice President Sales, Asia Pacific and Japan, Workday\n\nShan Moorthy\n\nErik Ersfjord [...] Guillem Bonavia\n\nChief Financial Officer, Shuttlerock\n\nAs AI moves from experiment to core infrastructure, CIOs are the primary architects of the machine-human workforce. This keynote sets the stage for the IT track, exploring how Workday’s open, AI-native platform eliminates technical debt by unifying the enterprise’s two most critical data sets: People and Money. We move beyond the hype to show how an open architecture allows IT leaders to stop simply maintaining systems and start orchestrating intelligence—creating a flexible digital backbone that turns raw data into measurable business agility.\n\nShan Moorthy\n\nShan Moorthy\n\nChief Technology Officer, Asia Pacific, Workday",

      "content": "AI agents such as Deployment Agent, Self-Service Agent, and Payroll Agent available on day one that help you do more with less.\n\nFlexibility to try and add new AI agents to test environments at no costs.\n\nNot at all. Workday actually reduces complexity for midsize organizations. You get a single platform for HR, global payroll, finance, and AI agents with streamlined processes, automation, and integration capabilities built in—so you’re managing your business from one system. [...] ### The simplicity and AI you need at the price you want.\n\nOnly Workday simplifies your back office with one unified solution for HR, payroll, and finance that’s simple to buy, fast to activate, and designed for midsize budgets. Governed AI agents, powered by Sana, handle more of the everyday work, so your team spends less time on routine tasks and more time on your business from day one.\n\n### The simplicity and AI you need at the price you want.\n\nOnly Workday simplifies your back office with one unified solution for HR, payroll, and finance that’s simple to buy, fast to activate, and designed for midsize budgets. We have answers.\n\nThe built-in AI in Workday GO helps small and midsize businesses adopt enterprise AI affordably and without the complexity of building custom systems. Key benefits of our platform include:\n\nAI-powered workflows that automate many of the routine, time-consuming tasks that are common in human resources and finance.\n\nBuilt-in AI that helps organizations make more informed decisions by providing predictive insights",

    {
      "url": "https://en-gb.newsroom.workday.com/2026-04-28-SNG-Deploys-Workday-to-Transform-HR-and-Finance-Operations-and-Accelerate-Community-Impact",
      "title": "SNG Deploys Workday to Transform HR and Finance Operations ...",
      "content": "About Workday  \n Workday is the enterprise AI platform for managing people, money, and agents.Workday unifies HR and finance on one intelligent platform with AI at the core to empower people at every level with the clarity, confidence, and insights they need to adapt quickly, make better decisions, and deliver outcomes that matter. Workday is used by more than 11,500 organizations around the world and across industries – from medium-sized businesses to more than 65% of the Fortune 500. For more information about Workday, visit workday.com.\n\n© 2026 Workday, Inc. All rights reserved. Workday and the Workday logo are registered trademarks of Workday, Inc. All other brand and product names are trademarks or registered trademarks of their respective holders. [...] LONDON, UK, April 29, 2026 – Workday Inc. (NASDAQ: WDAY), the enterprise AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, today announced that SNG (Sovereign Network Group)  has successfully deployed Workday Financial Management and Workday Human Capital Management (HCM. SNG will now operate on the unified and intelligent Workday platform, designed to scale alongside the organisation and its social mission. [...] SNG’s unified platform approach provides the foundation necessary for agile, data-driven decision-making. With Workday HCM, SNG is stripping away manual HR administration to enable teams to work faster and smarter. The platform provides intuitive tools that simplify routine tasks, while simultaneously giving leaders real-time insights into talent and skills to actively empower a high-performing workforce. Connecting these workforce insights directly to financial performance, Workday Financial Management delivers critical insights to enable smarter, faster accounting processes.",
      "score": 0.78886026,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-au/why-workday/our-technology/data-cloud.html",
      "title": "Workday Data Cloud: Enterprise Data Management & Analytics",
      "content": "Advanced analytics and AI.\n\nBy embracing open industry standards like Apache Iceberg and JDBC, we maximize flexibility and avoid integration vendor lock-in, protecting your Workday investment as your data strategy evolves.\n\nAdvanced analytics and AI.\n\nBy embracing open industry standards like Apache Iceberg and JDBC, we maximize flexibility and avoid integration vendor lock-in, protecting your Workday investment as your data strategy evolves.\n\n## AI Agents\n\n### Go all in on agentic AI—simply and securely.\n\nWorkday Data Cloud uses trusted people and finance data to power Workday Agents, partner agents, and your custom agents built on Workday Build. This unified foundation ensures all agents act directly within your business processes with governance and audit controls.       "url": "https://careers.ey.com/ey/job/London-Director%2C-AI-HR-Reimagined%2C-HRTT-SE1-2AF/1388437833/",
      "title": "Director, AI HR Reimagined, HRTT Job Details - EY careers",
      "content": "Be innovative in AI.HR operating model redesign – bringing different and new approaches and ideas\n Be passionate about using technology to create more human, inclusive experiences\n Be a people leader, with experience of leading others, supporting junior team members through client delivery to support their development\n Be platform-agnostic however have functional knowledge of leading platforms such as SAP SuccessFactors, Workday, ServiceNow, Moveworks, Eightfold,\n Be a strong communicator with the ability to influence senior stakeholders\n Be curious, innovative and driven by a desire to help our clients achieve their goals\n Be professional, quickly establishing personal credibility and demonstrating expertise [...] Experience in leading and shaping a AI.HR Point of View – being able to articulate trends, forces and resultant strategies for HR functions credibly\n Ability to bring provocation and thinking on the future role of HR in the AI Workforce – and translate this into new HR operating models of the future – holding debate and challenge conversations with clients on the topic\n Experience in business development and sales including demonstrating meeting / exceeding set financial performance targets. Proven track record in pursuing and leading delivery of £1m+ HR transformations – shaping specific solutions, responses, and commercials. Ability to build a pipeline of work and hold an active book of business [...] Lead development of a new AI.HR Point of View which is relevant, scalable and drive opportunities with our clients\n Bring leading insights from the external market and thought-provoking ideas to our clients as the world of work changes\n Reimagine HR operating and service delivery models, embedding GBS, automation, and AI.\n Shape AI and people technology strategies, simplifying landscapes and enabling human-centred experiences.\n Drive HR GBS transformation – from strategy and design to implementation – optimising efficiency, scalability, and impact.\n Guide clients to become AI-ready across data, process, governance, and people skills.\n Build trusted relationships with senior stakeholders, influencing C-suite agendas.\n\nBeing successful as a Director",

      "content": "These are the 10,000 most relevant open jobs. To see more, refine your search query above.\n\n## Work you’ll do\n\nCreate custom applications, software solutions and engineer tomorrow’s tech infrastructure.\n\nWork with our partners, the world’s leading tech platform and app companies, to solve complex business challenges and help companies build resiliency.\n\n## Why we’re different\n\nPush the boundaries\n\nPartner with the technology leaders—including, Adobe, SAP, Oracle, Salesforce and Workday—and with the start-ups changing the tech landscape.\n\nBe a tech pioneer\n\nBe part of one of the largest global communities of innovators working in a fast-paced environment to come up with and deliver solutions that make an impact.\n\nLevel-up your skills [...] Skip to footer\n\nCareers\n\n# Jobs in technology platforms: Create foundations for the future\n\nYou’ll build the tech that transforms how business works for the better.\n\n###### PRO TIP\n\nTry searching using a descriptive phrase or sentence describing your dream job to see customized AI-powered results\n\n###### You've reached the character limit\n\nPress enter to see the search results\n\nReady To Explore?\n\nFind your next opportunity\n\nResults\n\n### Filters -1\n\nNo results found for ‘’\n\nResults\n\nThere are currently no jobs available that match your search criteria.\n\nSign up to get job alerts\n\n/\n\nClick here to view our equal opportunity and other employement statements.\n\nThese are our 10,000 newest job listings. To see more, refine your search query above. [...] I was always an avid science student and loved travel, and I get to enjoy a mix of both in the world of technology consulting. I find utmost pride in helping bring value and drive change for our clients. I get to help solve real-world problems and continue to progress in my career at Accenture.\n\nThe inclusive culture at Accenture has been inspiring. I’ve always had a strong voice and have been given every stage I deserved to represent myself and technology.\n\nThere are four pillars that have helped me further my career ambitions and at the same time continue to add value for business: stay relevant; think beyond; add value; and never stop learning.”\n\n \n\n## Explore more on working at Accenture\n\n Working here\n Redefining hiring with AI\n Work environment\n Technology Insights",

      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/careers/university-relations/faculty-newsletter/april-2026.html",
      "title": "PwC faculty newsletter - April 2026",
      "content": "### Workforce Hopes and Fears Survey: US insights\n\nAI isn’t just changing how work gets done—it’s reshaping how people feel about work. PwC’s Workforce Hopes & Fears Survey shows employees are more excited than anxious about AI—yet daily GenAI use remains low. Learn how HR leaders play a key role in AI adoption based on our Workforce Hopes & Fears Survey.\n\n### AI-ready skills go beyond prompting\n\nA recent Computerworld article featuring PwC’s Margaret Burke explores why AI readiness is less about mastering tools and more about building human skills like critical thinking, judgment, and storytelling alongside AI fluency. Learn more here.\n\n### New PwC survey explores what separates companies that keep pace from those that pull ahead [...] PwC supported the San Francisco 49ers in launching a new app that uses generative AI and personalization to help fans access team news, navigate gameday, and connect more deeply with the franchise from anywhere in the world. Learn more here.\n\n### How organizations break AI gridlock by going beyond sandbox experiments\n\nPwC’s George Korizis emphasizes that scaling AI requires disciplined execution, not unchecked expansion. Organizations must balance innovation with governance, trust, and customer value, ensuring responsible deployment. By embedding structure, strategy, and human-centric design, PwC helps enterprises move beyond pilots to scalable, outcome-driven AI transformation that delivers measurable business impact. Read more here. [...] Earlier this month, we hosted an AI webinar for faculty that showcased several AI classroom activities and discussed the integration of AI within Audit and Tax. The recording of this session is now available on our Artificial Intelligence Resources page that can be accessed here. If you do not have access to our site, you can request it here.\n\n### PwC recognized among Fortune’s best workplaces shaping future-ready culture\n\nPwC ranks #35 on Fortune’s 2026 “100 Best Companies to Work For,” highlighting its commitment to employee experience, trust, and innovation. Learn more here.\n\n### PwC Foundation invests $2 million to advance human skills development",
