    },
    {
      "url": "https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2026/04/28/unlocking-human-ambition-to-drive-business-growth-with-ai/",
      "title": "Unlocking human ambition to drive business growth with AI",
      "content": "Frontier Transformation is changing how organizations operate, compete and grow. By embedding intelligence into the flow of work and grounding it in enterprise‑grade trust, businesses are operating with greater precision and expanding what their teams can achieve at global scale. With an open, model-diverse and secure platform, Microsoft enables organizations to unlock human ambition and leverage AI for growth — transforming their unique IQ into decisions and actions that drive measurable business outcomes. We are grateful for the continued trust of our customers and partners. Together, we are shaping how every organization can lead with AI. [...] _Judson Althoff is the chief executive officer of the commercial business at Microsoft. He is responsible for the product strategy, sales, services, support, marketing,operations and revenue growth of the company’s commercial business, which operates in more than 120 regional and national subsidiaries globally._\n\nTags: Azure OpenAI, Defender XDR, Microsoft 365 Copilot, Microsoft Agent 365, Microsoft Entra ID, Microsoft Fabric, Microsoft Foundry, Microsoft IQ, Microsoft Sentinel, Power Platform\n\n### Related Blogs\n\nJan 27, 2026 |Judson Althoff\n\n### How Microsoft is empowering Frontier Transformation with Intelligence + Trust\n\nNov 11, 2025 |Alysa Taylor\n\n### Bridging the AI divide: How Frontier Firms are transforming business\n\nOct 28, 2025 |Judson Althoff [...] serves more than 250,000 members with complex financial products and high service expectations. By deploying Microsoft 365 Copilot and Copilot Studio, the organization reduced administrative effort and accelerated service delivery, reaching 93% employee adoption and 90% weekly usage. With AI embedded in daily workflows, employees can instantly access and bring together member context, policies and procedures — enabling faster decision cycles and more proactive, personalized guidance. By automating routine work, teams are creating space to focus on higher-value client conversations that deepen relationships, advance financial outcomes and drive member growth — with human ambition at the center of every interaction. At the same time, moving from reactive work to real-time,",
      "score": 0.9856076,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.ey.com/en_ca/insights/financial-services/ai-is-reshaping-the-insurance-workforce",
      "title": "AI is reshaping the insurance workforce | EY - Canada",
      "content": "#### Make the “why” tangible\n\nThe workforce has evolved from one-size-fits-all into personalized experiences and expectations around career, rewards and wellbeing. People must understand how to use AI day to day, understand expectations and see the potential benefits. Successful adoption depends on trust, clarity and support. So create tailored adoption journeys with appropriate guardrails for responsible AI use.\n\n### A leadership moment, not a technology decision\n\nGenerative and agentic AI are no longer future possibilities for insurers: they’re already reshaping how work gets done. [...] This approach strengthens alignment between strategy and talent while reducing reliance on outdated job-based assumptions. Over time, making skills visible and measurable supports greater agility, consistency and accountability across the organization.\n\n#### Build transformation as capability\n\nOrganizations need change-capable people and processes to adapt and keep pace. Lessons from pilot initiatives must translate quickly into scale, clearing roadblocks along the way. Leaders should model the use of AI as a thought partner and reward others for doing the same. When AI is treated as an enduring way of working, not a one-time project, these capabilities can take hold.\n\n#### Make the “why” tangible [...] #### Summary\n\nAI is rapidly moving from experimentation to embedded, agent‑led work in insurance, reshaping how roles are performed and where judgment and accountability sit. As generative and agentic AI drive productivity, cost and service improvements, insurers must rethink skills, operating models and workforce strategies in tandem. Success will depend less on deploying tools and more on intentional capacity planning, skill‑based role design, cultural change and leadership accountability. The insurers that actively shape how people work with AI will be better positioned for resilience, growth and trust in the years ahead.\n\nAbout this article",
      "score": 0.9458012,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.servicenow.com/uk/blogs/2026/4-best-practices-ai-risk-management",
      "title": "4 Best Practices for AI Risk Management - ServiceNow Blog",
      "content": "The role of a machine learning engineer, for instance, is to make sure that AI delivers the intended outcomes without making mistakes. If an error does occur, the engineer should help ensure it doesn’t negatively impact the business. This makes it critical to collect and review AI outputs while still adhering to data regulations.\n\nServiceNow AI Control Tower is the enterprise control and governance plane for AI, helping teams discover, manage, and measure AI systems in compliance with regulations. It enables real-time monitoring, generates audit-ready compliance evidence, and triggers governance remediation workflows to drive end-to-end risk reduction.\n\n### 2. Give AI systems distinct roles [...] With defined roles, AI agents can work together to support complex, multi-step workflows. ServiceNow AI Agent Orchestrator coordinates teams of specialised agents across tasks, systems, and departments to work towards common goals and drive productivity at scale.\n\n### 3. Take small, controlled steps\n\nWhen leaders ask AI consultants whether they should go full speed ahead with a list of new AI use cases, the answer is usually no. The safest way to realise value from AI is to enhance core processes rather than pursue new use cases without the experience to govern them. [...] Leaders say they see the most value when using AI to make time-consuming, manual tasks more efficient. You can run the before and after processes side by side to test whether AI delivers on this promise. This allows you to measure which iteration of the workflow performs better and decide whether to continue development or pivot AI resources to more impactful use cases.\n\nIt’s helpful to take measured steps. Phasing your AI implementation gives you the control and audit trail needed to manage risk at each stage.\n\n### 4. Plan for breakage",
      "score": 0.89982784,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.ey.com/en_us/alliances/dell",
      "title": "EY-Dell Technologies Alliance | EY - US",
      "content": "Scaling AI securely in regulated industries\n\nIndustries with strict data and compliance requirements need more than a cloud-first AI approach. They need a solution that meets regulatory demands without slowing innovation.\n\nBuilding operational resilience in navigating evolving cyber threats\n\nIntelligent enterprise cyber resilience is essential for businesses to operate safely and securely in today’s environment. The EY-Dell Alliance helps protect critical operations and reduce downtime.\n\n## The team\n\nPhotographic portrait of Johnny Sandhu\n\n### Johnny Sandhu\n\nPhotographic portrait of Scott Leete\n\n### Scott Leete [...] Greg Sarafin and Orlan Bosting sitting on stage\n\nHow ecosystem thinking can help create a sustainable future\n\nGreg Sarafin and Orlan Boston discuss ecosystems, collaboration, technology and sustainability\n\n### How compliance-driven enterprises are scaling AI on their terms\n\nJohnny Sandhu, EY Global Dell Technologies Alliance Leader, explores how compliance-driven enterprises scale AI securely with private and hybrid models that cut costs and improve performance. Read more on CIO.com.\n\n### Digital doppelgangers: The future of AI at work\n\nRakesh Malhotra, Principal, Digital & Emerging Technologies, Ernst & Young LLP, unveils how agentic AI is transforming productivity and ethics across industries.\n\n### How to jump-start your digitalization journey [...] LONDON, 13 SEPTEMBER 2023 – The global EY organization (EY) today announces the launch of EY.ai, a unifying platform that brings together human capabilities and artificial intelligence (AI) to help clients transform their businesses through confident and responsible adoption of AI.\n\n## Events\n\nImage of a conference that takes place in a large conference room\n\n#### NVIDIA GTC San Jose 2026\n\nGet ready for the ultimate AI and accelerated computing experience! At GTC we will showcase our latest NVIDIA-based industry and domain solutions. Engage with our leaders and learn how AI is tangibly delivering results for organizations. Learn more.\n\nAudience at the conference hall.\n\n#### Mobile World Congress",
      "score": 0.67405045,

    {
      "url": "https://www.servicenow.com/uk/blogs/2026/how-is-servicenow-using-ai-agents",
      "title": "How Is ServiceNow Using AI Agents?",
      "content": "The results have been transformative: AI agents now handle 90% of employee IT support requests, with 96% resolution efficiency. This shift has allowed us to redeploy 85% of our human IT support agents to higher-value work, such as innovating ways to serve employees across HR, finance, and customer support.\n\nThe remaining 15% of human agents working on the service desk have been upskilled to manage AI agents, support the most complex cases, and provide VIP support. They use the ServiceNow AI Platform to see what fixes have been executed, monitor ongoing cases, and action tasks that require their input.\n\n### 4. Accelerate software request fulfilment [...] ### 2. Enhance security measures\n\nAs the size and complexity of our operations has grown, so has the need to protect our IT assets. A large volume of security alerts and support tickets made it harder to prioritise the most critical threats.\n\nOur IT and security teams responded quickly—shifting from reactive to proactive operations to continue protecting our company and customers while driving business growth.\n\nTeams operate side by side on the ServiceNow AI Platform, powered by a single source of truth: Configuration Management Database. This shared context enables human and AI support agents to detect and respond to risks faster, at a greater scale. The benefits include: [...] Faster cybersecurity operations: AI agents detect malicious activity, draft resolution notes, and generate post-impact analyses to help inform future investigations.\n Less manual work: AI handles repetitive tasks such as scheduling server patches, increasing productivity in this workflow by 53% and reducing the burden on the IT team.\n\nPreviously, when a user reported a phishing attempt, security analysts would have to write queries and search for similar incidents across the IT estate.",
      "score": 0.56025267,
      "raw_content": null
    },

    {
      "url": "https://newsroom.accenture.com/news/2025/accenture-helps-organizations-advance-agentic-ai-with-gemini-enterprise",
      "title": "Accenture Helps Organizations Advance Agentic AI with Gemini Enterprise",
      "content": "Accenture is advancing agentic AI with support for Gemini Enterprise, a new agentic platform designed to bring the full power of Google’s AI to every employee and every workflow. With Accenture’s deep experience in cloud and AI technologies, engineering capabilities, and technical expertise on Google Cloud technology, the two companies will help drive operational efficiencies and business value using Google’s latest AI innovations. [...] “With Gemini Enterprise, we are making it possible for every employee and workflow to benefit from the latest advancements in Google’s AI,” said Victor Morales, VP of GSI Partnerships, Google Cloud. “We’re combining our AI leadership with Accenture's strategic expertise to help clients solve their most complex challenges and achieve measurable outcomes.”\n\nAccenture is deepening its commitment to agentic reinvention with Google Cloud through the evolution of its joint generative AI Center of Excellence (CoE) by expanding it with agentic capabilities to support clients. Powered by Google Gemini models and now with Gemini Enterprise, the CoE will provide clients with the resources they need to scale and orchestrate multi-agent systems that deliver measurable business outcomes. [...] The companies are delivering value and enabling clients to solve complex business challenges with AI across industries, such as:\n\nTo demonstrate the rapid evolution of agentic AI and its transformative potential, Accenture recently hosted a Google Agentic AI Codefest competition to build AI agents that assist organizations across 15 industries. These agents are now available through Google Cloud Marketplace and include next-gen agents, conversational agents, and AgentEngine agents. More than 450 engineered agents built by Accenture are currently available on Google Cloud Marketplace, which will be accessible directly within Gemini Enterprise so that organizations can easily combine agents into one singular view.",
      "score": 0.86358756,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://bankingblog.accenture.com/agentic-ai-impact-workforce-change?amp=",
      "title": "AI impact on workforce change and the role of HR in financial services | Accenture Banking Blog",
      "content": "### How agentic AI is reshaping financial services work\n\nAcross banking and insurance, AI is automating routine tasks, augmenting complex work, but the real shift is how human judgement is redeployed to higher-value decisions. As one insurance client put it, the goal is to “take the robot out of the human”. Agentic AI does exactly that: semi-independent, specialist agents work alongside employees like capable coworkers.\n\nThe impact is neither uniform nor inevitable. AI does not affect every task in a role and outcomes are not predetermined. Leaders make deliberate choices about what should remain human work and what should shift to AI. This is less about redesigning roles and more about designing work for outcomes and intent, not job titles.\n\nSector-by-sector impact [...] ### Conclusion\n\nIn this penultimate blog, we explored how financial services firms must reshape their workforce. Agentic AI requires us to think differently about humans and agents working together. Banks and insurers are likely to employ smaller but far more digitally and human skilled workforces, driving sustained demand for reskilling. HR must be central to this transition, helping leaders navigate change and manage the hybrid workforce.\n\nIn the final blog in this series, I will explore how leadership, culture and operating models must also evolve to sustain this transformation.\n\n#### Andy Young\n\nManaging Director – Financial Services Talent & Organisation Lead\n\nView Profile\n\n#### Author\n\n#### Andy Young\n\nManaging Director – Financial Services Talent & Organisation Lead\n\nView Profile [...] Sector-by-sector impact\n\nAgentic AI will reshape how value is created across the financial services workforce — not just the workforce itself. The right strategy is therefore highly organization specific.\n\n#### Considering individuals, teams and networks\n\nReinventing work means designing for individuals and for the teams and networks that create value.\n\nMost knowledge work in financial services is team based. Yet few teams are adapting effectively. Our Talent Reinventors survey found that only 19% of employees say their team experiments with AI together, and just 17% feel psychologically safe to share new ideas.",
      "score": 0.8201387,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://bankingblog.accenture.com/agentic-ai-impact-workforce-change",
      "title": "AI impact on workforce change and the role of HR in financial services | Accenture Banking Blog",
      "content": "Agentic AI will reshape how value is created across the financial services workforce — not just the workforce itself. The right strategy is therefore highly organization specific.\n\n#### Considering individuals, teams and networks\n\nReinventing work means designing for individuals and for the teams and networks that create value.\n\nMost knowledge work in financial services is team based. Yet few teams are adapting effectively. Our Talent Reinventors survey found that only 19% of employees say their team experiments with AI together, and just 17% feel psychologically safe to share new ideas. [...] Accenture’s Banking Top Trends 2026: Unconstrained banking is here\n\nLearn More\n\n### How agentic AI is reshaping financial services work\n\nAcross banking and insurance, AI is automating routine tasks, augmenting complex work, but the real shift is how human judgement is redeployed to higher-value decisions. As one insurance client put it, the goal is to “take the robot out of the human”. Agentic AI does exactly that: semi-independent, specialist agents work alongside employees like capable coworkers. [...] ### Conclusion\n\nIn this penultimate blog, we explored how financial services firms must reshape their workforce. Agentic AI requires us to think differently about humans and agents working together. Banks and insurers are likely to employ smaller but far more digitally and human skilled workforces, driving sustained demand for reskilling. HR must be central to this transition, helping leaders navigate change and manage the hybrid workforce.\n\nIn the final blog in this series, I will explore how leadership, culture and operating models must also evolve to sustain this transformation.\n\n# Related Posts\n\nBackground Image\n\n###### By Andy Young\n\n### AI implications for leadership, culture and operating models in financial services\n\nRead more 768 Views\n\nBackground Image\n\n###### By Andy Young",
      "score": 0.79369855,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.cio.com/article/4022657/accenture-reimagines-it-operations-with-agentic-ai.html",
      "title": "Accenture reimagines IT operations with agentic AI | CIO",
      "content": "## Topics\n\nThor Olavsrud\n\n# Accenture reimagines IT operations with agentic AI\n\n## The global consulting firm has built an AI-powered integration platform to support IT operations.\n\nAccenture\n\nOne of the challenges of IT operations at a global consulting firm like Accenture is it runs a huge and diverse portfolio of technologies to drive its digital core. Maintaining and operating that core requires continuously developing deep skillsets that are becoming rarer as every industry competes within a limited talent pool.\n\nAccenture’s answer is the Accenture Advanced Technology Agent (AATA), an agentic AI-powered integration platform that sits between the company’s human workforce and its technology platforms, and something that’s earned the company a 2025 CIO 100 Award in IT Excellence. [...] To smooth that process, AATA is an agnostic, open system with agentic architecture that enables IT teams and end users to interact with the core to resolve issues or deliver customer solutions without having to connect to an agent or file a single ticket.\n\nAccenture started working on AATA in 2023, starting with a conceptual architectural model. “Then we started to think about how people would consume the technology to derive value, so we wanted it to be largely democratized,” Courtney says. “You could have an open standards orchestration layer and a consistent prompt layer, but then enable teams to be able to either consume natively available agents, plug in existing automation or gen AI processes, or actually create custom agents if that was what required.” [...] Steven Courtney, MD of global IT, technology vision, and strategy, Accenture\n\nSteven Courtney, MD of global IT, technology vision, and strategy, Accenture\n\nAccenture\n\nSome specific focus areas for AATA included variations in AI maturity. Recognizing not every organization will be in the same place when it comes to AI, Accenture built AATA to have open architecture with the flexibility to swap models in and out, allowing it to stay model agnostic and keep pace with the market.",
      "score": 0.78650844,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://bankingblog.accenture.com/agentic-ai-future-of-work",
      "title": "Agentic AI and the future of work in financial services | Accenture Banking Blog",
      "content": "We developed AI agents to sit on top of the claims platform and orchestrate out to other systems, supporting the human claims handler. The AI agents support tasks such as information gathering, data quality, policy and coverage checks, policy detail extraction, claim data summarization (including the first notification of loss, customer and expert inputs, customer correspondence) and even some support to litigation, fraud and reserve management. [...] ### What is agentic architecture?\n\nAI agents are programs that handle tasks and workflows to achieve specific goals. Humans set the goals, but the agents operate more independently, adapting their strategies as needed to achieve the goal. They take inputs, reason, decide on tasks to perform, interact with other agents and tools, review their outcomes and determine the next steps required.\n\nThe agents ‘know’ a domain context in which they have been trained within an organization (e.g. on specific training data and documents). They have long-term memory and can learn from past interactions to optimize decision-making. [...] Through all these tasks the agents support the claims handler to be in charge as the ‘human in the lead’ — for instance, they can look back into source data easily. We have been able to free up 20% of their capacity, focusing their time on better decision-making (improving claims accuracy by 1%) and supporting negotiation in the most complex and valuable claims.\n\nWe’ve seen similar work from a US-based P&C insurer through to complex commercial claims operations.\n\n### The ‘so what’ for work and workforce\n\nImagine agentic AI as your co-worker, where you have team members dedicated to you who specialize in their support to you and can even bring perspectives and strengths complementary to your own.",
      "score": 0.73914057,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.accenture.com/us-en/services/cybersecurity/cyber-protection/secure-ai",
      "title": "Secure AI and Agentic AI - Accenture",
      "content": "#### Agentic AI\n\nAccenture's AI Refinery platform and a scalable agentic architecture can help organizations build and maintain secure AI agents, ensuring they operate reliably and efficiently.\n\n## Reinvention in action\n\nslideText 1  ofText  / 1\n\n### Essentials for securing AI agents in the cloud\n\nAI agents are reshaping cloud workflows, but cybersecurity must evolve too. Accenture’s Zero Trust Model offers a proactive blueprint to secure AI-powered environments.\n\nRead more\n\n### Redefining Resilience in the generative AI era\n\nFind out how to safeguard against gen AI-powered attacks and protect your AI environments.\n\nRead more\n\n### Empowering a secure autonomous AI future [...] Generative AI and Agentic AI have introduced a new era of enterprise intelligence that organizations must embrace, or risk falling behind. However, it also elevates cybersecurity risks and introduces new vulnerabilities, which requires embedding security and trust by design into any AI program. And while most CEOs see cybersecurity as a major consideration for gen AI, only 6% feel they have a robust responsible AI foundation. Accenture can help close that gap and establish resiliency across your AI lifecycle.\n\n### How we help\n\n#### Govern AI usage\n\nImplement AI security governance frameworks, establish consistent policies and training for AI usage, and design and implement review and reporting processes.\n\n#### Protect AI environments [...] ### Sean Kelly\n\nManaging Director, Global Secure AI Lead\n\n### Ryan Leininger\n\nManaging Director – Accenture Cybersecurity, Global AI Resilience Lead\n\nLinkedIn\n\n### Damon McDougald\n\nGlobal Cyber Protection Domain Lead\n\nLinkedIn\n\n## Start now\n\n### Want to explore your next steps in securing AI at scale? Reach out today\n\nContact us",
      "score": 0.7190094,

    },

      "content": "#### Agentic AI\n\nAccenture's AI Refinery platform and a scalable agentic architecture can help organizations build and maintain secure AI agents, ensuring they operate reliably and efficiently.\n\n## Reinvention in action\n\nslideText 1  ofText  / 1\n\n### Essentials for securing AI agents in the cloud\n\nAI agents are reshaping cloud workflows, but cybersecurity must evolve too.       "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/alliances/dell-technology-solutions-hybrid-cloud-and-ai.html",
      "title": "Dell Technologies Solutions: Hybrid Infrastructure and AI | Deloitte US",
      "content": "#### Agentic AI\n\n### Agentic AI\n\nDeploy purpose-built AI within your private Dell infrastructure. With a multi-agent AI platform from Deloitte layered on your Dell infrastructure, you can move beyond individual tasks to automate complex, end-to-end workflows. Define, run, and monitor autonomous agents with human-in-the-loop oversight, leveraging our asset accelerator to keep AI systems operating safely and effectively.\n\n#### Hybrid cloud infrastructure\n\n### Hybrid cloud infrastructure\n\nScale with confidence from on-premises to the edge and into cloud. Deloitte’s hybrid infrastructure approach tailors workload requirements to data consumption patterns. By balancing security and scalability across compute, storage, and network, our solutions provide flexibility without sacrificing control. [...] #### AI factory – Silicon2Service\n\n### AI factory – Silicon2Service\n\nDeploy AI faster with an end-to-end infrastructure and services platform. Silicon2Service is purpose-built to accelerate adoption, scale intelligently and deliver measurable business impact, not just technical capability.\n\nDeploy AI faster with an end-to-end infrastructure and services platform. Silicon2Service is purpose-built to accelerate adoption, scale intelligently and deliver measurable business impact, not just technical capability.\n\n#### AI factory for public sector\n\n### AI factory for public sector\n\nThis mission-ready AI infrastructure designed specifically for the public sector helps accelerate AI implementation while meeting security, compliance and sovereignty requirements.\n\n#### Agentic AI\n\n### Agentic AI [...] ## Cloud agility. On-premise control. One hybrid platform.\n\nAs infrastructure demands change, many organizations are moving beyond legacy environments to hybrid approaches, combining public-cloud agility with on-premises control to unlock new digital capabilities and enterprise AI. Hybrid cloud infrastructure solutions from Deloitte and Dell Technologies (Dell) can help modernize core IT, strengthen security, accelerate transformation and improve cost discipline—without adding complexity.\n\n### Multiplying AI impact, together\n\n#### Whether you're modernizing legacy systems, deploying AI at scale, bringing intelligence to the edge, or orchestrating agentic AI, our solutions meet you where you are and scale with where you're headed.\n\n#### AI factory – Silicon2Service",

    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/alliances/events/dell-technologies-world",
      "title": "Dell Technologies World 2026 | Deloitte US",
      "content": "#### Beyond the AI factory: Activating agentic systems\n\n##### Tuesday, May 19 | 11:20-11:45 a.m.  PT | Premium Expo Theater\n\nOnce an AI factory is in place, true enterprise value is realized through agentic AI where intelligently designed and orchestrated multi-agent systems automate complex workflows and drive real-time decisions at scale. Deloitte’s Multi-Agent System (MAS) platform helps organizations build, connect, and govern AI agents with full observability and human-in-the-loop oversight. See it in action through our flagship Agentic Clinic of the Future joint offering with Dell Technologies where air-gapped, edge-based AI agents are helping to reimagine patient care delivery. The architecture is repeatable, secure, and built for any enterprise ready to scale AI in production. [...] ##### Monday, May 18 | 3-4 p.m. PT | Delfino 4105\n\nCost, security, and talent shouldn't stall your AI ambitions—and neither should stack complexity. Deloitte and Dell are working to demystify private AI by grounding their joint AI Factory reference architecture in experience to show enterprises how to recreate a secure, cloud-like experience on private infrastructure, as part of Deloitte's Silicon2Service offering. But deployment is only the beginning. Operating AI at scale demands full-stack observability: connecting applications, models, data and compute to enable sustained performance and utilization. Come learn how to implement and operate private AI with confidence.\n\n#### Beyond the AI factory: Activating agentic systems [...] While many organizations have succeeded in AI pilots, scaling those initiatives into enterprise-wide value remains a defining challenge. Deloitte helps bridge the gap between AI strategy and execution through the Deloitte Ascend™ platform, combining demonstrated solutions with deep industry experience to drive measurable outcomes. Central to this approach is the Deloitte Enterprise AI Navigator, built on Deloitte Ascend™ on Dell. By integrating financial impact, workflow implications, and technology readiness into a unified view, it equips leaders with the clarity needed to make confident AI investment decisions, translating them into quantifiable time and cost savings. Join us to learn how this end-to-end approach can help you accelerate your AI journey and turn enterprise-wide",

      "content": "Recruiting for this role ends on May 31, 2026\n\nWork You’ll Do \n\nOur ServiceNow practice is expanding its AI and automation capabilities following ServiceNow’s acquisition of Moveworks, ushering in a new era of agentic AI-driven enterprise workflows.     {
      "url": "https://news.sap.com/poland/2026/04/ai-juz-teraz-zmienia-sposob-funkcjonowania-sektora-ochrony-zdrowia-wspolpraca-sap-i-haleon-inc/",
      "title": "AI już teraz zmienia sposób funkcjonowania sektora ochrony zdrowia. Współpraca SAP i Haleon Inc. - SAP Poland Centrum Wiadomości",
      "content": "„Raport Deloitte pokazuje, że technologia AI jest dziś w ochronie zdrowia postrzegana przede wszystkim jako narzędzie do obniżania kosztów. 64% badanych managerów uważa, że oszczędności można uzyskać dzięki automatyzacji i standaryzacji procesów, 55% podkreśla możliwość lepszego planowania pracy personelu za sprawą wykorzystywania analiz predykcyjnych, a 49% respondentów zauważa spadek kosztów dzięki wdrożeniu cyfrowego kontaktu z pacjentami i zdalnemu monitorowaniu ich stanu zdrowia. Co ciekawe, w badaniu widać różnicę w podejściu między USA a innymi rynkami. Poza Stanami Zjednoczonymi 38% menedżerów zapowiada większy nacisk na profilaktykę i wczesne wykrywanie chorób, podczas gdy w USA mówi o tym tylko 7%. Widać więc, że europejski rynek rynek reaguje na zmiany demograficzne i starzenie [...] Czytając najnowsze wnioski z Raportu Deloitte dotyczące sektora ochrony zdrowia, można odnieść wrażenie, że branża znalazła się w tym samym położeniu, w którym tkwi wiele innych – pomiędzy fascynacją nową technologią a twardą rzeczywistością operacyjną. Koszty rosną szybciej niż przychody, brakuje rąk do pracy, procesy odstają od wymagań, a systemy zamiast upraszczać, jedynie komplikują. W tych warunkach 64% badanych managerów uważa, że oszczędności można uzyskać dzięki automatyzacji i standaryzacji procesów, 55% podkreśla możliwość lepszego planowania pracy personelu za sprawą wykorzystywania analiz predykcyjnych, a 49% respondentów zauważa spadek kosztów dzięki wdrożeniu cyfrowego kontaktu z pacjentami i zdalnemu monitorowaniu ich stanu zdrowia. Co ciekawe, w badaniu widać różnicę w [...] Haleon planuje wdrożyć aplikacje SAP z wbudowaną sztuczną inteligencją oraz uruchomić rozwiązania chmurowe, aby harmonizować dane z oprogramowania i źródeł zewnętrznych w ramach jednego, nadzorowanego i wiarygodnego źródła danych. Ta połączona platforma będzie wspierać innowacje wspomagane przez AI oraz rozwój agentic AI, czyli modeli, w których agenci AI mogą wcześniej identyfikować problemy i rekomendować działania w kluczowych procesach biznesowych. Program transformacji cyfrowej rozwija wieloletnią współpracę obu firm, a przejście do nowej platformy ma rozpocząć się jeszcze w tym roku.\n\n## Więcej wKlient\n\nFC Bayern celuje w chmurę w ramach RISE with SAP\n\n### FC Bayern celuje w chmurę w ramach RISE with SAP\n\nCyfrowa metamorfoza Douglas w oparciu o SAP Customer Experience",

      "title": "Sales and Commercial Transformation Solution | Deloitte US",
      "content": "New sources of revenue, diverse channels, and shifting retail P&L will require new skills, metrics of success, and cross-functional coordination across CPG commercial teams.\n\nOur team can help you:\n\n### Our thinking\n\n### Winning with retail\n\n### 2026 Consumer Products Industry Outlook\n\n### 2026 CAGNY conference highlights\n\n### The value-seeking consumer\n\n### Agentic commerce: AI shopping agents guide 2025\n\n### The future of the consumer packaged goods industry\n\n### Revenue growth management in CPG\n\n### Unlocking GenAI value in retail [...] ### Converge Consumer industry solutions\n\n### Enhance decision-making where it matters most\n\nTraditional approaches to decision-making can’t keep up with consumer expectations. Converge by Deloitte for Consumer combines our experience delivering complex industry solutions with our data, analytics and AI products, helping organizations identify growth opportunities and realize value faster at scale.\n\n### \n\n### Connect with us\n\nDrew Gaputis\n\n#### Drew Gaputis\n\n### Let's connect\n\n#### What we do\n\n#### Our thinking\n\n#### Careers\n\n##### Follow us\n\n© 2026. See Terms of Use for more information. [...] Our team can help you:\n\n Establish advanced capabilities associated with data, automation, and AI to gain deeper category, shopper, and retailer insights.\n Utilize predictive signals to detect purchase behavior and forecast near- and long-term trends.\n Understand motivations and defining characteristics of retail shoppers to deliver co-created innovation.\n\nCPGs need to stay ahead of increasingly robust retailer data and insights—which includes developing unique category knowledge, proactive innovation, product co-creation, and compelling brand storytelling.\n\nOur team can help you:\n\n#### Evolved channel strategy\n\nChannel proliferation, declining store traffic, and margin pressure will require CPGs to reexamine their traditional route to market.\n\n### Evolved channel strategy",

      "title": "Creating an exciting, customer-centric vision | CIO",
      "content": "## More from this author\n\n### Turning urgency into opportunity: The CIO as a compass in an AI-first world\n\n## Show me more\n\n### Creating an exciting, customer-centric vision\n\nImage\n\n### SAP 2027 deadline for S/4HANA out of reach for most customers\n\nImage\n\n### SAS makes AI governance the centerpiece of its agent strategy\n\nImage\n\n### From Blueprint to Bytecode - SP Setia's Alex Chi on Building Malaysia's Most Ambitious Property Tech Agenda\n\nImage\n\n### Building for Complexity - Azimut's Blueprint for Wealth Tech in Southeast Asia\n\nImage\n\n### Powering What Can’t Fail-Scaling Digital at Yinson Holdings\n\nImage\n\n### From Blueprint to Bytecode - SP Setia's Alex Chi on Building Malaysia's Most Ambitious Property Tech Agenda\n\nImage\n\n### How Chaos accelerates 3D visualization workflows with AI [...] 4. What are our results?\n\n 5. What is our plan?\n\nOf these five, I believe the most important is question 2: Who are our customers? It is important to note that the term customers here refers to the people we need to satisfy in general. This includes not only direct customers who purchase or use our products and services, but also customers as partners, such as staff members who support our organizational activities and external partner companies.\n\nSo, who are the customers for the IT department? In addition to management, business departments, employees and group companies that receive IT systems and services, this also includes supervisors, subordinates, colleagues and partner companies who work together in the course of our activities. [...] People are not motivated by mere logic that simply explains what is correct or rational. It is only when accompanied by the emotion of I want to try this or I want to see that future that they are convinced and take proactive action.",

      "url": "https://kpmg.com/us/en/webcasts/2026/kpmg-sifma-ops.html",
      "title": "KPMG at SIFMA Ops 2026",
      "content": "Stay ahead of the latest changes impacting the capital markets and wealth management industry, including regulatory shifts and the transformative power of Agentic AI.\n\nAgentic AI is changing wealth management\n\nAgentic AI transforms wealth management by automating tasks, enhancing client experiences and driving growth through continuous optimization.\n\nDemocratizing private markets\n\nExpanding retail access to alternative investments\n\nOrchestrating AI‑driven transformation with managed services\n\nThe 2026 KPMG Managed Services Outlook highlights a major shift in managed services industry trends in the US. The report shows how AI‑enabled managed services are driving transformation by helping organizations orchestrate complexity, manage risk, and move faster.\n\n## Connect with us\n\n### [...] ### AI Governance – Risk, Controls, and Compliance\n\nTuesday, May 12, 2:30 PM ET\n\nAs firms increasingly integrate artificial intelligence into core business and operational processes, establishing effective governance frameworks is becoming essential. This session will examine how organizations are managing the risks associated with AI adoption while ensuring appropriate controls, transparency, and regulatory compliance.\n\nPanelists\n\nBassam Khattab, Partner, Advisory, Managed Services US Leader, KPMG\n\nKevin Ehrlich, Managing Director and Associate General Counsel, Asset Management Group, SIFMA\n\nDuncan Robinson, Director of AI & Optimization, Franklin Templeton\n\nModerator\n\nMelissa MacGregor, Managing Director, Deputy General Counsel & Corporate Secretary, SIFMA [...] # KPMG at SIFMA Ops 2026\n\n## Let’s connect at SIFMA Ops!\n\nKPMG is a proud strategic partner of SIFMA and sponsor of the SIFMA Operations Conference & Exhibition 2026, where capital markets operations, technology, and regulatory leaders gather to explore the most critical priorities shaping operational excellence, resilience, and innovation across the financial services ecosystem.\n\n## Be part of something bigger—Run for a Purpose\n\nTuesday, May 12 | 6:30 AM\n\nKick off SIFMA Ops with an early morning 5K run or walk that brings the community together in support of Naples Children Foundation. Proudly sponsored by KPMG, this event turns miles into meaningful impact for vulnerable children. Register through SIFMA.\n\n## KPMG breakout sessions\n\n### AI Governance – Risk, Controls, and Compliance",
      "score": 0.6255073,

    },
    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/nl/en/home/insights/2026/04/strengthening-ai-performance-and-governance-within-a-government-organisation.html",
      "title": "AI performance and governance in government - KPMG Netherlands",
      "content": "In parallel, a targeted compliance review was undertaken. This not only evaluated transparency, explainability and risk management, but also informed the further design of governance structures and control mechanisms. Based on these insights, a strategic roadmap was developed for AI governance and for embedding AI within automation processes.\n\nCore processes were redesigned and targeted technological optimisations were implemented to improve performance and mitigate risk. In addition, the potential application of advanced technologies, including Agentic AI, was explored within clearly defined frameworks and governance arrangements, ensuring responsible deployment in support of advisory and decision-making processes.\n\n## The result [...] The organisation aimed to improve the efficiency of its AI systems, strengthen governance and operate in demonstrable compliance. The central question was therefore twofold. How can AI performance be enhanced, and how can those improvements be embedded sustainably and responsibly within the organisation?\n\n## The approach\n\nKPMG conducted a comprehensive gap analysis at technical, organisational and governance levels. Existing AI systems, the underlying infrastructure, processes and accountabilities were mapped and assessed holistically. [...] ## The team that made the difference\n\nA multidisciplinary AI Factory team from KPMG Netherlands, bringing together AI & Data specialists and Digital Process Excellence experts, supported the ministry in optimising AI-enabled processes and strengthening AI governance, delivering measurable operational improvements.\n\n## Get in touch with us\n\nJoris Juttmann\nBart Veenman\nartificial intelligence\nartificial intelligence\n\nBuilding a trustworthy foundation to harness the power of artificial intelligence.\n\nBuilding a trustworthy foundation to harness the power of artificial intelligence.\n\nartificial intelligence\n\nDiscover working at KPMG\n\nDiscover working at KPMG\n\n## Request for proposal\n\n## Subscription\n\n## Subscription",

      "content": "Introduction Acknowledgements Foreword Principle 4 Principle 2 Methodology Principle 1 Principle 5 Conclusion Principle 3 Principle 3 Workforce transformation and human accountability The company board expects that management proactively develops clear guiding principles for effective AI adoption in the organization. It considers how the AI adoption strategy affects the company’s human capital resources. This includes the future role of humans vs. AI as well as the required capability building within the workforce. Defined and safeguarded quality of (human) decision-making 3.1 Strategic workforce transformation and upskilling 3.2 Appropriate long-term talent pipeline 3.3 © 2026 Copyright owned by one or more of the KPMG International entities. All rights reserved.\nForeword This technology brings boards closer to decision terrains they have historically approached with caution or distance. Directors are faced with overseeing strategies for technology architectures, workforce transformation, data usage, and ethical guardrails, knowing that decisions companies make now will shape business models, risk exposure, organizational culture, and long-term resilience. Those decisions will determine whether an organization thrives in the new AI world or becomes irrelevant. [...] KPMG.     {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/in/en/insights/2026/04/kpmg-managed-services-outlook-survey-2026.html",
      "title": "KPMG Managed Services Outlook Survey 2026",
      "content": "KPMG in India logo\n\nTransformation\n\nArtificial Intelligence\n\nImpact\n\nMarket Engagement\n\n#### \n\n###### \n\nYour search term was too short.\n\nSearch terms must include 3 or more characters.\n\n# KPMG Managed Services Outlook Survey 2026\n\nExplore how modern businesses use managed services to fast-track their AI adoption\n\n## Accelerate AI with managed services\n\nTo bridge the gap between innovation and execution, companies are turning to managed services to quickly unlock the value of artificial intelligence (AI).\n\nGoing beyond simple cost-cutting, the model is helping leaders bypass tech debt and talent gaps to scale AI for digital transformation.       "content": "### 10 top priorities for CIOs in 2026\n\nA new year creates fresh opportunities — and challenges. Here are key IT priorities that will help your organization thrive this year.\n\n### 8 ways agentic AI will transform IT operations\n\nAgentic AI’s reasoning and planning capabilities can help supercharge your IT org by making decisions and handling tasks with little or no human intervention.\n\n### 7 cost optimization pitfalls to avoid\n\nBusinesses are moving away from traditional cost-cutting to embrace growth-oriented cost optimization. That requires CIOs to be even more strategic and business-aligned with their IT budget decisions.\n\n### 7 standout CIO skills every company wants [...] Image\n\n### Your AI coding agent isn’t a tool. It’s a junior developer. Treat it like one\n\nImage\n\n### Building for Complexity - Azimut's Blueprint for Wealth Tech in Southeast Asia\n\nImage\n\n### Powering What Can’t Fail-Scaling Digital at Yinson Holdings\n\nImage\n\n### Episode 5: The CIO’s view on starting, and restarting, AI initiatives\n\nImage\n\n### Building for Complexity - Azimut's Blueprint for Wealth Tech in Southeast Asia\n\nImage\n\n### CData powers AI agents with secure access to enterprise data\n\nImage\n\n### Powering What Can’t Fail-Scaling Digital at Yinson Holdings\n\nImage\n\n## From our editors straight to your inbox\n\nSponsored Links\n\n### About\n\n### Policies\n\n### More\n\n### Our Network [...] ### 7 standout CIO skills every company wants\n\nCIOs are expected to possess a wide range of technical, business, and management skills. But by mastering these exemplary IT leadership skills, you will set yourself apart.\n\n### 8 ways CIOs undermine their enterprise influence\n\nStrong influence across the company and leadership ranks takes years to build. It can vanish in the blink of an eye.\n\n### 6 signs of a dying digital transformation\n\nYour initiative launched with great expectations. Now it’s falling apart in front of your eyes. What should you do next?\n\n## Show me more\n\n### How the EU’s NIS2 directive is changing how CIOs think about digital infrastructure\n\nImage\n\n### Data debt: AI’s value killer hidden in plain sigh\n\nImage",
      "score": 0.20429464,

    {
      "url": "https://www.cio.com/article/4164622/enterprises-still-chase-incremental-not-transformational-ai-gains.html",
      "title": "Enterprises still chase incremental, not transformational, AI gains - CIO",
      "content": "Grant Gross, a senior writer at CIO, is a long-time IT journalist who has focused on AI, enterprise technology, and tech policy. He previously served as Washington, D.C., correspondent and later senior editor at IDG News Service. Earlier in his career, he was managing editor at Linux.com and news editor at tech careers site Techies.com. As a tech policy expert, he has appeared on C-SPAN and the giant NTN24 Spanish-language cable news network. In the distant past, he worked as a reporter and editor at newspapers in Minnesota and the Dakotas. A finalist for Best Range of Work by a Single Author for both the Eddie Awards and the Neal Awards, Grant was recently recognized with an ASBPE Regional Silver award for his article “Agentic AI: Decisive, operational AI arrives in business.” [...] Organizations need to take an AI-centric approach to all their workflows and try to redesign processes to remove repetitive human work as much as possible, Flynn recommends. Organizations should then add in human intervention when it’s needed, he says.\n\n“If you think about bolting AI onto your business problem, as you continue to add AI, the amount of effort that’s going on continues to increase, versus redesigning your processes such that AI is built into your process,” he adds.\n\nCreating an enduring AI strategy goes beyond rolling out a few AI tools for employees, Flynn says, adding that EY runs clients through an AI value blueprint that takes them through potential outcomes of various AI strategies. [...] Meanwhile, AI productivity improvements often depend on job cuts on the other side of deployment, he adds.\n\n“The problem with incremental productivity improvements is that in order to realize the benefits that your CFO demands, you have to implement a solution, prove that it works, and then you have to fire people,” Hopkins says. “Do you think those people who will be let go are going to help you do that? They’re not. It’s messy, ugly work.”\n\nThe Forrester data echoes another recent survey from AI agent platform vendor Decidr, which found that 40% of US businesses are getting the bulk of their AI value from ChatGPT-style tools, instead of agents or custom AI models.\n\n## Old ways of thinking",
      "score": 0.16450334,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.cio.com/article/4164484/creating-an-exciting-customer-centric-vision.html",
      "title": "Creating an exciting, customer-centric vision - CIO",
      "content": "## More from this author\n\n### Turning urgency into opportunity: The CIO as a compass in an AI-first world\n\n## Show me more\n\n### Creating an exciting, customer-centric vision\n\nImage\n\n### SAP 2027 deadline for S/4HANA out of reach for most customers\n\nImage\n\n### SAS makes AI governance the centerpiece of its agent strategy\n\nImage\n\n### From Blueprint to Bytecode - SP Setia's Alex Chi on Building Malaysia's Most Ambitious Property Tech Agenda\n\nImage\n\n### Building for Complexity - Azimut's Blueprint for Wealth Tech in Southeast Asia\n\nImage\n\n### Powering What Can’t Fail-Scaling Digital at Yinson Holdings\n\nImage\n\n### From Blueprint to Bytecode - SP Setia's Alex Chi on Building Malaysia's Most Ambitious Property Tech Agenda\n\nImage\n\n### How Chaos accelerates 3D visualization workflows with AI [...] 4. What are our results?\n\n 5. What is our plan?\n\nOf these five, I believe the most important is question 2: Who are our customers? It is important to note that the term customers here refers to the people we need to satisfy in general. This includes not only direct customers who purchase or use our products and services, but also customers as partners, such as staff members who support our organizational activities and external partner companies.\n\nSo, who are the customers for the IT department? In addition to management, business departments, employees and group companies that receive IT systems and services, this also includes supervisors, subordinates, colleagues and partner companies who work together in the course of our activities. [...] Whether or not the vision articulated by the CIO conveys a sense of excitement not only increases the buy-in of every individual in the organization but also significantly influences the organization’s driving force and the speed at which the vision is realized. The excitement I’m referring to here does not mean flashy rhetoric or pie-in-the-sky fantasies. Personally, I believe that excitement is the driving force that creates the future and the bridge to it. It is a vision of the future that every single employee can imagine as something that personally concerns them — thinking, if we move in this direction, we’ll be able to do better work than we do now, or we’ll be able to feel that our work is truly benefiting our customers and society.",
      "score": 0.038441,
      "raw_content": null
    }

    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/gx/en/services/alliances/microsoft/agentic-ai.html",
      "title": "Agentic AI business acceleration | PwC",
      "content": "## PwC’s agentic AI solutions built on Microsoft\n\nPwC and Microsoft providing businesses with flexible, high-impact AI solutions tailored to numerous use cases across a wide range of business functions. These include sales, HR, procurement, IT and inventory management—and much more. Agentic AI offers almost endless possibilities, from improving customer engagement to increasing sales conversions.\n\n## The future of work – a secure and responsible business-led transformation [...] Deliver personalised, real-time solutions powered by AI agents, transforming customer interactions and driving customer loyalty. AI agents can help businesses solve problems more quickly, transform the customer experience and bring new products to market faster.\n\n### Empowering the workforce with AI-driven productivity\n\nEnhance productivity and innovation with AI tools, automate repetitive tasks and upskill employees to use AI effectively so they can focus on strategic work.\n\nEnhance productivity and innovation with AI tools, automate repetitive tasks and upskill employees to use AI effectively so they can focus on strategic work.\n\n## PwC’s agentic AI solutions built on Microsoft [...] While AI agents provide significant autonomy, an effective model emphasises collaboration and dynamic oversight. Humans and AI increasingly work together, each harnessing their distinctive strengths. Implementing responsible AI necessitates a sophisticated approach to control. Human judgment is essential for responsible decision making, strategic guidance and ensuring alignment with human values.\n\nThe principle of human leadership can guide the development of clear protocols that delineate the boundaries of AI autonomy and facilitate appropriate human intervention. And with PwC’s investment in AI capabilities—and an industry-leading relationship with Microsoft—we can help you develop a secure and responsible business-led transformation, powered by AI agents.",
      "score": 0.8396422,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/gx/en/services/alliances/oracle/agentic-ai-for-oracle-fusion.html",
      "title": "Agentic AI for Oracle Fusion Cloud | PwC Global",
      "content": "For example, in Human Capital Management (HCM), AI-driven workforce planning agents can analyse historical employee data, predict hiring needs, and automate recruitment workflows. These agents can also assist in employee engagement by monitoring sentiment analysis from internal surveys and providing recommendations for improving workplace satisfaction and retention. According to the same PwC survey, more than half (56%) of CEOs interviewed said that GenAI has resulted in efficiencies in how employees use their time. [...] Key differentiators:\n\nIf you are an existing Oracle ERP user, the next step is to unlock and activate the power of AI agents. In particular, for Oracle Human Capital Management (HCM), AI can automate key processes such as recruitment, workforce planning, and employee engagement. Additionally, AI features in recruitment can lead to significant improvement in time to hire. AI-driven assistants can analyse workforce trends, predict attrition risks, and recommend personalised career development opportunities, ultimately improving employee satisfaction and retention.\n\nPwC can guide you through this journey, helping you leverage AI to enhance efficiency, automate processes, and gain deeper business insights. [...] ## PwC agentic AI use cases for Oracle Fusion Cloud\n\nOracle delivers AI agents built for business, helping you make better decisions faster and empowering your workforce to do more. PwC is at the forefront of this evolution, building agentic frameworks that can help transform workflows and decision-making across the enterprise. Our team of solution architects works with clients to develop solutions and services designed to address specific business problems or opportunities that drive measurable business value for enterprise leaders.\n\nUnlike generic AI solutions, PwC’s industry-focused methodologies allow us to implement tools that align with unique operational demands, helping businesses unlock efficiencies and achieve targeted outcomes.",
      "score": 0.82728493,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/tech-effect/ai-analytics/agentic-ai-workforce-redesign.html",
      "title": "Agentic AI workforce redesign: PwC",
      "content": "AI gives HR the opportunity to move beyond process management and become a strategic driver of workforce performance. With the right design, HR can shape how work gets done, how capabilities grow, and how the organization adapts to change. Here’s what CHROs should know:\n\nThe HR business leader can become a business strategist. As new HR generalist roles—like HR operations leads and talent strategists—direct AI agents to handle routine tasks, HRBPs can focus on business outcomes. They can, for example, create bespoke, x-capability focused pods and upskill these pods not by position, but as a team. [...] AI is transforming marketing into a proactive driver of growth. With AI agents handling tasks across content, campaigns, planning, and analytics, marketers can focus on creativity, strategy, and customer connection. In this new model, humans can bring empathy and brand insight while AI delivers precision and scale. Together, they can create faster, more personalized, and more effective experiences with insights and optimization opportunities identified proactively rather than reactively. Here’s what CMOs should know:\n\n### AI-enabled HR: Building a more capable and nimbler workforce [...] The rise of AI-enabled generalists is already changing how core business functions operate. In finance, IT, marketing, and HR, we’re seeing real examples of how agentic workflows are expanding roles, elevating responsibilities, and helping teams focus on outcomes instead of tasks. These changes aren’t uniform. Each function is evolving in its own way, and leading organizations are designing roles and structures to keep pace.\n\n### AI-enabled finance: Becoming an engine for growth",
      "score": 0.8091708,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://jobs.us.pwc.com/job/new-york/agentic-ai-and-contact-center-ai-architect/932/86711036816",
      "title": "Agentic AI & Contact Center AI Architect at PwC",
      "content": "Title: Agentic AI & Contact Center AI Architect at PwC\n# Agentic AI & Contact Center AI Architect. **Job Category** Data and Analytics **Level** Senior Manager **Specialty/Competency** Data, Analytics & AI **Time Type** Full time **Travel Requirements** Up to 60% **Job ID** 669836WD **Location(s)** FL-Tampa, IL-Chicago, DC-Washington, TX-Dallas, CA-Los Angeles, MD-Baltimore, MN-Minneapolis, CO-Denver, MA-Boston, NY-New York, NJ-Florham Park, CA-San Diego, CA-San Francisco, CA-Silicon Valley, PA-Philadelphia, WA-Seattle, PA-Pittsburgh, OR-Portland, TX-Houston. At PwC, our people in data and analytics focus on leveraging data to drive insights and make informed business decisions. In data analysis at PwC, you will focus on utilising advanced analytical techniques to extract insights from large datasets and drive data-driven decision-making. As part of the Data and Analytics team you will lead the architecture design and development of innovative Customer Interaction and Contact Center AI solutions. As a Senior Manager you will serve as a strategic advisor, leveraging your knowledge to guide cross-functional teams through every project phase, driving project success. This role offers the chance to shape proposals, lead pursuits, and contribute thought leadership while interacting with clients at a senior level. - Lead the design and implementation of innovative Customer Interaction and Contact Center AI solutions. - Leading design and development of AI solutions. - Leading teams to create end-to-end solutions for customer journey from intent recognition through resolution, across IVR and Agent tools. - Improving customer engagement and satisfaction through Conversational AI and Agentic AI solutions. - Mentoring and guiding team members in AI and technology leading practices. - Designing and delivering agentic AI products, applications, and solutions tailored to business needs, while providing implementation guidance throughout execution. PwC does not intend to hire experienced or entry level job seekers who will need, now or in the future, PwC sponsorship through the H-1B lottery, except as set forth within the following policy: https://pwc.to/H-1B-Lottery-Policy. At PwC, we recognize that conviction records may have a direct, adverse, and negative relationship to responsibilities such as accessing sensitive company or customer information, handling proprietary assets, or collaborating closely with team members. PwC currently is not hiring experienced or entry level job seekers who will need, now or in the future, PwC sponsorship through the H-1B lottery, except as set forth here.",

    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/gr/en/technology/artificial-intelligence/agentic-ai-financial-services.html",
      "title": "Agentic AI in Financial Services | PwC",
      "content": "Agentic AI enables FS firms to automate decision‑heavy processes end‑to‑end, improving compliance, speed, accuracy and customer outcomes across the value chain.\n\nAchieving this transformation requires a clear, actionable roadmap built on a responsible vision, AI fluency, modernised data platforms, value chain operating models and strong governance to scale agentic AI enterprise wide. [...] ## Business value across financial services\n\nBy taking over complex workflows from start to finish, agentic AI reduces cycle times from days to minutes, minimises manual effort and errors, and enables specialists to focus on activities that require more judgement. It also embeds controls and audit trails directly into operations, helping institutions to stay compliant and preserve critical expertise as their workforces age. [...] The impact of agentic AI is already visible across the industry. In banking, the most immediate value is emerging in financial crime, regulatory compliance, customer engagement and lending. Agentic AI is also having a significant impact in the insurance industry, transforming underwriting, claims, customer service and workforce sustainability. Capital markets organisations are leveraging agentic AI for high value analytical and operational activities like analysis and scenario modeling. And in private equity and investment operations, agents accelerate due diligence, surface operational risks across portfolios and enhance client advisory with real-time, personalised insights.",
      "score": 0.7935067,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.strategyand.pwc.com/de/en/functions/organisational-strategy/agentic-ai-in-hr.html",
      "title": "Agentic AI in HR | Strategy&",
      "content": "Sources: Gartner (Agentic AI in HR), PwC (AI Agent Survey May 2025), PwC (Global Artificial Intelligence Study 2023), Strategy& analysis\n\nThe outcome is predictable: stalled pilots and difficulty scaling beyond isolated lighthouse cases. Our research shows that most companies ask the wrong question: “Which agent should we implement first?” The right question should be: “Which processes consume time, drive cost, or constrain performance?”\n\nTherefore, we suggest answering three key questions early on to avoid the most frequent pitfalls.\n\n 1. Where are true potentials\n 2. How does agentic AI compare\n 3. How to implement agentic AI\n\n### Where are the true high-potential areas for agentic AI, and how will you assess them? [...] Click to increase view\n\n Activate to open full-size image in lightbox overlay\n\nThe focus on automation, AI and digital spend shows that the pivot to AI is already underway, with the center of gravity shifting from on-demand content generation to autonomy and orchestration directly serving business goals, or as we call it: agentification of processes.\n\n## What is agentic AI, and why the hype?\n\nAgentic AI refers to intelligent software that autonomously makes decisions and acts upon it to achieve defined goals without continuous human input. It operates around the clock, learns from its environment, and adapts as conditions change. In HR, its power lies in how it combines three complementary capability levers:\n\n 1 [...] Up to 60% reduction in time-to-hire through autonomous resume screening and scheduling\n\n Around 45% decrease in payroll errors, improving compliance and employee satisfaction\n\n Approximately 30% improvement in talent retention predictions through AI-assisted analytics\n\n Up to 30% reduction in administrative HR costs and 200% faster response times, enhancing employee experience\n\nSources: PwCs Global People Process Framework Analysis (2025), Salesforce (2025), Moka (2025), Strategy& analysis\n\nStrategically, grounding decisions around individual agents in a clear business case and measurable KPIs is critical to assess and deliver on ROI promises.\n\n## Why implementations stall, and how to avoid common pitfalls",
      "score": 0.7898345,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/gx/en/services/alliances/microsoft/agentic-ai-financial-services.html",
      "title": "Agentic AI in Financial Services | PwC",
      "content": "## Business value across financial services\n\nBy taking over complex workflows from start to finish, agentic AI reduces cycle times from days to minutes, minimises manual effort and errors, and enables specialists to focus on activities that require more judgement. It also embeds controls and audit trails directly into operations, helping institutions to stay compliant and preserve critical expertise as their workforces age. [...] The impact of agentic AI is already visible across the industry. In banking, the most immediate value is emerging in financial crime, regulatory compliance, customer engagement and lending. Agentic AI is also having a significant impact in the insurance industry, transforming underwriting, claims, customer service and workforce sustainability. Capital markets organisations are leveraging agentic AI for high value analytical and operational activities like analysis and scenario modeling. And in private equity and investment operations, agents accelerate due diligence, surface operational risks across portfolios and enhance client advisory with real-time, personalised insights. [...] Against this backdrop, many financial institutions are turning to agentic AI to strengthen resilience, accelerate key processes, transform the customer experience, and unlock new opportunities for growth.\n\n## Explore the whitepaper here\n\n## What agentic AI enables\n\nAgentic AI represents a step change from traditional analytics, automation and generative AI. Rather than completing isolated tasks, agents can reason, act and orchestrate multistep processes with minimal human intervention—accessing data, executing workflows and adapting to new conditions. When deployed responsibly, this enables financial institutions to improve efficiency and accuracy while maintaining essential human oversight for high-stake decisions.\n\n## Business value across financial services",
      "score": 0.7701369,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/technology/alliances/library/salesforce-agentic-ai.html",
      "title": "Unleashing agentic AI with Salesforce and Google: PwC",
      "content": "Businesses are not only reimagining the future of work thanks to augmented intelligence — we have now reached a pivotal moment where human ingenuity and AI can empower each other with great synergy. PwC is helping clients deploy intelligent agents that work together — grounded in proprietary data, designed for real-world outcomes, and orchestrated across cloud environments. With the right strategy, companies can design, build and operate a digital workforce guided by human oversight, which helps keep Responsible AI principles at the helm. As we look ahead to Google Cloud Next, here’s how AI agents driven by three powerhouses — Salesforce’s Agentforce, Google’s Agentspace, and PwC’s agent OS — are converging to help clients lead. [...] Whether you’re using Salesforce Agentforce, Google Agentspace or a custom agent built on Google Vertex AI or another platform, PwC’s agent OS can help enhance governance and compliance through its integrated risk management and oversight frameworks, and support interoperability across your enterprise technology stack.\n\nIt’s the operating system layer that helps make AI agents work together. [...] One way PwC is leveraging Gemini, Agentforce and Agentspace is by connecting its agents to a broader ecosystem through its new operating system, PwC’s agent OS — the first business transformation-focused solution for orchestrating enterprise-grade AI agents across platforms like Salesforce, Google Cloud, Oracle, Workday and others. While you can create any agent in Google Agentspace or Salesforce Agentforce using Gemini models, PwC uses agent OS within of any cloud or large language model (LLM), to orchestrate an entire cross-platform workflow that is tailored to a client’s needs. Think of it as the switchboard behind intelligent agent deployments — enabling companies to connect home-grown agents, third-party SDKs, and platform-native agentic platform like Agentspace or Agentforce in a",
      "score": 0.7640656,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://siliconangle.com/2025/10/08/agentic-ai-enterprise-automation-googlecloudpartneraiseries",
      "title": "Agentic AI enterprise automation in focus for PwC - SiliconANGLE",
      "content": "In August, PwC announced the launch of more than 120 enterprise AI agents developed with Google Cloud. In an example of how AI agents have delivered real business impact, a call center client significantly reduced its 2,000-person operation by using AI to streamline workflows and boost efficiency, according to Agarwal.\n\nAnother compelling example involved a client with 200 paralegals translating state laws into plain English. By deploying AI agents to automate the task, the team was reduced to just 20 while maintaining accuracy and efficiency, according to Agarwal.\n\n“It was an even greater gain,” he said. “But all they were doing was something that with the right context, we were able to back-test. We were able to prove it’s safe.” [...] Siliconangle\n\nSiliconangle\n\nUPDATED 15:00 EDT / OCTOBER 08 2025\n\nAgentic AI enterprise automation was discussed by Clive D'Souza, director and head of partner engineering and Vikas Agarwal, chief technology and innovation officer of PwC Advisory at PwC US, during the Google Cloud - Google Cloud Partner AI Series event. AI\n\n### Beyond the PoC: AI agents put enterprise automation to the test\n\nby Ryan Stevens\n\nThe next wave of artificial intelligence is driving a major transformation in how organizations operate and create value. Across industries, businesses are embracing agentic AI enterprise automation and prioritizing cultural adaptation to successfully navigate the evolving AI landscape. [...] The past year has seen AI agents evolve from concept to reality, fueled by rapid advances in processing power and adoption. These systems are now delivering tangible returns and driving productivity, according to Clive D’Souza (pictured, right), director and head of partner engineering at Google Cloud.\n\nVikas Agarwal, chief technology and innovation officer for PwC Advisory at PwC U.S., and Clive D'Souza, director and head of partner engineering at Google Cloud discuss agentic AI enterprise automation with theCUBE at the Google Cloud Partner AI Series.\n\nPwC’s Vikas Agarwal and Google Cloud’s Clive D’Souza talk with theCUBE about agentic AI enterprise automation and partner ecosystems.",
      "score": 0.758958,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/technology/alliances/library/pwc-sf-agentic-contact-center.html",
      "title": "Agentic contact center on Salesforce Agentforce: PwC",
      "content": "# The Agentic Contact Center\n\nhero image\n\nThe future of service is agentic, and it’s redefining how enterprises operate. PwC’s Agentic Contact Center, built on Salesforce Agentforce, brings humans and AI agents together to deliver faster, smarter, and more personalized support at scale. While the contact center is the frontline, our approach extends across the service ecosystem — from HR and IT to Field Service — helping organizations embed intelligence and agility wherever service happens.\n\nPlayback of this video is not currently available\n\n#### PwC and Salesforce: Redefining Service with Agentic AI\n\n## Salesforce introduced Agentforce Contact Center to unify voice and digital channels with AI agents and CRM data. [...] Read the case study\n\n## Designed for measurable outcomes\n\nEach element of our model is built to deliver tangible results — from faster handle times and improved customer satisfaction and engagement to a more efficient, empowered workforce. While the metrics vary by organization, PwC’s experience across industries demonstrates the transformative impact of an agentic model: reduced operational friction, enhanced personalization, and greater adaptability and long-term service performance.\n\n## See what’s possible with Agentic AI [...] PwC helped a national healthcare provider reimagine patient service with AI-powered personalization, omnichannel coordination, and automation. The result? Better outcomes, lower costs, and a more seamless care experience.\n\nRead more\n\n### Rush University System for Health: Improving how patients access care, with help from AI\n\nRush University System for Health partnered with PwC to simplify how patients get the help they need. By using AI-enabled agents to handle routine inquiries, Rush reduced everyday call volume and helped patients get answers faster. The result created more capacity for staff to focus on high-touch support and the moments where human care makes the biggest difference.\n\nRead the case study\n\n## Designed for measurable outcomes",

    {
      "url": "https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/28/openai_climbs_into_amazons_bedrock/",
      "title": "OpenAI jumps out of Microsoft's bed, into Amazon's Bedrock",
      "content": "Alongside the managed inference service, OpenAI's models will also be made available on Amazon's Bedrock Managed Agents and AgentCore platforms, which provide tools and blueprints for building enterprise agents and connecting them to enterprise data and services. AWS also announced a whole host of new agentic AI tools for its own end-customers at the same time, including Quick, a personalized assistant akin to Microsoft Copilot but for apps from multiple vendors, and various new flavors of Connect, which was originally Amazon's hosted CRM product but is expanding to help customers automate tasks in HR, health care, and supply chain management. [...] Science28 Apr 2026 | 9\n\n#### Vintage chatbot lives in the past like an elderly relative\n\nTalkie's training data stops at the end of 1930, and its creators hope it'll help us better understand how AI thinks\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 | 2\n\n#### Resilient, continuously active data – with no compromise\n\nWhen the gap between data generation and action is a strategic liability, it's time for a fix\n\nSponsored Feature\n\n#### IBM's AI coding 'partner' Bob hits general availability\n\n80,000 internal guinea pigs, Bobcoins, mainframe dreams and a name that really should have raised more flags\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 | 7\n\n#### Amazon unveils a Copilot for all your apps\n\nRetailer touts 'teammates' and always-on context as it muscles into an already crowded enterprise market\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 | 1 [...] Amazon contends that enterprises want to build agents and other AI-augmented tools using OpenAI's models, but have been stopped by security policy, data privacy, and sovereignty concerns.\n\nBy opening its models up to a trusted third party, OpenAI can sidestep many of these concerns. Bringing its models to AWS also means that customers don't need to jump through nearly as many hoops to adopt its models, since Amazon has already put the effort into connecting its services to Bedrock.",
      "score": 0.26367334,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/28/ibms_ai_coding_partner_bob/",
      "title": "IBM's AI coding 'partner' Bob hits general availability - The Register",
      "content": "IBM said in a statement it's teams that used Bob saw an \"average of 45 percent productivity gains across complex, multi-step workflows.\"\n\nThis makes IBM just the latest company to lean on its own staff to \"prove\" the efficacy of it AI platforms. PwC and Accenture have both told staff progression depends on buying into their AI visions.\n\nAs for IBM, the firm says Bob uses a mix of frontier LLMs, open-source models, small language models (SLMs) and IBM’s Granite SLM family to automate and augment the full software development lifecycle, from discovery and planning to design, coding, and testing.\n\nIt claims to embed security directly in to workflows and catches \"new categories of risk that traditional controls were not designed to catch, from prompt injection to unintended data exposure.\" [...] ### More about\n\n AI\n Developer\n IBM\n\n### Narrower topics\n\n AIOps\n AIX\n API\n DeepSeek\n Gemini\n Git\n Google AI\n GPT-3\n GPT-4\n IBM Power\n IBM Watson\n IBM Z\n i OS\n Large Language Model\n Machine Learning\n MCubed\n Neural Networks\n NLP\n OS/2\n Programming Language\n Retrieval Augmented Generation\n Software bug\n Star Wars\n Tensor Processing Unit\n TOPS\n\n### Broader topics\n\n Self-driving Car\n\n#### More about\n\n6 comment bubble on white COMMENTS\n\n#### More about\n\n AI\n Developer\n IBM\n\nMore like these\n\n×\n\n### More about\n\n AI\n Developer\n IBM\n\n### Narrower topics [...] ### More about\n\n AI\n Developer\n IBM\n\n### Narrower topics\n\n AIOps\n AIX\n API\n DeepSeek\n Gemini\n Git\n Google AI\n GPT-3\n GPT-4\n IBM Power\n IBM Watson\n IBM Z\n i OS\n Large Language Model\n Machine Learning\n MCubed\n Neural Networks\n NLP\n OS/2\n Programming Language\n Retrieval Augmented Generation\n Software bug\n Star Wars\n Tensor Processing Unit\n TOPS\n\n### Broader topics\n\n Self-driving Car\n\n#### TIP US OFF\n\nSend us news\n\n### Other stories you might like\n\n#### Don't pay Vect a ransom -
