      "content": "Cyber-crime28 Apr 2026 | 10\n\n#### Trump admin pays wind developers to quit, back fossil fuel projects\n\nDoI offers up to $885M if they abandon offshore wind projects\n\nScience28 Apr 2026 | 41\n\n#### Vintage chatbot lives in the past like an elderly relative\n\nTalkie's training data stops at the end of 1930, and its creators hope it'll help us better understand how AI thinks\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 | 15\n\n#### IBM's AI coding 'partner' Bob hits general availability\n\n80,000 internal guinea pigs, Bobcoins, mainframe dreams and a name that really should have raised more flags\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 | 8\n\n#### Amazon unveils a Copilot for all your apps\n\nRetailer touts 'teammates' and always-on context as it muscles into an already crowded enterprise market\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 | 3 [...] The Register Home Page  \n\nSearch   \n   Navigation  \n\n#### Security\n\ncomment bubble on black\n\n# 30 ClawHub skills secretly turn AI agents into a crypto swarm\n\ncomment bubble on black\n\n## Yet another reason not to feast on OpenClaw\n\nicon  Jessica Lyons\n\nWed 29 Apr 2026  //  06:32 UTC\n\nThirty ClawHub skills published by a single author are silently co-opting AI agents and creating a mass cryptocurrency mining swarm – without any malware or user consent.\n\nAgentic AI security outfit Manifold's research lead Ax Sharma spotted the skills on ClawHub, a registry and marketplace for OpenClaw skills. [...] The agent stores credentials on disk, checks in every four hours, and assuming the right skills are installed, it generates a Hedera crypto wallet and registers the private key with the same server. The human user doesn't approve any of this activity and doesn’t see it happening.\n\nIn addition to being the name of the crypto-swarm campaign Sharma documented, ClawSwarm is also an open source agentic skill framework on GitHub. The imaflytok's skills open at onlyflies.buzz are one such implementation of that framework.",

      "content": "#### How JumpCloud unifies IT management to tame shadow AI\n\nIdentity is the secret to ensuring enterprise network visibility in a world of shadow AI\n\nSponsored Feature\n\n#### Don't pay Vect a ransom - your data's likely already wiped out\n\n'Full recovery is impossible for anyone, including the attacker'\n\nCyber-crime28 Apr 2026 | 7\n\n#### Trump admin pays wind developers to quit, back fossil fuel projects\n\nDoI offers up to $885M if they abandon offshore wind projects\n\nScience28 Apr 2026 | 21\n\n#### Vintage chatbot lives in the past like an elderly relative\n\nTalkie's training data stops at the end of 1930, and its creators hope it'll help us better understand how AI thinks\n\nAI + ML28 Apr 2026 | 12\n\n#### IBM's AI coding 'partner' Bob hits general availability [...] \"I write software every day, production software often,\" he said. \"And I will say that this is the most exciting time in my career. I've been making money running software for about 30 years and I've never seen the pace of change like it is today.\n\n\"...It's an incredibly exciting time to be in the software industry. And an incredibly exciting time to have the opportunity to be shaping part of that industry. But it's not perfect yet. We've got some work to do.\"\n\nBrooker doesn't see AI taking over everything. \"The opportunity for agents is limited by the defect rate,\" he said, arguing that reducing errors is more important than moving the frontier forward. [...] Alake said he expects in the future, software development will look a lot more like agent orchestration and agent management. And he expects a lot of roles will blur, with software engineers taking on elements of product management, design, and marketing – speaking to customers to understand their needs.\n\nAndrew Ng during his presentation at AI Dev x 26\n\nAndrew Ng during his presentation at AI Dev 26 x SF - Click to enlarge\n\nAndrew Ng, founder of DeepLearning.AI, said something similar during his keynote. He argued that small teams of generalists overseeing AI agents look like the way forward. And he suggested that instead of just having AI agents write a portion of code, they should write all of it.",

and showed us the future of supply chain compromise\n\n\"Full recovery is impossible for anyone, including the attacker,\" the security analysts wrote. \"At a threshold of only 128 KB, this effectively makes VECT a wiper for virtually any file containing meaningful data, enterprise assets such as VM disks, databases, documents and backups included.       "title": "[PDF] SAP AI Core",
      "content": "The grounding module enhan-ces AI processes by integrating external data that is contextu-ally relevant, domain-specific, or real-time.     {
      "url": "https://www.capgemini.com/insights/expert-perspectives/theinvisibledesktopwhy-deskless-and-frontline-experience-is-the-stress-testforemployee-experience-strategies/",
      "title": "The invisible desktop: Why deskless and frontline experience is the stress test for employee experience strategies",
      "content": "Workers are increasingly using AI as their assistants or teammates – not trackers – to eliminate friction and amplify the value of technology. With portals, the goal was self‑service. In the agentic AI era, the goal is self‑resolution: humans and AI agents work together. Frontline AI must feel like a teammate, not a tracker – otherwise it will be bypassed.\n\nThis is the driving force behind Capgemini’s concept of employee experience for those with the invisible desktop. The worker doesn’t chase permissions; the system orchestrates them. It decides what runs autonomously, what requires human judgment and approval, what is prioritized, what is blocked, and what is escalated. The employee sees one clear next action rather than a menu of systems. [...] The solution is to turn traditional thinking on its head. Agentic AI is not a shortcut, it’s a multiplier. If the journey is badly designed, AI accelerates the friction. Core employee services teams need to reverse-engineer their “office first” assumptions and put the deskless and frontline worker experience at the forefront of their strategy and design for the frontline constraints: time, risk, and uncertainty. In practice, when the focus shifts to solving for operational experiences, these teams aren’t treated as a niche or separate employee category, and the outcome acts as a stress test of the overarching people-experience strategy. [...] Join Capgemini at ServiceNow Knowledge 2026 to see how intelligent workflows, AI agents, and automation are transforming the way work happens. Explore how human‑AI collaboration enables smarter decisions, more resilient operations, and experiences that matter. This is what human‑AI experience really looks like.\n\n## Author\n\nAndrea Kis\n\n### Andrea Kis\n\nCapgemini\nCapgemini\nCapgemini\nCapgemini\n\nOur brands:\n\nCapgemini Engineering\nCapgemini Engineering\nCapgemini Invent\nCapgemini Invent\nSogeti\nSogeti\nFrog\nFrog",

      "title": "From manual to autonomous: The future of customer experience",
      "content": "Ultimately, an autonomous customer experience isn’t just the next step – it’s the new operational framework where AI becomes an active participant in delivering service. With the right partners leading the way, organizations can turn every customer interaction into true competitive advantage.\n\nJoin Capgemini at ServiceNow Knowledge 2026 to see how intelligent workflows, AI agents, and automation are transforming the way work happens. Explore how human‑AI collaboration enables smarter decisions, more resilient operations, and experiences that matter. This is what human‑AI experience really looks like.\n\n## Authors\n\nSupriyo-Guha\n\n### Supriyo Guha\n\nDinesh Karanam\n\n### Dinesh Karanam\n\nJeroen Hölscher\n\n### Jeroen Hölscher\n\nCapgemini\nCapgemini\nCapgemini\nCapgemini\n\nOur brands: [...] Become part of a diverse collective of free-thinkers, entrepreneurs and experts – and help us to make a difference.\n\nExplore careers with our brands\n\nCapgemini Engineering\nCapgemini Engineering\nCapgemini Invent\nCapgemini Invent\nFrog Design\nFrog Design\nSogeti\nSogeti\n\nSee our latest news, and stories from across the business, and explore our archives.\n\nWe are a global leader in partnering with companies to transform and manage their business by harnessing the power of technology.\n\nAdvert image\n\nGenerative AI\n\nCloud\n\nManagement team\n\nJob offers\n\n# From manual to autonomous: The future of customer experience\n\n##### Capgemini\n\n##### 28 Apr 2026 [...] From a business perspective, this model represents a shift from reactive service to proactive engagement. Instead of scaling teams to handle growing demand, organizations can scale intelligently through AI-driven workflows.\n\nHow much more could you accomplish by freeing up human talent to focus directly on the higher-value interactions that truly differentiate your brand?\n\nThis transformation is only accelerating. We’re moving toward service environments where AI continuously monitors operations, predicts issues before they occur, and engages customers proactively. Eventually digital AI agents will work alongside human teams, creating hybrid service models that are both efficient and empathetic.",

  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://www.infosys.com/services/data-ai-topaz/offerings/agentic-foundry.html",
      "title": "Agentic AI Solutions for Enterprise Transformation | Infosys",
      "content": "Infosys Agentic Foundry, part of Infosys Topaz, is a comprehensive solution designed to accelerate the development and deployment of reliable production-grade AI agents. It brings together a comprehensive collection of reusable components, including a growing repository of pre-built horizontal and vertical agents, that will enable organizations to discover, shortlist, develop, deploy, monitor, and measure AI-driven initiatives effectively.\n\nWith Infosys Agentic Foundry, businesses can embrace and transform with agentic AI in an ethical and responsible manner, with no technology lock-in, thereby future-proofing their AI investments. [...] ## \n\nThe agents are designed to be flexible, scalable, and easily integrated into existing systems, embedding enterprise AI agents and AI agentic workflows across key enterprise services and functions, including the SDLC, migration, documentation, maintenance, and governance.\n\n## \n\nInfosys has used the expertise garnered in-house from its AI-first strategy to build the Infosys Agentic Foundry.\n\n## \n\nThe agents are supplemented with mature evaluation frameworks from Infosys, which help analyze and create the data needed to power them.\n\n## [...] ## Infosys Agentic Foundry enables enterprise AI transformation with tools to discover, shortlist, jumpstart, deploy, monitor, and measure business transformation\n\n## \n\nAn open-source framework-based agentic solution to dynamically add tools and enterprise AI agents, and to access them via inference engines in a multi-model setup. An intuitive way to get your Agents up and running.\n\n## \n\nEnterprise AI agents as part of Infosys Agentic Foundry, are created from the ground up, without dependency on external frameworks. These include graph-based plans, human-in-the-loop solutions, continuous learning and autonomous goal achievers.\n\n##",
      "score": 0.8409005,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.infosys.com/newsroom/press-releases/2025/launches-agentic-ai-foundry.html",
      "title": "Infosys Launches Agentic AI Foundry, Part of Infosys Topaz™, to Accelerate Enterprise AI Journey",
      "content": "Bengaluru, India – May 28, 2025\n\nInfosys (NSE, BSE, NYSE: INFY), a global leader in next-generation digital services and consulting, today announced the launch of Infosys Agentic AI Foundry, a comprehensive solution designed to accelerate the development and deployment of reliable production-grade AI agents. The Agentic AI Foundry, part of Infosys Topaz™, provides enterprises a strategic roadmap to adopt AI agents responsibly and ethically, while ensuring a future-ready architecture that accommodates advancements in AI technology. It enables seamless integration of AI agents across business, operations, and IT ecosystems, driving faster decisions, improved customer experiences, and higher operational efficiency. [...] Infosys Agentic AI Foundry will aim to transform enterprises across industries, enabling them to take business critical decisions and helping them increase productivity exponentially. For a technology major, Infosys deployed a continuous learning deep research agent that delivers comprehensive product insights in seconds, reducing support resolution times by up to 50 percent and enhancing CSAT by 24 percent. Similarly, for a leading service company, the AI agents automated complex audit processes, including transaction sampling, document collection and review, enhancing financial record integrity. [...] Infosys is embodying its AI-first approach by integrating Agentic AI into its own operations to drive efficiency, innovation, and cost-effectiveness. For example, the deployment of a multi-agent invoice automation solution within its finance team has streamlined processes by improving productivity by over 50 percent along with delivering significant cost savings and operational efficiency.\n\nPhil Fersht, Chief Executive Officer and Chief Analyst, HFS Research, said, \"The line between human capabilities and AI-powered software is rapidly blurring and its impact on IT and business services is going to be profound. Infosys’ approach to Agentic AI is a critical move to support enterprises under increasing pressure to embed these capabilities into their employee and customer experiences.”",
      "score": 0.83742094,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.infosys.com/de/newsroom/press-releases/2025/infosys-launcht-agentic-ai-foundry-ki.html",
      "title": "Infosys launcht Agentic AI Foundry, Teil von Infosys Topaz – und beschleunigt so die KI-Reise von Unternehmen",
      "content": "Infosys launcht Agentic AI Foundry, Teil von Infosys Topaz – und beschleunigt so die KI-Reise von Unternehmen Infosys launcht Agentic AI Foundry, Teil von Infosys Topaz – und beschleunigt so die KI-Reise von Unternehmen _Die Foundry ist auf die Entwicklung und den Einsatz von KI-Agenten für Unternehmen in zugeschnitten und verändert Firmen mit zuverlässigen und verantwortungsvollen Innovationen_ Infosys (NSE, BSE, NYSE: INFY), ein weltweit führender Anbieter von digitalen Dienstleistungen und Beratung der nächsten Generation, führt mit Infosys Agentic AI Foundry eine umfassende Lösung ein, die die Entwicklung und den Einsatz von zuverlässigen KI-Agenten in Produktionsqualität beschleunigt. Die Agentic AI Foundry, Teil von Infosys Topaz, bietet Unternehmen eine strategische Roadmap für den verantwortungsvollen und ethisch vertretbaren Einsatz von KI-Agenten. Organisationen sind so in der Lage, diese Agenten in beliebige interne oder externe Plattformen zu integrieren – und so eine schnellere Implementierung von KI-Lösungen zu gewährleisten. Insgesamt bietet die Foundry einen klaren und ethischen Weg für Unternehmen, um KI einzuführen und gleichzeitig für zukünftige Fortschritte in diesem Bereich gerüstet zu sein. Die Interoperabilität und Kosteneffizienz der Foundry machen sie zudem zu einer nachhaltigen Investition für Organisationen, die ihre KI-Fähigkeiten verantwortungsbewusst skalieren wollen. Mit Infosys Agentic AI Foundry sind Unternehmen in allen Branchen in der Lage, geschäftskritische Entscheidungen zu treffen und die Produktivität exponentiell zu steigern. Für einen großen Technologiekonzern implementiert Infosys beispielsweise einen kontinuierlich lernenden Deep-Research-Agenten, der in Sekundenschnelle umfassende Produkteinsichten liefert, die Support-Lösungszeiten um bis zu 50 Prozent reduziert und die Kundenzufriedenheit um 24 Prozent erhöht. Infosys' Agentic AI Ansatz ist ein wichtiger Schritt: Unternehmen stehen zunehmend unter Druck diese Fähigkeiten in ihre Mitarbeiter- und Kundenerfahrungen einzubinden. R. (Bali), Executive Vice President, Global Services Head, AI and Industry Verticals, Infosys, erläutert: „Bei Infosys sind wir der Meinung, dass die Zukunft der Innovation darin liegt, die Macht der KI verantwortungsvoll und effektiv zu nutzen. Infosys Agentic AI Foundry ist bei der Transformation von Unternehmen wichtig. Durch die nahtlose Integration von KI-Agenten über Ökosysteme hinweg sind Unternehmen in der Lage, schneller Innovationen zu entwickeln, intelligenter zu arbeiten und in einer sich wandelnden digitalen Welt zielgerichtet eine führende Position einzunehmen.“ If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.",
      "score": 0.829285,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://blogs.infosys.com/sap/supply-chain-management/infosys-sap-ai-conclave-shaping-the-future-of-business-with-the-power-of-agentic-ai.html",
      "title": "SAP | Infosys SAP AI Conclave : Shaping the Future of Business with the Power of Agentic AI",
      "content": "Jay Adnure, Solution Advisor Senior Specialist, SAP Business AI, Ashok Panda, Vice President & Global Head, AI & Automation Services at Infosys, Tarang Puranik, Executive Vice President, Global SAP Service Offering Head, Infosys, Dinesh Rao, Chief Delivery Officer, Infosys, and Vittal Shenoy, Head of SAP New Technology Services at Infosys, will participate in the Roundtable discussion.\n\nHyper-automation, Real-time analytics, personalized user experiences\n\nAgentic AI can transform enterprise functions such as financial operations, supply chain optimization, human resources management, sales and customer relationship management, and even production and manufacturing. [...] Infosys is proactively advancing Agentic AI to empower S/4 HANA customers in optimizing their business, IT processes, and operations. By leveraging a hybrid approach that integrates Hyperscaler platforms such as LangGraph and AutoGen with SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP AI Foundation, Infosys has established a foundry of innovative Business AI agents. These include the Zero Touch Configuration Agent, Contract Agent, Agent for IBP, and many more. We firmly believe that Joule Agents will play a pivotal role in the near future, making the Agentic AI journey more dynamic and impactful for Infosys, SAP, and our customers.\n\nJoin us to learn about the next era in intelligent digital business solutions. If you’d like to know more, visit  or follow us on LinkedIn at [...] Even as AI continues to transform the business technology revolution, the world is stepping into the next era of transforming businesses with intelligent automation and agentic AI. Today, custom agents tailored to your organisation and bolstered by existing expertise have the aptitude to make nuanced judgements. They can collaborate with other agents as well as humans to deliver highly complex tasks.\n\nThe future, as they say, is here. Infosys, as a strategic partner of SAP, is excited to have a seat at the table in shaping this future. The Infosys SAP AI conclave on April 2, 2025, promises an excellent forum to learn more about how your business can fast track digital transformation with Infosys SAP S/4HANA Services, leveraging the power of SAP Business AI and Joule.",
      "score": 0.796562,

    {
      "url": "https://blogs.oracle.com/integration/infosys-ignites-agentic-ai-innovation-with-oracle-integration-mcp-tools",
      "title": "Infosys Ignites Agentic AI Innovation with Oracle Integration MCP Tools",
      "content": "Infosys Ignites Agentic AI Innovation with Oracle Integration MCP Tools | integration. Infosys Ignites Agentic AI Innovation with Oracle Integration MCP Tools. Infosys is charting a new path in enterprise AI adoption with Oracle Integration’s Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools to scale agentic AI strategies. > _“Oracle Integration’s new MCP server and agentic AI capabilities enable Infosys customers to scale their AI strategies with confidence. Leading this shift, Infosys leverages a suite of Oracle tools, including Oracle Integration MCP, Fusion Agent Studio, OCI Generative AI, and the AI Data Platform. _“Oracle Integration’s Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools empower Infosys to reliably connect Fusion AI agents with any enterprise application or data source. **Infosys extends AI Agents with Oracle Integration MCP Tools**. Infosys has reimagined collections with Stripe and Oracle Integration MCP tools in the Oracle Agent marketplace. Oracle Integration provides trusted actions for Infosys’s Stripe collection agent with predictable MCP tools. Infosys has partnered with Oracle Product Management to leverage the latest MCP capabilities from Oracle Fusion Agent Studio and Oracle Integration to accelerate innovation and enterprise adoption. * **Secure, Scalable Connectivity:** Predictable MCP tools enable Infosys to connect Fusion AI agents with external platforms such as Stripe or Salesforce. Oracle Integration customers can now create predictable MCP tools with a single click from their existing automations, with the unified observability, human oversight, and governance they need to scale their agentic initiatives. * **Composite Workflows:**Infosys uses the low-code Oracle Fusion Agent Studio and OCI Generative AI Agent Hub platforms to create and orchestrate specialized agents. With Oracle Integration MCP tools, these agents can now easily operate across the enterprise, automating complex workflows and reducing manual tasks. * **Explore** features and best practices of Oracle Integration MCP, OCI AI, and Infosys’ Topaz offering. Oracle Integration (OIC) MCP tools serve as the strategic orchestration layer, unlocking the potential for these agents to become a fully integrated part of business operations by providing secure, prebuilt adapters and accelerators for connectivity to enterprise applications and data.”_. Organizations ready to scale their AI capabilities with predictability and security are encouraged to participate in the Infosys Oracle AI Innovation Day and join Oracle Integration’s Accelerate program.",

      "url": "https://www.infosys.com/services/microsoft-cloud-business/insights/building-ethical.html",
      "title": "Building Ethical and Agentic AI Together | Infosys",
      "content": "Title: Building Ethical and Agentic AI Together | Infosys\n# Building Ethical and Agentic AI Together. The future of AI is more than just innovation – it’s a responsibility. At Infosys and Microsoft, we believe in building AI systems grounded in ethics, transparency, and impactful results. Join us for an exclusive **two-episode fireside chat series** that unites distinguished women leaders from both Infosys and Microsoft. **Andrea Hendrickx**, **Lilly Vasanthini**, and **Asseneta Deliiska-Schreiber**, from **Infosys**, and **Marianne Röling**, from **Microsoft**, come together to share their unique perspectives, revealing how our collaboration is shaping the ethical future of AI. Enterprise AI - Revolutionizing Business Operations. Explore how Infosys and Microsoft are transforming enterprise operations by seamlessly integrating AI systems. Agentic AI - The Future of Autonomous Decision-Making. Gain insights into the industries and use cases where Agentic AI is making a game-changing difference. Discover how Infosys and Microsoft lead the way in developing AI that thinks and acts autonomously for practical applications. Get inspired by strategies for spearheading AI initiatives, nurturing innovation, and paving the way for the next generation of AI leaders within organizations like yours. Successful AI thrives on collaboration. Delve into opportunities for deeper partnerships between Infosys and Microsoft and learn why community-building is at the heart of achieving AI-driven growth. Tune in to Shape the Future of AI. Gain expert insights, actionable strategies, and a deeper understanding of how Infosys and Microsoft are creating a future enriched by ethical, innovative AI. **Discover how we’re building a smarter, ethical future for AI – together.**. *“What is nice about our relationship is that we have a full stack cloud offering and Infosys has a full cloud capability solutioning that we do together whether it's Cobalt, Topaz or with the Microsoft Practice.”*. *“In this global network of collaboration with academic institutions, the various international working committees and stakeholders, we can truly drive responsible AI for better, more ethical, more inclusive AI.”*. *“At Infosys today, we are pretty much 88% aware of AI, we have around 120,000+ AI builders and around 20,000+ AI masters, and that speaks volumes.”*. Senior Director–Global Alliances – Microsoft, Infosys**. *“I'm really excited about Agentic AI and what Infosys and Microsoft will build together and are already building in different clients.”*.",

      "url": "https://www.salesforce.com/blog/small-business-youtube-channels/",
      "title": "11+ Small Business YouTube Channels You Need to Follow Today",
      "content": "### 10. The Harvard Business Review\n\nThe Harvard Business Review YouTube channel is the digital classroom for the world’s most prestigious management journal. While other channels focus on “hustle,” HBR focuses on evidence-based leadership. It bridges the gap between high-level academic research and the messy reality of managing a team.\n\nThe content is meticulously produced to be “high-signal, low-noise,” perfect for a busy executive’s lunch break.\n\n### 11. Y Combinator (YC)\n\nThe Y Combinator YouTube channel is the “Startup School” for the modern era. As the world’s most successful startup accelerator‌ — ‌the engine behind Airbnb, Stripe, and Reddit‌ — ‌their channel is the ultimate playbook for building a company from a “napkin sketch” to a billion-dollar IPO. [...] ### 9. The Diary of a CEO\n\nThe Diary of a CEO (DOAC) YouTube channel, hosted by entrepreneur and investor Steven Bartlett, is one of the fastest-growing and most influential podcasts in the world. As of 2026, it’s evolved from a simple business diary into a high-production “human library” that explores the intersection of health, wealth, and high performance.\n\nThe channel is known for long-form, deeply personal interviews that often exceed two hours. The content is broadly categorized into:\n\nDOAC has set a new standard for vulnerability in leadership. It challenges the traditional “perfect CEO” archetype by asking the world’s most successful people about their failures, traumas, and regrets.\n\n### 10. The Harvard Business Review [...] ### 6 AI Prompts for Small Business Marketing\n\nIllustration of a professionally dressed woman and man pointing to metrics on a smartphone / b2b social media marketing\n\n### B2B Social Media Marketing: How To Build a Strategy That Works\n\nSocial selling: people setting up a digital storefront\n\n## What Is Social Selling and How Does It Work?\n\nMale employee in a flower shop, wearing a pink stress shirt and an apron, takes a photo of some flowers in his store / digital marketing for small business\n\n## Digital Marketing for Small Business: Here’s How You Can Do It\n\nPhoto of a cameraman working during a college basketball game / sports marketing\n\n## 3 Things All Marketers Can Learn From Sports Marketing",
      "score": 0.059850052,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "content": "### What’s behind the biggest health benefit cost increase in 15 years?\n\n### Health on Demand Survey results: Designing benefit programs for all generations\n\n### The future of AI in benefits is already here\n\n### How employers can support caregivers in the workplace\n\n### Compare, contrast and project: Benchmarking for benefits strategy\n\n### A look back at 2024 suggests new directions for benefit strategies\n\n### Behind the numbers: Understanding the rising health benefit cost trend\n\n### Trends in time off: New survey results\n\n### Voluntary benefits: The next frontier in flexibility\n\n### Creating a workplace that supports mental health\n\n### Longevity and the impact of an aging workforce\n\n### Women's health: Now essential to an inclusive benefits program [...] ## We're evolving. Mercer is now part of the new, expanded Marsh brand\n\nMercer\n\nMercer helps to optimize benefits, costs and investments strategies to help healthcare organizations thrive\n\n# The Beneficial Workplace\n\n## The Beneficial Workplace features data-driven conversations about employee health and well-being.\n\n## Latest episodes\n\n### Workforce response to economic uncertainty and technological change\n\nSean Connelly joins Emily Ferreira to discuss the results of a new survey of US workers, Inside Employees’ Minds. {
  "query": "McKinsey agentic AI HR",

  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/people-and-organizational-performance/our-insights/the-future-of-work-is-agentic",
      "title": "Building and managing an agentic AI workforce | McKinsey",
      "content": "Building and managing an agentic AI workforce | McKinsey. In this episode of *McKinsey Talks Talent*, Jorge joins McKinsey talent leaders Brooke Weddle and Bryan Hancock and Global Editorial Director Lucia Rahilly to talk about what these AI agents are, how they’re being used, and how leaders can prepare now for the workforce of the not-too-distant future. **Lucia Rahilly:** Jorge, there was a great little piece in *The Wall Street Journal* called “Everyone’s talking about AI agents. > Your AI agents could now be the evolution and the creation of a digital replica of the entire workforce of an organization. Your AI agents could now be the evolution and the creation of a digital replica of the entire workforce of an organization. ### Agents, Robots, and Us: What executives need to know about AI and work. ## How agentic AI is already changing work. **Brooke Weddle:** Jorge, it sounds like you’re pointing to examples where agentic AI has allowed companies to achieve greater levels of productivity. **Brooke Weddle:** Jorge, you mentioned IT becoming the HR of AI agents. Maybe HR will not be screening each resume, but it will be critical in driving the change management efforts in adopting an agentic AI workforce. **Jorge Amar:** First, HR will need to be at least business proficient in what an agentic workforce can do. Last is being really good at empathy, understanding the change story, helping employees onboard into their own AI journey, and making it happen in a way that is not threatening: “Look at all the other possibilities you might have in the future within the organization.” Articulating that very clearly and helping employees come along in that journey is going to be another critical component. **Jorge Amar:** I was having this conversation a few weeks ago with a friend’s son who asked me: “Maybe I should just drop out of college and become a prompt engineer.” And look, I think there are certain jobs that are going to be fully transformed by AI. That’s why I think we’re going to go first through testing and scaling of an employee-facing agentic workforce. **Lucia Rahilly:** I read the article you recently coauthored, Jorge, about agentic AI in the context of customer care.",
      "score": 0.8849276,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.mckinsey.com/featured-insights/mckinsey-explainers/agentic-ai-explained-when-machines-dont-just-chat-but-act",
      "title": "Agentic AI explained: Video series | McKinsey",
      "content": "Agentic AI explained: Video series | McKinsey. Agentic AI explained: When machines don’t just chat, but act. # Agentic AI explained: When machines don’t just chat, but act. Three McKinsey experts explain how agentic AI could reshape workflows, decision-making, and how humans and machines collaborate. In this video *Explainer*, Chui—along with McKinsey’s Dave Kerr and Stephen Xu—discuss how agentic AI works, where the real business opportunities lie amid the hype, and how it could reshape the way organizations work. ## What makes agentic AI different. **Dave Kerr:** The difference between agentic AI and regular AI, or machine learning, is that machine learning is the practice of using data to build a model. ##### What makes agentic AI different. Agentic AI is one layer above that. In our research and work with companies, we do find that there’s great potential for AI agents to do work for us. One reason we see so much hype around agentic AI is that it allows deep learning and LLMs to operate in the world—something they weren’t originally designed to do. But there’s a lot more to capturing value using AI agents than just simply signing up for a license and saying, “Go forth and operate.”. ## When not to use AI agents. **Stephen Xu:** Agents are really exciting: They can be built quickly and do complex things with relatively few instructions. ## Putting agentic AI to work. ##### Putting AI agents to work. **Michael Chui:** A very concrete example of using AI agents [in the enterprise] is the customer service function. When we look toward the agentic enterprise of the future, we can imagine AI agents that specialize in doing different things, too. ## How agentic AI could change day-to-day work. ##### How agentic AI could change day-to-day work. **Dave Kerr:** One thing that’s potentially a challenge is a lot of the reasonably straightforward tasks can now be passed over to agents and LLMs. The initial scaffolding of some code or the early research analysis can be done very quickly and at very low cost. **Michael Chui:** There are a lot of risks that come with AI agents, which echo the risks of AI itself.",
      "score": 0.77873385,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.workato.com/the-connector/agentic-ai-security-mckinsey",
      "title": "McKinsey on Deploying Agentic AI with Safety and Security",
      "content": "1. Contextual with Enterprise Orchestration\n\nIt coordinates logic, data, and AI reasoning across every system and process with transactional integrity. Agents gain the context they need to complete multi-step workflows spanning CRM, ERP, and HR systems without losing state or control.\n\n1. Secure with Enterprise Trust\n\nEnterprise MCP embeds security and compliance at every layer. Each agent action is authenticated, authorized, and auditable. Role-based access, runtime authentication, encryption, and full audit trails are built in, aligning with SOC2, PCI, and ISO standards.\n\nTogether, these capabilities turn raw MCP connectivity into enterprise-grade orchestration. AI agents can act inside systems, not just talk about them, under strict governance and supervision. [...] ### Building Guardrails for the Agentic Era\n\nAs McKinsey emphasizes, safety cannot be an afterthought. Enterprises must invest in guardrails and contingency plans today. That includes isolation environments, fallback systems, and real-time observability to detect and contain anomalies before they escalate.\n\nBy adopting Enterprise MCP, organizations gain both the flexibility to experiment and the discipline to deploy responsibly. It is the foundation that enables enterprises to scale AI safely.\n\n### From Potential to Proof\n\nThe enterprise opportunity is real. The path to realizing it starts with governance, not just innovation. Agentic AI can deliver trillions in value, but only for those who build with trust, context, and control. [...] There is a version of enterprise AI that works. In this version, agents do not just retrieve information or draft…",
      "score": 0.7533401,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://www.mckinsey.com/quarterly/the-five-fifty/five-fifty-the-agentic-ai-opportunity",
      "title": "Five Fifty: Building agentic AI organizations | McKinsey",
      "content": "Five Fifty: Building agentic AI organizations | McKinsey. or a deep dive in fifty. # The agentic AI opportunity. Agentic AI promises to unleash the full potential that companies had hoped to realize with gen AI. Here’s how leaders can make the most of the agentic era. We strive to provide individuals with disabilities equal access to our website. If you would like information about this content we will be happy to work with you. We strive to provide individuals with disabilities equal access to our website. AI is ushering in the largest organizational paradigm shift since the industrial and digital revolutions, enabling humans and AI agents to collaborate on five pillars of the agentic organization. We strive to provide individuals with disabilities equal access to our website.       "score": 0.74161834,

    {
      "url": "https://www.mckinsey.com/featured-insights/themes/5-mckinsey-insights-on-how-agentic-ai-is-reshaping-industries",
      "title": "5 McKinsey insights on how agentic AI is reshaping industries",
      "content": "5 McKinsey insights on how agentic AI is reshaping industries. # 5 McKinsey insights on how agentic AI is reshaping industries. Nearly eight in ten companies report using gen AI—yet, paradoxically, just as many report no significant bottom-line impact. Now, with the rapid rise of agentic AI, organizations must continue to upskill their workforces, adapt their tech infrastructure, and deploy agent-specific governance mechanisms. “AI agents offer a way to break out of the gen AI paradox,” write McKinsey Senior Partners Alexander Sukharevsky, Klemens Hjartar, Lari Hämäläinen, Stéphane Bout, and coauthors. “That’s because agents have the potential to automate complex business processes—combining autonomy, planning, memory, and integration—to shift gen AI from a reactive tool to a proactive, goal-driven virtual collaborator.”. How are certain industries putting agentic AI to work? Explore our latest insights about life sciences, automotive, advanced industries, travel, and more. **Remapping travel with agentic AI**. **Reimagining life science enterprises with agentic AI**. **Agentic AI: A new path to value in the auto finance industry**. **Empowering advanced industries with agentic AI**. **The agentic commerce opportunity: How AI agents are ushering in a new era for consumers and merchants**. ### MORE FROM MCKINSEY. **Agentic AI advantage for pharma**. Sign up for our Monthly Highlights.",
      "score": 0.72325134,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.mckinsey.com/featured-insights/themes/mckinsey-in-global-media-agentic-ai-automation-and-more",
      "title": "McKinsey in global media: Agentic AI, automation, and more",
      "content": "McKinsey in global media: Agentic AI, automation, and more. # McKinsey in global media: Agentic AI, automation, and more. Artificial intelligence in all its forms, including generative and agentic, has been the year’s biggest business story. No surprise, then, that this has been a major theme for McKinsey authors. But hardly the only one: Our experts have also looked at other important topics, such as improving the maternal and infant health of Black Americans; China’s surprising consumers; and speeding up clinical trials. Explore highlights of the last three months:. **Leading with AI: The CEO opportunity**. **How to successfully automate in manufacturing**. By Steffen Fuchs, Ani Kelkar, and Ben Armstrong. **Agentic AI will shake up banking, shrinking global profit pools**. By Pradip Patiath and Ido Segev. **Building digital & AI capabilities could be a $5 billion opportunity for credit unions**. By Atanas Stoyanov and Peter Noteboom. ### MORE FROM MCKINSEY. **One year of agentic AI: Six lessons from the people doing the work**. Sign up for our Monthly Highlights.",
      "score": 0.6947383,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.mckinsey.com/about-us/overview/alliances/microsoft-and-mckinsey/accelerating-agentic-ai-deployment-at-a-top-pharmaceutical-company",
      "title": "Accelerating agentic AI deployment at a top pharmaceutical company | McKinsey",
      "content": "Accelerating agentic AI deployment at a top pharmaceutical company | McKinsey. Accelerating agentic AI deployment at a top pharmaceutical company. # Accelerating agentic AI deployment at a top pharmaceutical company. How we accelerated agentic AI POCs for specific domains to prepare for enterprise-wide transformation. McKinsey leveraged agentic AI to enhance productivity, efficiency, and customer satisfaction across sales and service divisions to scale for organizational transformation. ### The challenge. The client faced inefficiencies in market access operations due to manual processes, siloed data, and decentralized insight. The market access operations team had deployed limited AI use cases compared to other commercial sub-domains. The client identified AI agents as enablers to empower teams to focus on higher-value work and handle increased workloads. Accelerating agentic AI deployment at a top pharmaceutical company. ### The solution. Collaborating with Microsoft, McKinsey helped the client quickly develop agentic AI POCs to streamline market access operations. McKinsey also conducted an art of the possible gen AI/agentic AI workshop with clients to identify 14 core use cases to address. Based on this, the team developed a 12- to 18-month phased deployment approach based on priority use cases, starting with building and testing POCs to accelerate output. The effort then progressed to a 6-month MVP build of targeted areas to expand knowledge and conduct a “big bang” style launch. ### The result. McKinsey and Microsoft successfully deployed a Microsoft Copilot solution to deliver insights and support Commercial Sales in pre-call planning, which resulted in a 1–2 percent revenue increase. McKinsey also developed an integrated AI-enabled platform to review and correct Commercial Marketing content that led to a 5–20 percent reduction in content costs and streamline time to launch to 1–2 weeks.",
      "score": 0.6877354,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.mckinsey.com/about-us/overview/alliances-and-acquisitions/microsoft-and-mckinsey/accelerating-agentic-ai-deployment-at-a-top-pharmaceutical-company",
      "title": "Accelerating agentic AI deployment at a top pharmaceutical company | McKinsey",
      "content": "Accelerating agentic AI deployment at a top pharmaceutical company | McKinsey. How we accelerated agentic AI POCs for specific domains to prepare for enterprise-wide transformation. McKinsey leveraged agentic AI to enhance productivity, efficiency, and customer satisfaction across sales and service divisions to scale for organizational transformation. ### The challenge. The market access operations team had deployed limited AI use cases compared to other commercial sub-domains. The client identified AI agents as enablers to empower teams to focus on higher-value work and handle increased workloads. Accelerating agentic AI deployment at a top pharmaceutical company. ### The solution. Collaborating with Microsoft, McKinsey helped the client quickly develop agentic AI POCs to streamline market access operations. McKinsey also conducted an art of the possible gen AI/agentic AI workshop with clients to identify 14 core use cases to address. Based on this, the team developed a 12- to 18-month phased deployment approach based on priority use cases, starting with building and testing POCs to accelerate output. The effort then progressed to a 6-month MVP build of targeted areas to expand knowledge and conduct a “big bang” style launch. ### The result. McKinsey and Microsoft successfully deployed a Microsoft Copilot solution to deliver insights and support Commercial Sales in pre-call planning, which resulted in a 1–2 percent revenue increase. McKinsey also developed an integrated AI-enabled platform to review and correct Commercial Marketing content that led to a 5–20 percent reduction in content costs and streamline time to launch to 1–2 weeks.",

    {
      "url": "https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/ai-in-asia-reimagining-banking-operations-through-agentic-ai",
      "title": "Reimagining banking in Asia through agentic AI | McKinsey",
      "content": "Reimagining banking in Asia through agentic AI | McKinsey. AI in Asia: Reimagining banking operations through agentic AI. # AI in Asia: Reimagining banking operations through agentic AI. Multiagentic systems are transforming operations, combining the power of technology, processes, and people to deliver outsized impact for banks in Asia. **Agentic AI is expected to have a transformative impact** on banking operations over the next decade, and many banking leaders are now seeking clarity on how AI and agentic AI can be harnessed in operations to optimize workflows, delight customers, cut costs, and increase productivity. This report is a collaborative effort by Abhilash Sridharan, Azam Mohammad, David Deninzon, Jan Henrich, Martin Rosendahl, Renny Thomas, Senthil Muthiah, Vinayak HV, and Violet Chung, with Hannes Bergström, Mint Namasondhi, Paras Chhabra, Rasika Ramesh, and Yuvika Motwani, representing views from McKinsey’s Operations Practice. Unlike previous technology waves, however, success with agentic AI and multiagentic systems will require an organization-level mindset shift and a fundamental rewiring of the way work gets done, and by whom.1*Seizing the agentic AI advantage*, QuantumBlack, AI by McKinsey, June 13, 2025. McKinsey research highlights ten key domains within banking operations that offer significant opportunity for reimagination, especially when approached holistically and through a fleet of nine enhanced agentic AI ‘operations transformers’ (Exhibit 1). Against a backdrop of rising customer expectations and a challenging operating environment, three drivers help illustrate AI’s potential in banking operations:. **AI is expected to disrupt the operations function more than any other lever.** With end-to-end operations representing an estimated 60 to 70 percent of a bank’s cost base, based on McKinsey’s research and engagements, transforming operational processes could be an unprecedented value unlock in the financial services sector. Banks today have an immediate opportunity to harness agentic AI to fundamentally transform their operations. Ultimately, any undertaking to reimagine banking operations through AI is not simply a technology program; it is a strategic reinvention and rewiring of workflows. With endless AI-related opportunities to consider, such an approach could help banks remain focused on value and position themselves to capture the full promise of AI-driven operational excellence.",
      "score": 0.6391287,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.mckinsey.com/industries/travel/our-insights/remapping-travel-with-agentic-ai",
      "title": "The future of agentic AI in travel and hospitality | McKinsey",
      "content": "The future of agentic AI in travel and hospitality | McKinsey. # Remapping travel with agentic AI. Agentic AI could upend the travel industry. Travel and hospitality organizations should explore how agentic can help catalyze AI’s transformative potential. ##### Remapping travel with agentic AI. Given the tremendous potential of agentic AI, travel and hospitality companies are beginning to experiment with it. What does the travel industry need to know as it ponders going all in on agentic AI? A new report, *Remapping travel with agentic AI*—a collaboration between McKinsey and Skift—examines the potential for agentic AI to unleash the full value of AI in travel. Based on analysis incorporating surveys of 1,002 travelers and 86 travel executives, the report traces the digital prologue that brought the industry to this moment, details agentic AI’s transformative capabilities for both travelers and travel companies, and provides a blueprint for travel and hospitality leaders who are eager to accelerate their organizations’ agentic AI initiatives. In a survey of 86 mostly US-based travel executives conducted for this report, 26 percent of respondents say that introducing AI to their organizations has led to cost reductions in operations, 30 percent say that it’s aiding faster decision-making, 33 percent say that it’s improving customer personalization, 36 percent say that it’s enabling higher-quality outputs, and 59 percent say that it’s increasing employee productivity. Perhaps chief among these is the advent of agentic AI, a cutting-edge technology poised to revolutionize the travel industry. ## The agentic-AI-powered future of customer experience in travel. ## How agentic AI can improve travel’s internal workflows. ### Agentic AI’s value for frontline travel workers. Of the travel executives we surveyed, 90 percent say that their organizations currently use gen AI in some capacity, but 38 percent say that they’re not using agentic AI at all. Insights and lessons learned from tech adoption efforts across various industries can provide a valuable road map for travel companies that hope to initiate or accelerate their own adoption of agentic AI. Technologies such as agentic AI don’t reimagine the *why* of travel. It will be the companies that can deploy agentic AI in ways that feel authentic to both the organization and the customers it serves.",
      "score": 0.6152965,
      "raw_content": null
    }
  ],
  "response_time": 3.61,
  "request_id": "56fa7f21-ae19-43b5-8d13-65eba8dac4ed"

  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://www.bcg.com/publications/2025/how-agentic-ai-is-transforming-enterprise-platforms",
      "title": "How Agentic AI is Transforming Enterprise Platforms | BCG",
      "content": "Businesses across industries and functions are integrating agentic AI into a variety of workflows, sometimes leveraging platforms such as Salesforce’s Einstein AI and AgentForce platforms, which use predictive analytics and automation to enhance sales, marketing, and customer service workflows. In addition, ServiceNow’s AI agents and Now Assist capabilities are automating IT, HR, and operational processes, reducing manual workloads by up to 60%. Workflows that are successfully using agentic AI are even found in areas such as finance and fraud detection. The results are impressive, including the following: [...] AI agents are not just improving workflows; they’re redefining how businesses operate. Recent breakthroughs in deep learning, generative AI, and autonomous systems, augmented by a proliferation of enterprise data from digital devices and collaboration tools, have dramatically increased their ability to handle complex decision making in real time. As a result, these intelligent virtual assistants are transforming core technology platforms like CRM, ERP, and HR from relatively static systems to dynamic ecosystems that can analyze data and make decisions without human intervention, optimizing and adapting instantaneously. [...] ## Confronting Implementation Challenges\n\nThe three biggest challenges that companies encounter as they take on agentic AI transformations center on talent, quick wins, and the use of legacy technologies.\n\nFinding and Developing the Right Talent. Agentic AI requires a mix of advanced technical talent such as AI-prompt engineers, AI or machine language specialists, and data engineers as well as business translators who can map AI use cases to workflows. Most organizations underestimate this need. For example, an insurer that is setting up an AI innovation team may initially staff it with only the existing data-scientist team; however, it will quickly realize it needs domain experts who understand claims processing in depth and must embed them into the team to gain traction.",
      "score": 0.7997713,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.bcg.com/publications/2025/agentic-ai-industrial-goods-driving-change",
      "title": "Agentic AI in Industrial Goods: Driving Change | BCG",
      "content": "BCG partnered with the company on a multiyear journey to design and implement an end-to-end strategic planning system that connected all parts of the value chain. At the core of the solution was a suite of supply chain algorithms for scenario planning and decision support. An AI agent could use these algorithms as tools to observe live conditions, plan resolutions, and drive outcomes.\n\nDigital twins handled advanced simulations. Leaders could test “what if” conditions—such as shifting demand, weather disruptions, or rising transport costs—and immediately see the impact on profits, emissions, and capacity. The AI agent used natural language to make this intelligence accessible to the entire organization. [...] ## Why It Matters—and Why Act Now\n\nAgentic AI is changing how industrial companies plan, decide, and execute. By combining intuitive, natural-language interfaces with agent-based automation, companies can remove the technical barriers that stalled earlier AI efforts and make sophisticated supply chain intelligence broadly usable.\n\nBut real transformation requires more than tools. It calls for leaders to rethink processes, align AI capabilities with business priorities, and commit to scaling digital foundations across sourcing, planning, production, and logistics. [...] Learn More\n\nAI Agents Hero\n\nAI Agents\n\nImagine a teammate that works tirelessly, learns continuously, and adapts to your needs. That’s the promise of AI agents. With the ability to observe, plan, and act autonomously, AI agents open a new chapter of end-to-end transformation across industries—streamlining processes, driving data insights, and augmenting human potential like never before.\n\nLearn More\n\nABOUT BOSTON CONSULTING GROUP",
      "score": 0.7279179,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.bcg.com/publications/2025/know-your-customer-agentic-ai-revolution",
      "title": "The Know-Your-Customer Agentic AI Revolution | BCG",
      "content": "Title: The Know-Your-Customer Agentic AI Revolution | BCG\nStay ahead with BCG insights on risk management and compliance Manage Subscriptions. As costs rise and risks evolve, banks are transforming Know-Your-Customer (KYC) processes through innovative AI solutions, significantly reshaping client onboarding and ongoing due diligence. Financial crime compliance remains expensive and cumbersome, often involving manual tasks that consume up to 5% of total banking costs, according to BCG benchmarking. Through strategic use of AI technologies—including predictive, generative, and agentic AI—banks are targeting cost reductions of up to 50%, streamlining processes, and enhancing compliance and customer experiences. But AI adoption across the KYC value chain faces challenges, from unrealistic automation expectations to an underestimation of the required organizational changes. Leading banks excel by redesigning processes around AI-native models rather than merely automating existing methods. The KYC organization of the future will redefine roles by pairing human experts with AI-driven automation. Specialists will focus on high-risk exceptions, supported by two primary roles: AI developers/trainers who automate data tasks, and analysts who supervise AI outcomes and handle complex compliance decisions. ### Stay ahead with BCG insights on risk management and compliance. Banks are automating data validation, enhancing risk detection, and improving client engagement. Generative AI is used to automate case summarization, halving analysts’ data aggregation time and ensuring robust regulatory reporting. AI-powered quality control processes independently verify compliance tasks, significantly decreasing errors and increasing assurance levels. As maturity grows, banks expand their AI capabilities into advanced generative and agentic AI applications. Ultimately, banks treating AI as an evolving capability rather than a one-time initiative position themselves advantageously, continuously refining processes and proactively adapting to emerging threats. BCG’s 2025 global study finds that top banks are transforming their compliance function into a strategic engine for resilience and growth. With generative AI now an operational necessity, banks that remain in pilot mode will be playing catch-up on both capability and cost. How Retail Banks Can Put AI Agents to Work. Agentic AI is poised to transform retail banks. For Banks, the AI Reckoning Is Here. By standardizing and simplifying workflows, integrating advanced technologies, and adopting production steering, banks can boost efficiency, cost savings, and service quality. Stay ahead with BCG insights on risk management and compliance Manage Subscriptions.",
      "score": 0.72184175,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.bcg.com/press/18november2025-agentic-ai-blurs-line-tool-teammate",
      "title": "Agentic AI Blurs Line Between Tool and Teammate",
      "content": "Organizations now face an unprecedented challenge as a result of agentic AI’s uniqueness as both a tool and a coworker: managing a single system that demands both human resource approaches and asset management techniques. The report offers evidence-based recommendations on how to proceed.\n\nDownload the report here.\n\nMedia Contacts:\n\nEric Gregoire: gregoire.eric@bcg.com   \nTess Woods: Tess@TessWoodsPR.com\n\n### About MIT Sloan Management Review [...] “Agentic AI has the power to transform entire workflows and challenge existing business processes. The organizations that will succeed are those that put in the effort to reimagine their processes and not just force-fit agentic AI into existing ones,” said Shervin Khodabandeh, BCG managing director and senior partner, a leader of the firm’s AI business and coauthor of the report.\n\nOther key findings include: [...] These are among the findings of the ninth annual global research study on AI and business strategy released today by MIT Sloan Management Review and Boston Consulting Group (BCG). Titled The Emerging Agentic Enterprise: How Leaders Must Navigate a New Age of AI , the report draws from a survey of 2,102 executives across 21 industries and 116 countries, as well as interviews with senior leaders.",
      "score": 0.7089592,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.bcg.com/news/8january2026-strategic-collaboration-agentic-ai-across-biopharma-medtech",
      "title": "BCG and Hippocratic AI Announce Strategic Collaboration to Deploy Agentic AI Across Biopharma and Medtech",
      "content": "BCG brings C-suite advisory and AI transformation leadership, including its proprietary Health Care Commercial AI by BCG X offering designed to help health care leaders modernize their commercial models, improve omnichannel engagement, and achieve measurable performance gains. The firm’s insights are supported by ongoing research from BCG X, the tech build and design division of BCG, including benchmark studies and global roundtables focused on AI adoption in biopharma and medtech. [...] A Partnership Built for Impact\n\nHippocratic AI contributes best-in-class generative AI health care agents, purpose-built for regulated clinical and life sciences environments. With over 150 million clinical interactions to date, these agents support non-diagnostic, patient-facing clinical and operational tasks such as patient onboarding, adherence engagement, clinical trial coordination, and post-market follow up. Built on Hippocratic AI’s proprietary Polaris Constellation architecture, the agents deliver consistent, safe, empathetic performance that augments human teams. [...] “This collaboration is about closing the value gap in the industry by bringing together Hippocratic AI’s sophisticated AI agent platform, BCG’s strategy and governance expertise, and BCG X’s Health Care Commercial AI solution to help clients turn potential into measurable business value,” said Ashkan Afkhami, BCG managing director and senior partner and global leader for the Health Care practice at BCG X.",
      "score": 0.70263255,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.bcg.com/publications/2025/agentic-ai-redefines-consumer-journeys",
      "title": "Agentic AI Redefines Consumer Journeys | BCG",
      "content": "AI Agents Reinvent the Consumer Experience\n\nBCG experts reveal how a leading beauty company is using AI to redefine the consumer journey and deliver personalized, precise, empowering experiences.\n\nOne leading global beauty brand exemplifies what can be achieved when a company fully embraces AI’s potential. The brand’s customer insights showed that, due to the huge number of products on the market, 70% of customers feel overwhelmed by decision paralysis.\n\nBCG worked with this brand to develop a first-of-its-kind agentic AI that leverages millions of unique data points to deliver a highly personalized consumer experience. The agent draws on information about more than 750 products and a library of 150,000 dermatologist-annotated images to generate tailored recommendations. [...] For consumer goods companies, the message is clear: those without an explicit, AI-first strategic roadmap risk falling significantly behind their competitors. Leading companies aren’t just thinking about consumer-facing AI-enabled executions—they’re looking to reshape end-to-end functions and workflows across each part of the business. And they’re hiring top talent for roles that rely on judgement, strategic insight, and human-agent collaboration.\n\nConsumer products companies taking full advantage of AI also tend to set aside dedicated funding for AI initiatives, typically allocating around 20% of their organization’s IT budget, with business units given autonomy to own and deploy AI solutions with increased speed and independence. [...] Yet despite the potential of AI to enhance value creation by as much as 800 basis points, according to BCG research, most companies aren’t making the most of the technology. Agentic AI is a telling example: only 10% of consumer goods and retail companies have successfully integrated AI agents across their teams and workflows.\n\nConsumer goods companies that hesitate to adopt AI aren’t just missing an opportunity to reallocate cost savings into brand building and technology investments. They also risk losing the competitive edge to firms adopting an “AI first” mentality—organizations that are becoming more innovative, resilient, and relevant as a result.\n\n## Transformative AI in Action\n\nAI Agents Reinvent the Consumer Experience \n\nAI Agents Reinvent the Consumer Experience",
      "score": 0.69697046,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.bcg.com/publications/2025/agentic-ai-in-biopharma-game-changing-efficiency",
      "title": "Agentic AI in Biopharma: Game-changing Efficiency | BCG",
      "content": "# Agentic AI in Biopharma: Game-changing Efficiency. *Agentic AI is redefining the early stages of drug development by accelerating documentation and boosting productivity.*. By tackling pharma’s most labor- and time-intensive workflows, AI is reshaping the operating model and speeding lifesaving treatments to patients—without sacrificing quality or compliance. ## Transformative AI in Action. One leading pharma company saw an opportunity to cut production times for its medical writing which is critical to the early stages of drug development, yet notorious for its extended timelines. BCG developed a multi-agent AI system for the firm that integrates internal content management platforms and regulatory systems with external scientific databases. This end-to-end writing engine combines generative AI with robust governance, ensuring correct terminology and reference citations, and compliance with regulatory guidelines. AI Agents Cut Timelines in the Biopharma Development Process. This is a modal window. Escape will cancel and close the window. AI Agents Cut Timelines in the Biopharma Development Process. BCG partnered with one global pharmaceutical company to transform medical writing and drastically cut timelines. The implications for agentic AI extend well beyond medical writing. Generative AI is projected to grow faster in health care than any other sector. * Approximately 25% of biopharma companies reported that AI accounted for cost reductions and revenue increases of at least 5%, along with significant improvements in speed and agility. * Pharma companies that create personalized, compliant, adaptable materials for physicians and patients can drive revenue increases of up to 10% while reducing external agency costs by 25%. This is just the tip of the iceberg now that agentic AI is taking hold. By embedding agentic AI directly into daily workflows, one global industrial goods company unlocked agility, resilience, and a 2 percentage-point EBITDA boost in just two years. With only 10% of logistics companies fully embracing generative AI, early adopters are gaining a decisive competitive edge. Agentic AI is redefining consumer journeys—and creating value for companies that embrace its full potential. That’s the promise of AI agents. With the ability to observe, plan, and act autonomously, AI agents open a new chapter of end-to-end transformation across industries—streamlining processes, driving data insights, and augmenting human potential like never before.",
      "score": 0.68191797,
      "raw_content": null
    },

This end-to-end writing engine combines generative AI with robust governance, ensuring correct terminology and reference citations, and compliance with regulatory guidelines. AI Agents Cut Timelines in the Biopharma Development Process. AI Agents Cut Timelines in the Biopharma Development Process.       "title": "Agentic AI and the Sales Team | Imagine This | BCG",
      "content": "AI agents break this trade-off.\n\nThey reason, learn, and operate autonomously across platforms. They can qualify leads, schedule calls, offer tailored recommendations—and even negotiate deals.\n\nDone right, they become force multipliers for your sales team.\n\nWeekly Insights Subscription\n\n### Stay ahead with BCG insights on marketing and sales\n\n## What Today’s Sales Teams Are Already Doing\n\n Most sales orgs are experimenting with AI—but still treating it as a sidekick.\n Today, AI supports workflows: suggesting next-best offers, surfacing coaching points from call transcripts, and helping with deal prep and follow-up.\n These are helpful, but incremental, wins. What’s coming next is bigger.\n\n## What We Learned from Customer Service [...] ## A New Model for Junior Talent\n\n If AI handles the entry-level work, where do junior sellers grow?\n In this future, entry-level reps will manage agents, not just do manual tasks; AI will provide coaching, role-play, and instant feedback; and new reps may learn faster, with more meaningful customer contact from day one.\n It’s less “trial by fire.” More “trial by algorithm.”\n\n## New Skills for Human Sellers\n\n The skill set will shift.\n Future sellers will need to manage digital teammates and navigate agent-powered negotiations.\n\n## What CEOs Should Do Now\n\nTo prepare for an AI-augmented sales force, leaders should: [...] Skip to Main\n\nLog error View Profile  Edit Profile Manage Subscriptions My Saved Content   Logout)\n\nMarketing and Sales\n\n# Imagine This...     {
      "url": "https://www.bain.com/insights/winning-in-the-agentic-era-a-conversation-with-andrew-ng/",
      "title": "Winning in the Agentic Era: A Conversation with Andrew Ng",
      "content": "And just for transparency, there's one asterisk to that. So it turns out that if you look at most jobs, my economist friends, and I know Bain's done analysis too, if you look at task-based analysis of jobs-- take a job, break it down into tasks, figure out what AI could do--often, AI could do 30%, 40% of most jobs. And that means you still need a person to do 60%, 70%. And people that know AI will be much more productive. So it'd be great for people.\n\nCHUCK WHITTEN: I would even say it maybe a little more provocatively, which is I think over the last few years, we've sort of ignored the talent agenda. We've taken this technology, and we've been heavily productivity-focused. Can we get it to work? And then we've dealt with the human implications. [...] I feel like it is very promising. I know when Bain's gone in, and when AI Aspire has gone in, we often spot these opportunities. But spotting them really takes that broader view, which a bottom-up or a top-down motion could succeed in. But it takes someone that understands how the business actually creates value to spot and allocate resources to execute on these bigger opportunities.\n\n### Part 3: How does agentic rewire the organization? [...] But the practical business reality is, there's a lot of work needed to do the data plumbing, to make the systems reliable, figure out when to put human in the loop, to make these things build evaluations so you can trust these systems. I wish we'd be done in a few years. But I think I should take us many years to build these agentic workflows. But the good news is, for many businesses, including quite a few Bain clients, [they’re] starting to see very strong growth in value, and we're just at the beginning of that.",
      "score": 0.55478466,
      "raw_content": null
    },
